'''金刀比羅神社'''（ことひらじんじゃ）は、[[北海道]][[根室市]]にある[[神社]]である。
==祭神== *大物主神・事代主神・倉稲魂命
==由緒== [[1806年]]（文化3年）高田屋嘉兵衛によって創建され、[[1881年]]（明治14年）現在地に移された。同年に郷社、[[1919年]]（大正8年）に県社に列格している。
==所在地== *北海道根室市琴平町1-4
==外部リンク== *[http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/top.html 金刀比羅神社（北海道神社庁）]
[[category:北海道の神社|ことひらしんしや]] {{shinto-stub}}

revision.comment: [[金刀比羅神社例大祭]] 2010年3月12日 (金) 09:21 (UTC)を統合、執筆者 Makky0728。
revision.text:
{{Mergefrom|金刀比羅神社例大祭|金刀比羅神社 (根室市)|date=2010年3月}} '''金刀比羅神社'''（ことひらじんじゃ、こんぴらじんじゃ）は、[[北海道]][[根室市]]にある[[神社]]である。毎年8月に行われる[[金刀比羅神社例大祭]]は、北海道三大祭の一つとされている。
==祭神== *[[大物主神]]・事代主神・倉稲魂命
==由緒== [[1806年]]（[[文化 (元号)|文化]]3年）[[高田屋嘉兵衛]]によって創建され、[[1881年]]（明治14年）現在地に移された。同年に郷社、[[1919年]]（大正8年）に県社に列格している。
==所在地== *北海道根室市琴平町1-4
==外部リンク== *[http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/top.html 金刀比羅神社（北海道神社庁）]
{{DEFAULTSORT:ことひらしんしや}} [[Category:金刀比羅神社|しんしや ねむろし]] [[Category:府県社]] [[Category:根室国]] [[Category:北海道の神社]] [[Category:根室市]] {{shinto-stub}}
{{Mergeto|金刀比羅神社 (根室市)|金刀比羅神社 (根室市)|date=2010年3月}} 金刀比羅神社例大祭とは、北海道最東端「根室市」の琴平町にある金刀比羅神社の例大祭のこと。北海道三大祭りのひとつに数えられ、毎年８月９日（宵宮祭）、１０日（本祭・御神輿渡御）、１１日（御神輿渡御）に行われる。 同祭りでは４つの祭典区からなり、「第一祭典区」「第三祭典区」「東部祭典区」「西部祭典区」の４つである。各祭典区に先太鼓・金棒・山車・手古舞などがあり、９日の夜「音と力の競演」と題し山車・手古舞の部を。１０日に先太鼓・金棒の部を行う。 また、平成１８年には「金刀比羅神社御創祀２００年」を迎え、御神輿が御色直しを行い、さらに社務所を新築し神輿殿・お祭り資料館を併設した。

revision.comment: 統合後のwikifyおよび加筆修正、テンプレート除去。
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 金刀比羅神社 |画像 = |所在地 = 北海道根室市琴平町1-4 |位置 = |祭神 = [[大物主神]]・[[事代主|事代主神]]・[[ウカノミタマ|倉稲魂命]] |社格 = 県社 |創建 = 1806年 |本殿 = 入母屋流造 |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 8月9〜11日 |神事 = }}
'''金刀比羅神社'''（ことひらじんじゃ、こんぴらじんじゃ）は、[[北海道]][[根室市]]にある[[神社]]である。
== 祭神 == *[[大物主神]]・[[事代主|事代主神]]・[[ウカノミタマ|倉稲魂命]]
== 由緒 == [[1806年]]（[[文化 (元号)|文化]]3年）[[高田屋嘉兵衛]]によって創建され、[[1881年]]（[[明治]]14年）現在地に[[遷座]]した。同年に郷社、[[1919年]]（[[大正]]8年）に[[県社]]に列格している。[[2005年]]（[[平成]]18年）には「金刀比羅神社御創祀200年」を迎え、御神輿が御色直しを行い、さらに社務所を新築し「神輿殿・お祭り資料館」を併設した。
== 金刀比羅神社例大祭 == 毎年8月の同神社の例大祭は「金刀比羅神社例大祭」「こんぴらさんのおまつり」として「北海道三大祭」のひとつに数えられ、8月9日（宵宮祭）、10日（本祭・御神輿渡御）、11日（御神輿渡御）の3日間に渡って行なわれる。
先太鼓・金棒・[[山車]]・手古舞の奉納を行なう祭典区は[[大正時代]]に置かれ、「第一祭典区」「第三祭典区」「東部祭典区」「西部祭典区」の4つからなる。9日の夜には「音と力の競演」と題して山車・手古舞の部が、10日には先太鼓・金棒の部が行なわれる。
== 所在地 == *北海道根室市琴平町1-4
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/top.html 北海道神社庁] ** [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/16/16001.html 金刀比羅神社] * [http://www.city.nemuro.hokkaido.jp/section/kanko/event_cale/konpira/konpira.html 根室市 ねむろのイベント情報：金刀比羅神社例大祭]
{{DEFAULTSORT:ことひらしんしや ねむろし}} [[Category:金刀比羅神社]] [[Category:府県社]] [[Category:根室国]] [[Category:北海道の神社]] [[Category:根室市]] {{shinto-stub}}

== 金刀比羅神社例大祭 == 毎年8月の同神社の例大祭は「金刀比羅神社例大祭」「こんぴらさんのおまつり」として、8月9日（宵宮祭）、10日（本祭・御神輿渡御）、11日（御神輿渡御）の3日間に渡って行なわれる。
先太鼓・金棒・[[山車]]・手古舞の奉納を行なう祭典区は[[大正時代]]に置かれ、「第一祭典区」「第三祭典区」「東部祭典区」「西部祭典区」の4つからなる。9日の夜には「音と力の競演」と題して山車・手古舞の部が、10日には先太鼓・金棒の部が行なわれる。
== 所在地 == *北海道根室市琴平町1-4
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/top.html 北海道神社庁] ** [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/16/16001.html 金刀比羅神社] * [http://www.city.nemuro.hokkaido.jp/section/kanko/event_cale/konpira/konpira.html 根室市 ねむろのイベント情報：金刀比羅神社例大祭]

{{神社 |名称 = 金刀比羅神社 |画像 = |所在地 = 北海道根室市琴平町1-4 |位置 = |祭神 = [[大物主神]]・[[事代主|事代主神]]・[[ウカノミタマ|倉稲魂命]] |社格 = 県社 |創建 = 1806年 |本殿 = 入母屋流造 |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 8月9〜11日 |神事 = }}
'''金刀比羅神社'''（ことひらじんじゃ、こんぴらじんじゃ）は、[[北海道]][[根室市]]にある[[神社]]である。
== 祭神 == *[[大物主神]]・[[事代主|事代主神]]・[[ウカノミタマ|倉稲魂命]]
== 由緒 == [[1806年]]（[[文化 (元号)|文化]]3年）[[高田屋嘉兵衛]]によって創建され、[[1881年]]（[[明治]]14年）現在地に[[遷座]]した。同年に郷社、[[1919年]]（[[大正]]8年）に[[県社]]に列格している。[[2005年]]（[[平成]]18年）には「金刀比羅神社御創祀200年」を迎え、御神輿が御色直しを行い、さらに社務所を新築し「神輿殿・お祭り資料館」を併設した。
== 金刀比羅神社例大祭 == 毎年8月9日（宵宮祭）、10日（本祭・御神輿渡御）、11日（御神輿渡御）の3日間に渡って、金刀比羅神社例大祭が行なわれる。
先太鼓・金棒・[[山車]]・手古舞の奉納を行なう祭典区は[[大正時代]]に置かれ、「第一祭典区」「第三祭典区」「東部祭典区」「西部祭典区」の4つからなる。9日の夜には「音と力の競演」と題して山車・手古舞の部が、10日には先太鼓・金棒の部が行なわれる。
== 所在地 == *北海道根室市琴平町1-4
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/top.html 北海道神社庁] ** [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/16/16001.html 金刀比羅神社] * [http://www.city.nemuro.hokkaido.jp/section/kanko/event_cale/konpira/konpira.html 根室市 ねむろのイベント情報：金刀比羅神社例大祭] *[http://www.geocities.co.jp/HeartLand/3922/ ねむトピ（金刀比羅神社例大祭の画像、動画が載っているサイト）]
{{DEFAULTSORT:ことひらしんしや ねむろし}} [[Category:金刀比羅神社]] [[Category:府県社]] [[Category:根室国]] [[Category:北海道の神社]] [[Category:根室市]] {{shinto-stub}}

{{神社 |名称 = 金刀比羅神社 |画像 = |所在地 = 北海道根室市琴平町1-4 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|43|20|37.64|N|145|35|14.90|E|scale:25000}} |祭神 = [[大物主神]]・[[事代主|事代主神]]・[[ウカノミタマ|倉稲魂命]] |社格 = 県社 |創建 = 1806年（文化3年） |本殿 = 入母屋流造 |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 8月9〜11日 |神事 = }}
'''金刀比羅神社'''（ことひらじんじゃ、こんぴらじんじゃ）は、[[北海道]][[根室市]]にある[[神社]]である。
== 祭神 == *[[大物主神]]・[[事代主|事代主神]]・[[ウカノミタマ|倉稲魂命]]
== 由緒 == [[1806年]]（[[文化 (元号)|文化]]3年）[[高田屋嘉兵衛]]によって創建され、[[1881年]]（[[明治]]14年）現在地に[[遷座]]した。同年に郷社、[[1919年]]（[[大正]]8年）に[[県社]]に列格している。[[2005年]]（[[平成]]18年）には「金刀比羅神社御創祀200年」を迎え、御神輿が御色直しを行い、さらに社務所を新築し「神輿殿・お祭り資料館」を併設した。
== 金刀比羅神社例大祭 == 毎年8月9日（宵宮祭）、10日（本祭・御神輿渡御）、11日（御神輿渡御）の3日間に渡って、金刀比羅神社例大祭が行なわれる。
先太鼓・金棒・[[山車]]・手古舞の奉納を行なう祭典区は[[大正時代]]に置かれ、「第一祭典区」「第三祭典区」「東部祭典区」「西部祭典区」の4つからなる。9日の夜には「音と力の競演」と題して山車・手古舞の部が、10日には先太鼓・金棒の部が行なわれる。
== 所在地 == *北海道根室市琴平町1-4
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/top.html 北海道神社庁] ** [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/16/16001.html 金刀比羅神社] * [http://www.city.nemuro.hokkaido.jp/section/kanko/event_cale/konpira/konpira.html 根室市役所（ねむろのイベント情報：金刀比羅神社例大祭）] * [http://www.geocities.co.jp/HeartLand/3922/ ねむトピ（金刀比羅神社例大祭の画像、動画が載っているサイト）] * [http://www.nemuro-kankou.com/events/kotohira.html 根室市観光協会:金刀比羅神社例大祭] * [http://crabnavi.web.fc2.com/nemuro_kotohira/top.html ねむろ金刀比羅神社例大祭（根室の金刀比羅神社例大祭と観光名所を紹介したサイト）]

== 由緒 == [[1806年]]（[[文化 (元号)|文化]]3年）[[根室国]][[根室郡]]で[[高田屋嘉兵衛]]によって創建され、[[1881年]]（[[明治]]14年）現在地に[[遷座]]した。同年に郷社、[[1919年]]（[[大正]]8年）に[[県社]]に列格している。[[2005年]]（[[平成]]18年）には「金刀比羅神社御創祀200年」を迎え、御神輿が御色直しを行い、さらに社務所を新築し「神輿殿・お祭り資料館」を併設した。
== 金刀比羅神社例大祭 == 毎年8月9日（宵宮祭）、10日（本祭・御神輿渡御）、11日（御神輿渡御）の3日間に渡って、金刀比羅神社例大祭が行なわれる。
先太鼓・金棒・[[山車]]・手古舞の奉納を行なう祭典区は[[大正時代]]に置かれ、「第一祭典区」「第三祭典区」「東部祭典区」「西部祭典区」の4つからなる。9日の夜には「音と力の競演」と題して山車・手古舞の部が、10日には先太鼓・金棒の部が行なわれる。
== 所在地 == *北海道根室市琴平町1-4

== 由緒 == [[1806年]]（[[文化 (元号)|文化]]3年）[[根室国]][[根室郡]]で[[高田屋嘉兵衛]]によって創建され、[[1881年]]（[[明治]]14年）現在地に[[遷座]]した。同年に郷社、[[1919年]]（[[大正]]8年）に[[県社]]に列格している。[[2005年]]（[[平成]]18年）には「金刀比羅神社御創祀200年」を迎え、御神輿が御色直しを行い、さらに社務所を新築し「神輿殿・お祭り資料館」を併設した。
== 金刀比羅神社例大祭 == 毎年8月9日（宵宮祭）、10日（本祭・御神輿渡御）、11日（御神輿渡御）の3日間に渡って、金刀比羅神社例大祭が行なわれる。
先太鼓・金棒・[[山車]]・手古舞の奉納を行なう祭典区は[[大正時代]]に置かれ、「第一祭典区」「第三祭典区」「東部祭典区」「西部祭典区」の4つからなる。9日の夜には「音と力の競演」と題して山車・手古舞の部が、10日には先太鼓・金棒の部が行なわれる。
== 所在地 == *北海道根室市琴平町1-4
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/top.html 北海道神社庁] ** [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/16/16001.html 金刀比羅神社] * [http://www.city.nemuro.hokkaido.jp/section/kanko/event_cale/konpira/konpira.html 根室市役所（ねむろのイベント情報：金刀比羅神社例大祭）] * [http://www.geocities.co.jp/HeartLand/3922/ ねむトピ（金刀比羅神社例大祭の画像、動画が載っているサイト）] * [http://www.nemuro-kankou.com/events/kotohira.html 根室市観光協会:金刀比羅神社例大祭] * [http://crabnavi.ninpou.jp/nemuro-kotohira/top.html ねむろ金刀比羅神社例大祭（根室の金刀比羅神社例大祭と観光名所を紹介したサイト）]

拝殿 {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|43|20|37.64|N|145|35|14.90|E}}}} |所在地 = [[北海道]][[根室市]]琴平町1-4 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|43|20|37.64|N|145|35|14.90|E|display=title,inline}} |祭神 = [[大物主神]]・[[事代主|事代主神]]・[[ウカノミタマ|倉稲魂命]] |社格 = 旧[[県社]] |創建 = [[1806年]]（文化3年） |本殿 = 入母屋流造 |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 8月9〜11日 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=43|lat_min=20|lat_sec=37.6|lon_deg=145|lon_min=35|lon_sec=14.9|label=金刀比羅
神社|position=left|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|AlternativeMap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} }} '''金刀比羅神社'''（ことひらじんじゃ、こんぴらじんじゃ）は、[[北海道]][[根室市]]にある[[神社]]である。 == 祭神 == *[[大物主神]]・[[事代主|事代主神]]・[[ウカノミタマ|倉稲魂命]] *[[北方領土]]の[[多楽島]]・[[志発島]]・[[水晶島]]・[[色丹島]]・[[国後島]]にあった神社の御神体も預かっており，各社の例祭日に祭典が行われる。 == 由緒 == [[1806年]]（[[文化 (元号)|文化]]3年）[[根室国]][[根室郡]]で[[高田屋嘉兵衛]]によって創建され、[[1881年]]（[[明治]]14年）現在地に[[遷座]]した。同年に郷社、[[1919年]]（[[大正]]8年）に[[県社]]に列格している。[[2005年]]（[[平成]]18年）には「金刀比羅神社御創祀200年」を迎え、御神輿が御色直しを行い、さらに社務所を新築し「神輿殿・お祭り資料館」を併設した。 == 金刀比羅神社例大祭 == 毎年8月9日（宵宮祭）、10日（本祭・御神輿渡御）、11日（御神輿渡御）の3日間に渡って、金刀比羅神社例大祭が行なわれる。 先太鼓・金棒・[[山車]]・手古舞の奉納を行なう祭典区は[[大正時代]]に置かれ、「第一祭典区」「第三祭典区」「東部祭典区」「西部祭典区」の4つからなる。9日の夜には「音と力の競演」と題して山車・手古舞の部が、10日には先太鼓・金棒の部が行なわれる。 == 所在地 == *北海道根室市琴平町1-4 == 外部リンク == ・[https://www.nemuro-kotohira.com/ 金刀比羅神社　公式ウェブサイト] ・[http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/top.html 北海道神社庁] *[http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/16/16001.html 金刀比羅神社] *[http://www.city.nemuro.hokkaido.jp/section/kanko/event_cale/konpira/konpira.html 根室市役所（ねむろのイベント情報：金刀比羅神社例大祭）] *{{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.co.jp/HeartLand/3922/ |title=ねむトピ（金刀比羅神社例大祭の画像、動画が載っているサイト） |date=19990427034754}} *[http://www.nemuro-kankou.com/events/kotohira.html 根室市観光協会:金刀比羅神社例大祭] *[http://crabnavi.ninpou.jp/nemuro-kotohira/top.html ねむろの観光とおまつり（根室金刀比羅神社例大祭と観光名所を紹介したサイト）] {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ことひらしんしや}} [[Category:金刀比羅神社]] [[Category:北海道の神社]] [[Category:根室国|社]] [[Category:北海道の旧県社]] [[Category:根室市の建築物]] [[Category:根室市の歴史]]

{{神社 |名称 = 金刀比羅神社 |画像=[[ファイル:Kotohira Shrine (Nemuro City).jpg|270px]]
拝殿 {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|43|20|37.64|N|145|35|14.90|E}}}} |所在地 = [[北海道]][[根室市]]琴平町1-4 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|43|20|37.64|N|145|35|14.90|E|display=title,inline}} |祭神 = [[大物主神]]・[[事代主|事代主神]]・[[ウカノミタマ|倉稲魂命]] |社格 = 旧[[県社]] |創建 = [[1806年]]（文化3年） |本殿 = 入母屋流造 |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 8月9〜11日 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=43|lat_min=20|lat_sec=37.6|lon_deg=145|lon_min=35|lon_sec=14.9|label=金刀比羅
神社|position=left|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|AlternativeMap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} }} '''金刀比羅神社'''（ことひらじんじゃ、こんぴらじんじゃ）は、[[北海道]][[根室市]]にある[[神社]]である。 == 祭神 == *[[大物主神]]・[[事代主|事代主神]]・[[ウカノミタマ|倉稲魂命]] *[[北方地域|北方領土]]の[[多楽島]]・[[志発島]]・[[水晶島]]・[[色丹島]]・[[国後島]]にあった神社の御神体も預かっており，各社の例祭日に祭典が行われる。 == 由緒 == [[1806年]]（[[文化 (元号)|文化]]3年）[[根室国]][[根室郡]]で[[高田屋嘉兵衛]]によって創建され、[[1881年]]（[[明治]]14年）現在地に[[遷座]]した。同年に郷社、[[1919年]]（[[大正]]8年）に[[県社]]に列格している。[[2005年]]（[[平成]]18年）には「金刀比羅神社御創祀200年」を迎え、御神輿が御色直しを行い、さらに社務所を新築し「神輿殿・お祭り資料館」を併設した。 == 金刀比羅神社例大祭 == 毎年8月9日（宵宮祭）、10日（本祭・御神輿渡御）、11日（御神輿渡御）の3日間に渡って、金刀比羅神社例大祭が行なわれる。 先太鼓・金棒・[[山車]]・手古舞の奉納を行なう祭典区は[[大正時代]]に置かれ、「第一祭典区」「第三祭典区」「東部祭典区」「西部祭典区」の4つからなる。9日の夜には「音と力の競演」と題して山車・手古舞の部が、10日には先太鼓・金棒の部が行なわれる。 2020年（令和2年）北海道[[民俗文化財|無形民俗文化財]]に指定された。 == 所在地 == *北海道根室市琴平町1-4 == 外部リンク == ・[https://www.nemuro-kotohira.com/ 金刀比羅神社　公式ウェブサイト] ・[http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/top.html 北海道神社庁] *[http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/16/16001.html 金刀比羅神社] *[http://www.city.nemuro.hokkaido.jp/section/kanko/event_cale/konpira/konpira.html 根室市役所（ねむろのイベント情報：金刀比羅神社例大祭）]{{リンク切れ|date=2023年8月}} *{{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.co.jp/HeartLand/3922/ |title=ねむトピ（金刀比羅神社例大祭の画像、動画が載っているサイト） |date=19990427034754}} *[http://www.nemuro-kankou.com/events/kotohira.html 根室市観光協会:金刀比羅神社例大祭]{{リンク切れ|date=2023年8月}} *[http://crabnavi.ninpou.jp/nemuro-kotohira/top.html ねむろの観光とおまつり（根室金刀比羅神社例大祭と観光名所を紹介したサイト）] {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ことひらしんしや}} [[Category:金刀比羅神社]] [[Category:北海道の神社]] [[Category:根室国|社]] [[Category:北海道の旧県社]] [[Category:根室市の建築物]] [[Category:根室市の歴史]]

金刀比羅神社_(根室市)&action=edit [[Category:K]][[ja:金刀比羅神社 (根室市)]] {{Shrine | Name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | Image = [[File:Kotohira Shrine (Nemuro City).jpg|270px]]
Main Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|43|20|37.64|N|145|35|14.90|E}}}} | Location = Kotobira Town 1-4, {{ill|Nemuro|ja|根室市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}} | Position = {{ill-coordinates|43|20|37.64|N|145|35|14.90|E|inline,title}} | Deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命}} | Classification = Former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} | Established = 1806 (Bunka 3) | MainHall = Irimoya-zukuri (hip-and-gable style) | AlternateName = | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = August 9–11 | Rituals = | Map = {{Location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=43|lat_min=20|lat_sec=37.6|lon_deg=145|lon_min=35|lon_sec=14.9|label=Kotohira
Shrine|position=left|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|AlternativeMap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社), also known as Konpira Shrine, is located in {{ill|Nemuro|ja|根室市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}. == Deities == * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命}} * The shrine also holds sacred objects from shrines located on the {{ill|Northern Territories|ja|北方地域}} of {{ill|Taraku Island|ja|多楽島}}, {{ill|Shibotsu Island|ja|志発島}}, {{ill|Suisho Island|ja|水晶島}}, {{ill|Shikotan|ja|色丹島}}, and {{ill|Kunashiri|ja|国後島}}, conducting festivals on their respective anniversaries. == History == Kotohira Shrine was established in 1806 (Bunka 3) in {{ill|Nemuro Province|ja|根室国}}, {{ill|Nemuro District|ja|根室郡}} by {{ill|Kahē Takadaya|ja|高田屋嘉兵衛}}. It was relocated to its current site in 1881 (Meiji 14). The shrine was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} in 1881 and promoted to {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} in 1919 (Taishō 8). In 2005 (Heisei 18), the shrine celebrated its 200th anniversary, during which the sacred palanquin (mikoshi) was refurbished. A new shrine office was also constructed, including a "Mikoshi Hall and Festival Museum." == Kotohira Shrine Grand Festival == The annual grand festival is held over three days from August 9 (evening festival) to August 11 (main festival with portable shrine procession). The festival includes taiko drumming, performances with golden staves, floats, and traditional hand dances. Four festival districts, established during the {{ill|Taishō period|ja|大正時代}}, participate: "First Festival District," "Third Festival District," "Eastern Festival District," and "Western Festival District." On the night of August 9, a competition titled "Sound and Strength Performance" features floats and hand dances, while August 10 showcases taiko drumming and golden staves. The festival was designated an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Asset of Hokkaido|ja|無形民俗文化財}} in 2020 (Reiwa 2). == Address == * Kotobira Town 1-4, Nemuro, Hokkaido == External Links == * [https://www.nemuro-kotohira.com/ Official Website of Kotohira Shrine] * [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/top.html Hokkaido Shrine Association] * [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/16/16001.html Kotohira Shrine Information] * [http://www.city.nemuro.hokkaido.jp/section/kanko/event_cale/konpira/konpira.html Nemuro City Event Information: Kotohira Shrine Festival] {{ill-broken-link|date=August 2023}} * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.co.jp/HeartLand/3922/ |title=Nemuro Topics (Kotohira Shrine Festival images and videos) |date=April 27, 1999}} * [http://www.nemuro-kankou.com/events/kotohira.html Nemuro Tourist Association: Kotohira Shrine Grand Festival] {{ill-broken-link|date=August 2023}} * [http://crabnavi.ninpou.jp/nemuro-kotohira/top.html Nemuro Tourism and Festivals: Kotohira Shrine Festival and Sightseeing] {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Kotohira Shrines]] [[Category:Shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:Nemuro Province|Shrines]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines of Hokkaido]] [[Category:Buildings in Nemuro]] [[Category:History of Nemuro]] [[ja:金刀比羅神社 (根室市)]]

It was relocated to its current site in 1881 (Meiji 14). The shrine was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} in 1881 and promoted to {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} in 1919 (Taishō 8). In 2005 (Heisei 18), the shrine celebrated its 200th anniversary, during which the sacred palanquin (mikoshi) was refurbished. A new shrine office was also constructed, including a "Mikoshi Hall and Festival Museum." == Kotohira Shrine Grand Festival == The annual grand festival is held over three days from August 9 (evening festival) to August 11 (main festival with portable shrine procession). The festival was designated an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Asset of Hokkaido|ja|無形民俗文化財}} in 2020 (Reiwa 2). == Address == * Kotobira Town 1-4, Nemuro, Hokkaido == External Links == * [https://www.nemuro-kotohira.com/ Official Website of Kotohira Shrine] * [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/top.html Hokkaido Shrine Association] * [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/16/16001.html Kotohira Shrine Information] * [http://www.city.nemuro.hokkaido.jp/section/kanko/event_cale/konpira/konpira.html Nemuro City Event Information: Kotohira Shrine Festival] {{ill-broken-link|date=August 2023}} * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.co.jp/HeartLand/3922/ |title=Nemuro Topics (Kotohira Shrine Festival images and videos) |date=April 27, 1999}} * [http://www.nemuro-kankou.com/events/kotohira.html Nemuro Tourist Association: Kotohira Shrine Grand Festival] {{ill-broken-link|date=August 2023}} * [http://crabnavi.ninpou.jp/nemuro-kotohira/top.html Nemuro Tourism and Festivals: Kotohira Shrine Festival and Sightseeing] {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:original pages]][[Category:Kotohira Shrines]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:Shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:Nemuro Province|Shrines]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines of Hokkaido]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:Buildings in Nemuro]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:History of Nemuro]] [[ja:金刀比羅神社 (根室市)]]

金刀比羅神社_(根室市)&action=edit [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:K]][[ja:金刀比羅神社 (根室市)]] {{Shrine | Name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | Image = [[File:Kotohira Shrine (Nemuro City).jpg|270px]]
Main Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|43|20|37.64|N|145|35|14.90|E}}}} | Location = Kotobira Town 1-4, {{ill|Nemuro|ja|根室市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}} | Position = {{ill-coordinates|43|20|37.64|N|145|35|14.90|E|inline,title}} | Deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命}} | Classification = Former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} | Established = 1806 (Bunka 3) | MainHall = Irimoya-zukuri (hip-and-gable style) | AlternateName = | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = August 9–11 | Rituals = | Map = {{Location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=43|lat_min=20|lat_sec=37.6|lon_deg=145|lon_min=35|lon_sec=14.9|label=Kotohira
Shrine|position=left|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|AlternativeMap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社), also known as Konpira Shrine, is located in {{ill|Nemuro|ja|根室市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}. == Deities == * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命}} * The shrine also holds sacred objects from shrines located on the {{ill|Northern Territories|ja|北方地域}} of {{ill|Taraku Island|ja|多楽島}}, {{ill|Shibotsu Island|ja|志発島}}, {{ill|Suisho Island|ja|水晶島}}, {{ill|Shikotan|ja|色丹島}}, and {{ill|Kunashiri|ja|国後島}}, conducting festivals on their respective anniversaries. == History == Kotohira Shrine was established in 1806 (Bunka 3) in {{ill|Nemuro Province|ja|根室国}}, {{ill|Nemuro District|ja|根室郡}} by {{ill|Kahē Takadaya|ja|高田屋嘉兵衛}}. It was relocated to its current site in 1881 (Meiji 14). The shrine was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} in 1881 and promoted to {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} in 1919 (Taishō 8). In 2005 (Heisei 18), the shrine celebrated its 200th anniversary, during which the sacred palanquin (mikoshi) was refurbished. A new shrine office was also constructed, including a "Mikoshi Hall and Festival Museum." == Kotohira Shrine Grand Festival == The annual grand festival is held over three days from August 9 (evening festival) to August 11 (main festival with portable shrine procession). The festival includes taiko drumming, performances with golden staves, floats, and traditional hand dances. Four festival districts, established during the {{ill|Taishō period|ja|大正時代}}, participate: "First Festival District," "Third Festival District," "Eastern Festival District," and "Western Festival District." On the night of August 9, a competition titled "Sound and Strength Performance" features floats and hand dances, while August 10 showcases taiko drumming and golden staves. The festival was designated an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Asset of Hokkaido|ja|無形民俗文化財}} in 2020 (Reiwa 2). == Address == * Kotobira Town 1-4, Nemuro, Hokkaido == External Links == * [https://www.nemuro-kotohira.com/ Official Website of Kotohira Shrine] * [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/top.html Hokkaido Shrine Association] * [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/16/16001.html Kotohira Shrine Information] * [http://www.city.nemuro.hokkaido.jp/section/kanko/event_cale/konpira/konpira.html Nemuro City Event Information: Kotohira Shrine Festival] {{ill-broken-link|date=August 2023}} * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.co.jp/HeartLand/3922/ |title=Nemuro Topics (Kotohira Shrine Festival images and videos) |date=April 27, 1999}} * [http://www.nemuro-kankou.com/events/kotohira.html Nemuro Tourist Association: Kotohira Shrine Grand Festival] {{ill-broken-link|date=August 2023}} * Shrine|position=left|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|AlternativeMap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社), also known as Konpira Shrine, is located in {{ill|Nemuro|ja|根室市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}. == Deities == * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命}} * The shrine also holds sacred objects from shrines located on the {{ill|Northern Territories|ja|北方地域}} of {{ill|Taraku Island|ja|多楽島}}, {{ill|Shibotsu Island|ja|志発島}}, {{ill|Suisho Island|ja|水晶島}}, {{ill|Shikotan|ja|色丹島}}, and {{ill|Kunashiri|ja|国後島}}, conducting festivals on their respective anniversaries. == History == Kotohira Shrine was established in 1806 (Bunka 3) in {{ill|Nemuro Province|ja|根室国}}, {{ill|Nemuro District|ja|根室郡}} by {{ill|Kahē Takadaya|ja|高田屋嘉兵衛}}. It was relocated to its current site in 1881 (Meiji 14). The shrine was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} in 1881 and promoted to {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} in 1919 (Taishō 8). In 2005 (Heisei 18), the shrine celebrated its 200th anniversary, during which the sacred palanquin (mikoshi) was refurbished. A new shrine office was also constructed, including a "Mikoshi Hall and Festival Museum." == Kotohira Shrine Grand Festival == The annual grand festival is held over three days from August 9 (evening festival) to August 11 (main festival with portable shrine procession). The festival includes taiko drumming, performances with golden staves, floats, and traditional hand dances. Four festival districts, established during the {{ill|Taishō period|ja|大正時代}}, participate: "First Festival District," "Third Festival District," "Eastern Festival District," and "Western Festival District." On the night of August 9, a competition titled "Sound and Strength Performance" features floats and hand dances, while August 10 showcases taiko drumming and golden staves. The festival was designated an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Asset of Hokkaido|ja|無形民俗文化財}} in 2020 (Reiwa 2). == Address == * Kotobira Town 1-4, Nemuro, Hokkaido == External Links == * Main Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|43|20|37.64|N|145|35|14.90|E}}}} | location = Kotobira Town 1-4, {{ill|Nemuro|ja|根室市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}} | Position = {{ill-coordinates|43|20|37.64|N|145|35|14.90|E|inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命}} | Classification = Former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} | Established = 1806 (Bunka 3) | MainHall = Irimoya-zukuri (hip-and-gable style) | AlternateName = | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = August 9–11 | Rituals = | Map = {{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=43|lat_min=20|lat_sec=37.6|lon_deg=145|lon_min=35|lon_sec=14.9|label=Kotohira
Shrine|position=left|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|AlternativeMap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社), also known as Konpira Shrine, is located in {{ill|Nemuro|ja|根室市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}. == Deities == * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命}} * The shrine also holds sacred objects from shrines located on the {{ill|Northern Territories|ja|北方地域}} of {{ill|Taraku Island|ja|多楽島}}, {{ill|Shibotsu Island|ja|志発島}}, {{ill|Suisho Island|ja|水晶島}}, {{ill|Shikotan|ja|色丹島}}, and {{ill|Kunashiri|ja|国後島}}, conducting festivals on their respective anniversaries. == History == Kotohira Shrine was established in 1806 (Bunka 3) in {{ill|Nemuro Province|ja|根室国}}, {{ill|Nemuro District|ja|根室郡}} by {{ill|Kahē Takadaya|ja|高田屋嘉兵衛}}. It was relocated to its current site in 1881 (Meiji 14). The shrine was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} in 1881 and promoted to {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} in 1919 (Taishō 8). In 2005 (Heisei 18), the shrine celebrated its 200th anniversary, during which the sacred palanquin (mikoshi) was refurbished. A new shrine office was also constructed, including a "Mikoshi Hall and Festival Museum." == Kotohira Shrine Grand Festival == The annual grand festival is held over three days from August 9 (evening festival) to August 11 (main festival with portable shrine procession). The festival includes taiko drumming, performances with golden staves, floats, and traditional hand dances. Four festival districts, established during the {{ill|Taishō period|ja|大正時代}}, participate: "First Festival District," "Third Festival District," "Eastern Festival District," and "Western Festival District." On the night of August 9, a competition titled "Sound and Strength Performance" features floats and hand dances, while August 10 showcases taiko drumming and golden staves. The festival was designated an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Asset of Hokkaido|ja|無形民俗文化財}} in 2020 (Reiwa 2). == Address == * Kotobira Town 1-4, Nemuro, Hokkaido == External Links == * [https://www.nemuro-kotohira.com/ Official Website of Kotohira Shrine] * [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/top.html Hokkaido Shrine Association] * [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/16/16001.html Kotohira Shrine Information] * [http://www.city.nemuro.hokkaido.jp/section/kanko/event_cale/konpira/konpira.html Nemuro City Event Information: Kotohira Shrine Festival] {{ill-broken-link|date=August 2023}} * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.co.jp/HeartLand/3922/ |title=Nemuro Topics (Kotohira Shrine Festival images and videos) |date=April 27, 1999}} * [http://www.nemuro-kankou.com/events/kotohira.html Nemuro Tourist Association: Kotohira Shrine Grand Festival] {{ill-broken-link|date=August 2023}} * Main Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|43|20|37.64|N|145|35|14.90|E}}}} | location = Kotobira Town 1-4, {{ill|Nemuro|ja|根室市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}} | Position = {{ill-coordinates|43|20|37.64|N|145|35|14.90|E|inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命}} | Classification = Former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} | Established = 1806 (Bunka 3) | MainHall = Irimoya-zukuri (hip-and-gable style) | AlternateName = | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = August 9–11 | Rituals = | Map = {{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=43|lat_min=20|lat_sec=37.6|lon_deg=145|lon_min=35|lon_sec=14.9|label=Kotohira
Shrine|position=left|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|AlternativeMap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社), also known as Konpira Shrine, is located in {{ill|Nemuro|ja|根室市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}. == Deities == * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命}} * The shrine also holds sacred objects from shrines located on the {{ill|Northern Territories|ja|北方地域}} of {{ill|Taraku Island|ja|多楽島}}, {{ill|Shibotsu Island|ja|志発島}}, {{ill|Suisho Island|ja|水晶島}}, {{ill|Shikotan|ja|色丹島}}, and {{ill|Kunashiri|ja|国後島}}, conducting festivals on their respective anniversaries. == History == Kotohira Shrine was established in 1806 (Bunka 3) in {{ill|Nemuro Province|ja|根室国}}, {{ill|Nemuro District|ja|根室郡}} by {{ill|Kahē Takadaya|ja|高田屋嘉兵衛}}. It was relocated to its current site in 1881 (Meiji 14). The shrine was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} in 1881 and promoted to {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} in 1919 (Taishō 8). In 2005 (Heisei 18), the shrine celebrated its 200th anniversary, during which the sacred palanquin (mikoshi) was refurbished. A new shrine office was also constructed, including a "Mikoshi Hall and Festival Museum." == Kotohira Shrine Grand Festival == The annual grand festival is held over three days from August 9 (evening festival) to August 11 (main festival with portable shrine procession). The festival includes taiko drumming, performances with golden staves, floats, and traditional hand dances. Four festival districts, established during the {{ill|Taishō period|ja|大正時代}}, participate: "First Festival District," "Third Festival District," "Eastern Festival District," and "Western Festival District." On the night of August 9, a competition titled "Sound and Strength Performance" features floats and hand dances, while August 10 showcases taiko drumming and golden staves. The festival was designated an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Asset of Hokkaido|ja|無形民俗文化財}} in 2020 (Reiwa 2). == Address == * Kotobira Town 1-4, Nemuro, Hokkaido == External Links == * [https://www.nemuro-kotohira.com/ Official Website of Kotohira Shrine] * [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/top.html Hokkaido Shrine Association] * [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/16/16001.html Kotohira Shrine Information] * [http://www.city.nemuro.hokkaido.jp/section/kanko/event_cale/konpira/konpira.html Nemuro City Event Information: Kotohira Shrine Festival] {{ill-broken-link|date=August 2023}} * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.co.jp/HeartLand/3922/ |title=Nemuro Topics (Kotohira Shrine Festival images and videos) |date=April 27, 1999}} * 金刀比羅神社_(根室市)&action=edit [[Category:Link complete]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:K]][[ja:金刀比羅神社 (根室市)]] {{Shrine | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | image = [[File:Kotohira Shrine (Nemuro City).jpg|270px]]
Main Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|43|20|37.64|N|145|35|14.90|E}}}} | location = Kotobira Town 1-4, {{ill|Nemuro|ja|根室市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}} | Position = {{ill-coordinates|43|20|37.64|N|145|35|14.90|E|inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命}} | Classification = Former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} | Established = 1806 (Bunka 3) | MainHall = Irimoya-zukuri (hip-and-gable style) | Alternatename = | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = August 9–11 | Rituals = | Map = {{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=43|lat_min=20|lat_sec=37.6|lon_deg=145|lon_min=35|lon_sec=14.9|label=Kotohira
Shrine|position=left|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|AlternativeMap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社), also known as Konpira Shrine, is located in {{ill|Nemuro|ja|根室市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}. == Deities == * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命}} * The shrine also holds sacred objects from shrines located on the {{ill|Northern Territories|ja|北方地域}} of {{ill|Taraku Island|ja|多楽島}}, {{ill|Shibotsu Island|ja|志発島}}, {{ill|Suisho Island|ja|水晶島}}, {{ill|Shikotan|ja|色丹島}}, and {{ill|Kunashiri|ja|国後島}}, conducting festivals on their respective anniversaries. == History == Kotohira Shrine was established in 1806 (Bunka 3) in {{ill|Nemuro Province|ja|根室国}}, {{ill|Nemuro District|ja|根室郡}} by {{ill|Kahē Takadaya|ja|高田屋嘉兵衛}}. It was relocated to its current site in 1881 (Meiji 14). The shrine was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} in 1881 and promoted to {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} in 1919 (Taishō 8). In 2005 (Heisei 18), the shrine celebrated its 200th anniversary, during which the sacred palanquin (mikoshi) was refurbished. A new shrine office was also constructed, including a "Mikoshi Hall and Festival Museum." == Kotohira Shrine Grand Festival == The annual grand festival is held over three days from August 9 (evening festival) to August 11 (main festival with portable shrine procession). The festival includes taiko drumming, performances with golden staves, floats, and traditional hand dances. Four festival districts, established during the {{ill|Taishō period|ja|大正時代}}, participate: "First Festival District," "Third Festival District," "Eastern Festival District," and "Western Festival District." On the night of August 9, a competition titled "Sound and Strength Performance" features floats and hand dances, while August 10 showcases taiko drumming and golden staves. The festival was designated an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Asset of Hokkaido|ja|無形民俗文化財}} in 2020 (Reiwa 2). == Address == * Kotobira Town 1-4, Nemuro, Hokkaido == External Links == * [https://www.nemuro-kotohira.com/ Official Website of Kotohira Shrine] * [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/top.html Hokkaido Shrine Association] * [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/16/16001.html Kotohira Shrine Information] * [http://www.city.nemuro.hokkaido.jp/section/kanko/event_cale/konpira/konpira.html Nemuro City Event Information: Kotohira Shrine Festival] {{ill-broken-link|date=August 2023}} * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.co.jp/HeartLand/3922/ |title=Nemuro Topics (Kotohira Shrine Festival images and videos) |date=April 27, 1999}} * [http://www.nemuro-kankou.com/events/kotohira.html Nemuro Tourist Association: Kotohira Shrine Grand Festival] {{ill-broken-link|date=August 2023}} * [http://crabnavi.ninpou.jp/nemuro-kotohira/top.html Nemuro Tourism and Festivals: Kotohira Shrine Festival and Sightseeing] {{Shinto-stub}} [[category:unsorted completion categories]] Main Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|43|20|37.64|N|145|35|14.90|E}}}} | location = Kotobira Town 1-4, {{ill|Nemuro|ja|根室市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}} | Position = {{ill-coordinates|43|20|37.64|N|145|35|14.90|E|inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命}} | Classification = Former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} | Established = 1806 (Bunka 3) | MainHall = Irimoya-zukuri (hip-and-gable style) | Alternatename = | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = August 9–11 | Rituals = | Map = {{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=43|lat_min=20|lat_sec=37.6|lon_deg=145|lon_min=35|lon_sec=14.9|label=Kotohira
Shrine|position=left|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|AlternativeMap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社), also known as Konpira Shrine, is located in {{ill|Nemuro|ja|根室市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}. == Deities == * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命}} * The shrine also holds sacred objects from shrines located on the {{ill|Northern Territories|ja|北方地域}} of {{ill|Taraku Island|ja|多楽島}}, {{ill|Shibotsu Island|ja|志発島}}, {{ill|Suisho Island|ja|水晶島}}, {{ill|Shikotan|ja|色丹島}}, and {{ill|Kunashiri|ja|国後島}}, conducting festivals on their respective anniversaries. == History == Kotohira Shrine was established in 1806 (Bunka 3) in {{ill|Nemuro Province|ja|根室国}}, {{ill|Nemuro District|ja|根室郡}} by {{ill|Kahē Takadaya|ja|高田屋嘉兵衛}}. It was relocated to its current site in 1881 (Meiji 14). The shrine was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} in 1881 and promoted to {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} in 1919 (Taishō 8). In 2005 (Heisei 18), the shrine celebrated its 200th anniversary, during which the sacred palanquin (mikoshi) was refurbished. A new shrine office was also constructed, including a "Mikoshi Hall and Festival Museum." == Kotohira Shrine Grand Festival == The annual grand festival is held over three days from August 9 (evening festival) to August 11 (main festival with portable shrine procession). The festival includes taiko drumming, performances with golden staves, floats, and traditional hand dances. Four festival districts, established during the {{ill|Taishō period|ja|大正時代}}, participate: "First Festival District," "Third Festival District," "Eastern Festival District," and "Western Festival District." On the night of August 9, a competition titled "Sound and Strength Performance" features floats and hand dances, while August 10 showcases taiko drumming and golden staves. The festival was designated an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Asset of Hokkaido|ja|無形民俗文化財}} in 2020 (Reiwa 2). == Address == * Kotobira Town 1-4, Nemuro, Hokkaido == External Links == * [https://www.nemuro-kotohira.com/ Official Website of Kotohira Shrine] * [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/top.html Hokkaido Shrine Association] * [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/16/16001.html Kotohira Shrine Information] * [http://www.city.nemuro.hokkaido.jp/section/kanko/event_cale/konpira/konpira.html Nemuro City Event Information: Kotohira Shrine Festival] {{ill-broken-link|date=August 2023}} * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.co.jp/HeartLand/3922/ |title=Nemuro Topics (Kotohira Shrine Festival images and videos) |date=April 27, 1999}} * [http://www.nemuro-kankou.com/events/kotohira.html Nemuro Tourist Association: Kotohira Shrine Grand Festival] {{ill-broken-link|date=August 2023}} * Shrine|position=left|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|AlternativeMap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社), also known as Konpira Shrine, is located in {{ill|Nemuro|ja|根室市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}. == Deities == * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命}} * The shrine also holds sacred objects from shrines located on the {{ill|Northern Territories|ja|北方地域}} of {{ill|Taraku Island|ja|多楽島}}, {{ill|Shibotsu Island|ja|志発島}}, {{ill|Suisho Island|ja|水晶島}}, {{ill|Shikotan|ja|色丹島}}, and {{ill|Kunashiri|ja|国後島}}, conducting festivals on their respective anniversaries. == History == Kotohira Shrine was established in 1806 (Bunka 3) in {{ill|Nemuro Province|ja|根室国}}, {{ill|Nemuro District|ja|根室郡}} by {{ill|Kahē Takadaya|ja|高田屋嘉兵衛}}. It was relocated to its current site in 1881 (Meiji 14). The shrine was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} in 1881 and promoted to {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} in 1919 (Taishō 8). In 2005 (Heisei 18), the shrine celebrated its 200th anniversary, during which the sacred palanquin (mikoshi) was refurbished. A new shrine office was also constructed, including a "Mikoshi Hall and Festival Museum." == Kotohira Shrine Grand Festival == The annual grand festival is held over three days from August 9 (evening festival) to August 11 (main festival with portable shrine procession). The festival includes taiko drumming, performances with golden staves, floats, and traditional hand dances. Four festival districts, established during the {{ill|Taishō period|ja|大正時代}}, participate: "First Festival District," "Third Festival District," "Eastern Festival District," and "Western Festival District." On the night of August 9, a competition titled "Sound and Strength Performance" features floats and hand dances, while August 10 showcases taiko drumming and golden staves. The festival was designated an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Asset of Hokkaido|ja|無形民俗文化財}} in 2020 (Reiwa 2). == Address == * Kotobira Town 1-4, Nemuro, Hokkaido == External Links == * It was relocated to its current site in 1881 (Meiji 14). The shrine was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} in 1881 and promoted to {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} in 1919 (Taishō 8). In 2005 (Heisei 18), the shrine celebrated its 200th anniversary, during which the sacred palanquin (mikoshi) was refurbished. A new shrine office was also constructed, including a "Mikoshi Hall and Festival Museum." == Kotohira Shrine Grand Festival == The annual grand festival is held over three days from August 9 (evening festival) to August 11 (main festival with portable shrine procession). The festival includes taiko drumming, performances with golden staves, floats, and traditional hand dances. Four festival districts, established during the {{ill|Taishō period|ja|大正時代}}, participate: "First Festival District," "Third Festival District," "Eastern Festival District," and "Western Festival District." On the night of August 9, a competition titled "Sound and Strength Performance" features floats and hand dances, while August 10 showcases taiko drumming and golden staves. The festival was designated an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Asset of Hokkaido|ja|無形民俗文化財}} in 2020 (Reiwa 2). == Address == * Kotobira Town 1-4, Nemuro, Hokkaido == External Links == * Main Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|43|20|37.64|N|145|35|14.90|E}}}} | location = Kotobira Town 1-4, {{ill|Nemuro|ja|根室市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}} | Position = {{ill-coordinates|43|20|37.64|N|145|35|14.90|E|inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命}} | Classification = Former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} | Established = 1806 (Bunka 3) | MainHall = Irimoya-zukuri (hip-and-gable style) | Alternatename = | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = August 9–11 | Rituals = | Map = {{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=43|lat_min=20|lat_sec=37.6|lon_deg=145|lon_min=35|lon_sec=14.9|label=Kotohira
Shrine|position=left|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|AlternativeMap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社), also known as Konpira Shrine, is located in {{ill|Nemuro|ja|根室市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}. == Deities == * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命}} * The shrine also holds sacred objects from shrines located on the {{ill|Northern Territories|ja|北方地域}} of {{ill|Taraku Island|ja|多楽島}}, {{ill|Shibotsu Island|ja|志発島}}, {{ill|Suisho Island|ja|水晶島}}, {{ill|Shikotan|ja|色丹島}}, and {{ill|Kunashiri|ja|国後島}}, conducting festivals on their respective anniversaries. == History == Kotohira Shrine was established in 1806 (Bunka 3) in {{ill|Nemuro Province|ja|根室国}}, {{ill|Nemuro District|ja|根室郡}} by {{ill|Kahē Takadaya|ja|高田屋嘉兵衛}}. It was relocated to its current site in 1881 (Meiji 14). The shrine was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} in 1881 and promoted to {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} in 1919 (Taishō 8). In 2005 (Heisei 18), the shrine celebrated its 200th anniversary, during which the sacred palanquin (mikoshi) was refurbished. A new shrine office was also constructed, including a "Mikoshi Hall and Festival Museum." == Kotohira Shrine Grand Festival == The annual grand festival is held over three days from August 9 (evening festival) to August 11 (main festival with portable shrine procession). The festival includes taiko drumming, performances with golden staves, floats, and traditional hand dances. Four festival districts, established during the {{ill|Taishō period|ja|大正時代}}, participate: "First Festival District," "Third Festival District," "Eastern Festival District," and "Western Festival District." On the night of August 9, a competition titled "Sound and Strength Performance" features floats and hand dances, while August 10 showcases taiko drumming and golden staves. The festival was designated an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Asset of Hokkaido|ja|無形民俗文化財}} in 2020 (Reiwa 2). == Address == * Kotobira Town 1-4, Nemuro, Hokkaido == External Links == * 金刀比羅神社_(根室市)&action=edit [[Category:Link complete]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution]][[Category:Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)]][[Category:Pages needing attribution]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution]][[Category:Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)]][[Category:Pages needing attribution]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution]][[Category:Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)]][[Category:Pages needing attribution]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution]][[Category:Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)]][[Category:Pages needing attribution]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution]][[Category:Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)]][[Category:Pages needing attribution]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution]][[Category:Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)]][[Category:Pages needing attribution]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution]][[Category:Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)]][[Category:Pages needing attribution]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution]][[Category:Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)]][[Category:Pages needing attribution]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution]][[Category:Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)]][[Category:Pages needing attribution]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution]][[Category:Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)]][[Category:Pages needing attribution]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution]][[Category:Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)]][[Category:Pages needing attribution]] [[Category:K]][[ja:金刀比羅神社 (根室市)]] {{Shrine | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kotohira Shrine (Nemuro City).jpg|270px]]
Main Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|43|20|37.64|N|145|35|14.90|E}}}} | location = Kotobira Town 1-4, {{ill|Nemuro|ja|根室市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}} | Position = {{ill-coordinates|43|20|37.64|N|145|35|14.90|E|inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命}} | Classification = Former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} | Established = 1806 (Bunka 3) | MainHall = Irimoya-zukuri (hip-and-gable style) | Alternatename = | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = August 9–11 | Rituals = | Map = {{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=43|lat_min=20|lat_sec=37.6|lon_deg=145|lon_min=35|lon_sec=14.9|label=Kotohira
Shrine|position=left|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|AlternativeMap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社), also known as Konpira Shrine, is located in {{ill|Nemuro|ja|根室市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}. == Deities == * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命}} * The shrine also holds sacred objects from shrines located on the {{ill|Northern Territories|ja|北方地域}} of {{ill|Taraku Island|ja|多楽島}}, {{ill|Shibotsu Island|ja|志発島}}, {{ill|Suisho Island|ja|水晶島}}, {{ill|Shikotan|ja|色丹島}}, and {{ill|Kunashiri|ja|国後島}}, conducting festivals on their respective anniversaries. == History == Kotohira Shrine was established in 1806 (Bunka 3) in {{ill|Nemuro Province|ja|根室国}}, {{ill|Nemuro District|ja|根室郡}} by {{ill|Kahē Takadaya|ja|高田屋嘉兵衛}}. It was relocated to its current site in 1881 (Meiji 14). The shrine was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} in 1881 and promoted to {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} in 1919 (Taishō 8). In 2005 (Heisei 18), the shrine celebrated its 200th anniversary, during which the sacred palanquin (mikoshi) was refurbished. A new shrine office was also constructed, including a "Mikoshi Hall and Festival Museum." == Kotohira Shrine Grand Festival == The annual grand festival is held over three days from August 9 (evening festival) to August 11 (main festival with portable shrine procession). The festival includes taiko drumming, performances with golden staves, floats, and traditional hand dances. Four festival districts, established during the {{ill|Taishō period|ja|大正時代}}, participate: "First Festival District," "Third Festival District," "Eastern Festival District," and "Western Festival District." On the night of August 9, a competition titled "Sound and Strength Performance" features floats and hand dances, while August 10 showcases taiko drumming and golden staves. The festival was designated an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Asset of Hokkaido|ja|無形民俗文化財}} in 2020 (Reiwa 2). == Address == * Kotobira Town 1-4, Nemuro, Hokkaido == External Links == * [https://www.nemuro-kotohira.com/ Official Website of Kotohira Shrine] * [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/top.html Hokkaido Shrine Association] * [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/16/16001.html Kotohira Shrine Information] * [http://www.city.nemuro.hokkaido.jp/section/kanko/event_cale/konpira/konpira.html Nemuro City Event Information: Kotohira Shrine Festival] {{ill-broken-link|date=August 2023}} * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.co.jp/HeartLand/3922/ |title=Nemuro Topics (Kotohira Shrine Festival images and videos) |date=April 27, 1999}} * 金刀比羅神社_(根室市)&action=edit [[Category:Link complete]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)]][[Category:K]][[ja:金刀比羅神社 (根室市)]] {{Shrine | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kotohira Shrine (Nemuro City).jpg|270px]]
Main Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|43|20|37.64|N|145|35|14.90|E}}}} | location = Kotobira Town 1-4, {{ill|Nemuro|ja|根室市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}} | Position = {{ill-coordinates|43|20|37.64|N|145|35|14.90|E|inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命}} | Classification = Former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} | Established = 1806 (Bunka 3) | MainHall = Irimoya-zukuri (hip-and-gable style) | Alternatename = | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = August 9–11 | Rituals = | Map = {{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=43|lat_min=20|lat_sec=37.6|lon_deg=145|lon_min=35|lon_sec=14.9|label=Kotohira
Shrine|position=left|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|AlternativeMap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社), also known as Konpira Shrine, is located in {{ill|Nemuro|ja|根室市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}. == Deities == * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命}} * The shrine also holds sacred objects from shrines located on the {{ill|Northern Territories|ja|北方地域}} of {{ill|Taraku Island|ja|多楽島}}, {{ill|Shibotsu Island|ja|志発島}}, {{ill|Suisho Island|ja|水晶島}}, {{ill|Shikotan|ja|色丹島}}, and {{ill|Kunashiri|ja|国後島}}, conducting festivals on their respective anniversaries. == History == Kotohira Shrine was established in 1806 (Bunka 3) in {{ill|Nemuro Province|ja|根室国}}, {{ill|Nemuro District|ja|根室郡}} by {{ill|Kahē Takadaya|ja|高田屋嘉兵衛}}. It was relocated to its current site in 1881 (Meiji 14). The shrine was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} in 1881 and promoted to {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} in 1919 (Taishō 8). In 2005 (Heisei 18), the shrine celebrated its 200th anniversary, during which the sacred palanquin (mikoshi) was refurbished. A new shrine office was also constructed, including a "Mikoshi Hall and Festival Museum." == Kotohira Shrine Grand Festival == The annual grand festival is held over three days from August 9 (evening festival) to August 11 (main festival with portable shrine procession). The festival includes taiko drumming, performances with golden staves, floats, and traditional hand dances. Four festival districts, established during the {{ill|Taishō period|ja|大正時代}}, participate: "First Festival District," "Third Festival District," "Eastern Festival District," and "Western Festival District." On the night of August 9, a competition titled "Sound and Strength Performance" features floats and hand dances, while August 10 showcases taiko drumming and golden staves. The festival was designated an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Asset of Hokkaido|ja|無形民俗文化財}} in 2020 (Reiwa 2). == Address == * Kotobira Town 1-4, Nemuro, Hokkaido == External Links == * [https://www.nemuro-kotohira.com/ Official Website of Kotohira Shrine] * [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/top.html Hokkaido Shrine Association] * [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/16/16001.html Kotohira Shrine Information] * [http://www.city.nemuro.hokkaido.jp/section/kanko/event_cale/konpira/konpira.html Nemuro City Event Information: Kotohira Shrine Festival] {{ill-broken-link|date=August 2023}} * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.co.jp/HeartLand/3922/ |title=Nemuro Topics (Kotohira Shrine Festival images and videos) |date=April 27, 1999}} * [http://www.nemuro-kankou.com/events/kotohira.html Nemuro Tourist Association: Kotohira Shrine Grand Festival] {{ill-broken-link|date=August 2023}} * [http://crabnavi.ninpou.jp/nemuro-kotohira/top.html Nemuro Tourism and Festivals: Kotohira Shrine Festival and Sightseeing] {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kotohira Shrine (Nemuro City).jpg|270px]]
Main Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|43|20|37.64|N|145|35|14.90|E}}}} | location = Kotobira Town 1-4, {{ill|Nemuro|ja|根室市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}} | Position = {{ill-coordinates|43|20|37.64|N|145|35|14.90|E|inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命}} | Classification = Former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} | Established = 1806 (Bunka 3) | MainHall = Irimoya-zukuri (hip-and-gable style) | Alternatename = | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = August 9–11 | Rituals = | Map = {{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=43|lat_min=20|lat_sec=37.6|lon_deg=145|lon_min=35|lon_sec=14.9|label=Kotohira
Shrine|position=left|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|AlternativeMap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社), also known as Konpira Shrine, is located in {{ill|Nemuro|ja|根室市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}. == Deities == * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命}} * The shrine also holds sacred objects from shrines located on the {{ill|Northern Territories|ja|北方地域}} of {{ill|Taraku Island|ja|多楽島}}, {{ill|Shibotsu Island|ja|志発島}}, {{ill|Suisho Island|ja|水晶島}}, {{ill|Shikotan|ja|色丹島}}, and {{ill|Kunashiri|ja|国後島}}, conducting festivals on their respective anniversaries. == History == Kotohira Shrine was established in 1806 (Bunka 3) in {{ill|Nemuro Province|ja|根室国}}, {{ill|Nemuro District|ja|根室郡}} by {{ill|Kahē Takadaya|ja|高田屋嘉兵衛}}. It was relocated to its current site in 1881 (Meiji 14). The shrine was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} in 1881 and promoted to {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} in 1919 (Taishō 8). In 2005 (Heisei 18), the shrine celebrated its 200th anniversary, during which the sacred palanquin (mikoshi) was refurbished. A new shrine office was also constructed, including a "Mikoshi Hall and Festival Museum." == Kotohira Shrine Grand Festival == The annual grand festival is held over three days from August 9 (evening festival) to August 11 (main festival with portable shrine procession). The festival includes taiko drumming, performances with golden staves, floats, and traditional hand dances. Four festival districts, established during the {{ill|Taishō period|ja|大正時代}}, participate: "First Festival District," "Third Festival District," "Eastern Festival District," and "Western Festival District." On the night of August 9, a competition titled "Sound and Strength Performance" features floats and hand dances, while August 10 showcases taiko drumming and golden staves. The festival was designated an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Asset of Hokkaido|ja|無形民俗文化財}} in 2020 (Reiwa 2). == Address == * Kotobira Town 1-4, Nemuro, Hokkaido == External Links == * [https://www.nemuro-kotohira.com/ Official Website of Kotohira Shrine] * [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/top.html Hokkaido Shrine Association] * [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/16/16001.html Kotohira Shrine Information] * [http://www.city.nemuro.hokkaido.jp/section/kanko/event_cale/konpira/konpira.html Nemuro City Event Information: Kotohira Shrine Festival] {{ill-broken-link|date=August 2023}} * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.co.jp/HeartLand/3922/ |title=Nemuro Topics (Kotohira Shrine Festival images and videos) |date=April 27, 1999}} * [http://www.nemuro-kankou.com/events/kotohira.html Nemuro Tourist Association: Kotohira Shrine Grand Festival] {{ill-broken-link|date=August 2023}} * [http://crabnavi.ninpou.jp/nemuro-kotohira/top.html Nemuro Tourism and Festivals: Kotohira Shrine Festival and Sightseeing] {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] It was relocated to its current site in 1881 (Meiji 14). The shrine was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} in 1881 and promoted to {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} in 1919 (Taishō 8). In 2005 (Heisei 18), the shrine celebrated its 200th anniversary, during which the sacred palanquin (mikoshi) was refurbished. A new shrine office was also constructed, including a "Mikoshi Hall and Festival Museum." == Kotohira Shrine Grand Festival == The annual grand festival is held over three days from August 9 (evening festival) to August 11 (main festival with portable shrine procession). The festival includes taiko drumming, performances with golden staves, floats, and traditional hand dances. Four festival districts, established during the {{ill|Taishō period|ja|大正時代}}, participate: "First Festival District," "Third Festival District," "Eastern Festival District," and "Western Festival District." On the night of August 9, a competition titled "Sound and Strength Performance" features floats and hand dances, while August 10 showcases taiko drumming and golden staves. The festival was designated an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Asset of Hokkaido|ja|無形民俗文化財}} in 2020 (Reiwa 2). == Address == * Kotobira Town 1-4, Nemuro, Hokkaido == External Links == * Main Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|43|20|37.64|N|145|35|14.90|E}}}} | location = Kotobira Town 1-4, {{ill|Nemuro|ja|根室市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}} | Position = {{ill-coordinates|43|20|37.64|N|145|35|14.90|E|inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命}} | Classification = Former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} | Established = 1806 (Bunka 3) | MainHall = Irimoya-zukuri (hip-and-gable style) | Alternatename = | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = August 9–11 | Rituals = | Map = {{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=43|lat_min=20|lat_sec=37.6|lon_deg=145|lon_min=35|lon_sec=14.9|label=Kotohira
Shrine|position=left|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|AlternativeMap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社), also known as Konpira Shrine, is located in {{ill|Nemuro|ja|根室市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}. == Deities == * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命}} * The shrine also holds sacred objects from shrines located on the {{ill|Northern Territories|ja|北方地域}} of {{ill|Taraku Island|ja|多楽島}}, {{ill|Shibotsu Island|ja|志発島}}, {{ill|Suisho Island|ja|水晶島}}, {{ill|Shikotan|ja|色丹島}}, and {{ill|Kunashiri|ja|国後島}}, conducting festivals on their respective anniversaries. == History == Kotohira Shrine was established in 1806 (Bunka 3) in {{ill|Nemuro Province|ja|根室国}}, {{ill|Nemuro District|ja|根室郡}} by {{ill|Kahē Takadaya|ja|高田屋嘉兵衛}}. It was relocated to its current site in 1881 (Meiji 14). The shrine was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} in 1881 and promoted to {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} in 1919 (Taishō 8). In 2005 (Heisei 18), the shrine celebrated its 200th anniversary, during which the sacred palanquin (mikoshi) was refurbished. A new shrine office was also constructed, including a "Mikoshi Hall and Festival Museum." == Kotohira Shrine Grand Festival == The annual grand festival is held over three days from August 9 (evening festival) to August 11 (main festival with portable shrine procession). The festival includes taiko drumming, performances with golden staves, floats, and traditional hand dances. Four festival districts, established during the {{ill|Taishō period|ja|大正時代}}, participate: "First Festival District," "Third Festival District," "Eastern Festival District," and "Western Festival District." On the night of August 9, a competition titled "Sound and Strength Performance" features floats and hand dances, while August 10 showcases taiko drumming and golden staves. The festival was designated an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Asset of Hokkaido|ja|無形民俗文化財}} in 2020 (Reiwa 2). == Address == * Kotobira Town 1-4, Nemuro, Hokkaido == External Links == * Main Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|43|20|37.64|N|145|35|14.90|E}}}} | location = Kotobira Town 1-4, {{ill|Nemuro|ja|根室市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}} | Position = {{ill-coordinates|43|20|37.64|N|145|35|14.90|E|inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命}} | Classification = Former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} | Established = 1806 (Bunka 3) | MainHall = Irimoya-zukuri (hip-and-gable style) | Alternatename = | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = August 9–11 | Rituals = | Map = {{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=43|lat_min=20|lat_sec=37.6|lon_deg=145|lon_min=35|lon_sec=14.9|label=Kotohira
Shrine|position=left|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|AlternativeMap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社), also known as Konpira Shrine, is located in {{ill|Nemuro|ja|根室市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}. == Deities == * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命}} * The shrine also holds sacred objects from shrines located on the {{ill|Northern Territories|ja|北方地域}} of {{ill|Taraku Island|ja|多楽島}}, {{ill|Shibotsu Island|ja|志発島}}, {{ill|Suisho Island|ja|水晶島}}, {{ill|Shikotan|ja|色丹島}}, and {{ill|Kunashiri|ja|国後島}}, conducting festivals on their respective anniversaries. == History == Kotohira Shrine was established in 1806 (Bunka 3) in {{ill|Nemuro Province|ja|根室国}}, {{ill|Nemuro District|ja|根室郡}} by {{ill|Kahē Takadaya|ja|高田屋嘉兵衛}}. It was relocated to its current site in 1881 (Meiji 14). The shrine was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} in 1881 and promoted to {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} in 1919 (Taishō 8). In 2005 (Heisei 18), the shrine celebrated its 200th anniversary, during which the sacred palanquin (mikoshi) was refurbished. A new shrine office was also constructed, including a "Mikoshi Hall and Festival Museum." == Kotohira Shrine Grand Festival == The annual grand festival is held over three days from August 9 (evening festival) to August 11 (main festival with portable shrine procession). The festival includes taiko drumming, performances with golden staves, floats, and traditional hand dances. Four festival districts, established during the {{ill|Taishō period|ja|大正時代}}, participate: "First Festival District," "Third Festival District," "Eastern Festival District," and "Western Festival District." On the night of August 9, a competition titled "Sound and Strength Performance" features floats and hand dances, while August 10 showcases taiko drumming and golden staves. The festival was designated an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Asset of Hokkaido|ja|無形民俗文化財}} in 2020 (Reiwa 2). == Address == * Kotobira Town 1-4, Nemuro, Hokkaido == External Links == * [https://www.nemuro-kotohira.com/ Official Website of Kotohira Shrine] * [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/top.html Hokkaido Shrine Association] * [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/16/16001.html Kotohira Shrine Information] * [http://www.city.nemuro.hokkaido.jp/section/kanko/event_cale/konpira/konpira.html Nemuro City Event Information: Kotohira Shrine Festival] {{ill-broken-link|date=August 2023}} * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.co.jp/HeartLand/3922/ |title=Nemuro Topics (Kotohira Shrine Festival images and videos) |date=April 27, 1999}} * [http://www.nemuro-kankou.com/events/kotohira.html Nemuro Tourist Association: Kotohira Shrine Grand Festival] {{ill-broken-link|date=August 2023}} * Main Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|43|20|37.64|N|145|35|14.90|E}}}} | location = Kotobira Town 1-4, {{ill|Nemuro|ja|根室市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}} | Position = {{ill-coordinates|43|20|37.64|N|145|35|14.90|E|inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命}} | Classification = Former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} | Established = 1806 (Bunka 3) | MainHall = Irimoya-zukuri (hip-and-gable style) | Alternatename = | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = August 9–11 | Rituals = | Map = {{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=43|lat_min=20|lat_sec=37.6|lon_deg=145|lon_min=35|lon_sec=14.9|label=Kotohira
Shrine|position=left|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|AlternativeMap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社), also known as Konpira Shrine, is located in {{ill|Nemuro|ja|根室市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}. == Deities == * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命}} * The shrine also holds sacred objects from shrines located on the {{ill|Northern Territories|ja|北方地域}} of {{ill|Taraku Island|ja|多楽島}}, {{ill|Shibotsu Island|ja|志発島}}, {{ill|Suisho Island|ja|水晶島}}, {{ill|Shikotan|ja|色丹島}}, and {{ill|Kunashiri|ja|国後島}}, conducting festivals on their respective anniversaries. == History == Kotohira Shrine was established in 1806 (Bunka 3) in {{ill|Nemuro Province|ja|根室国}}, {{ill|Nemuro District|ja|根室郡}} by {{ill|Kahē Takadaya|ja|高田屋嘉兵衛}}. It was relocated to its current site in 1881 (Meiji 14). The shrine was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} in 1881 and promoted to {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} in 1919 (Taishō 8). In 2005 (Heisei 18), the shrine celebrated its 200th anniversary, during which the sacred palanquin (mikoshi) was refurbished. A new shrine office was also constructed, including a "Mikoshi Hall and Festival Museum." == Kotohira Shrine Grand Festival == The annual grand festival is held over three days from August 9 (evening festival) to August 11 (main festival with portable shrine procession). The festival includes taiko drumming, performances with golden staves, floats, and traditional hand dances. Four festival districts, established during the {{ill|Taishō period|ja|大正時代}}, participate: "First Festival District," "Third Festival District," "Eastern Festival District," and "Western Festival District." On the night of August 9, a competition titled "Sound and Strength Performance" features floats and hand dances, while August 10 showcases taiko drumming and golden staves. The festival was designated an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Asset of Hokkaido|ja|無形民俗文化財}} in 2020 (Reiwa 2). == Address == * Kotobira Town 1-4, Nemuro, Hokkaido == External Links == * 金刀比羅神社_(根室市)&action=edit [[Category:Link complete]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)-qq]] [[Category:Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)-qq]] [[Category:Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)-qq]] [[Category:Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)-qq]] [[Category:Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)-qq]] [[Category:Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)-qq]] [[Category:Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)-qq]] [[Category:Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)-qq]] [[Category:Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)-qq]] [[Category:Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)-qq]] [[Category:Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)-qq]] [[Category:Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)]][[Category:K]][[ja:金刀比羅神社 (根室市)]] {{Shrine | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kotohira Shrine (Nemuro City).jpg|270px]]
Main Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|43|20|37.64|N|145|35|14.90|E}}}} | location = Kotobira Town 1-4, {{ill|Nemuro|ja|根室市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}} | Position = {{ill-coordinates|43|20|37.64|N|145|35|14.90|E|inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命}} | Classification = Former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} | Established = 1806 (Bunka 3) | MainHall = Irimoya-zukuri (hip-and-gable style) | Alternatename = | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = August 9–11 | Rituals = | Map = {{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=43|lat_min=20|lat_sec=37.6|lon_deg=145|lon_min=35|lon_sec=14.9|label=Kotohira
Shrine|position=left|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|AlternativeMap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社), also known as Konpira Shrine, is located in {{ill|Nemuro|ja|根室市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}. == Deities == * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命}} * The shrine also holds sacred objects from shrines located on the {{ill|Northern Territories|ja|北方地域}} of {{ill|Taraku Island|ja|多楽島}}, {{ill|Shibotsu Island|ja|志発島}}, {{ill|Suisho Island|ja|水晶島}}, {{ill|Shikotan|ja|色丹島}}, and {{ill|Kunashiri|ja|国後島}}, conducting festivals on their respective anniversaries. == History == Kotohira Shrine was established in 1806 (Bunka 3) in {{ill|Nemuro Province|ja|根室国}}, {{ill|Nemuro District|ja|根室郡}} by {{ill|Kahē Takadaya|ja|高田屋嘉兵衛}}. It was relocated to its current site in 1881 (Meiji 14). The shrine was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} in 1881 and promoted to {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} in 1919 (Taishō 8). In 2005 (Heisei 18), the shrine celebrated its 200th anniversary, during which the sacred palanquin (mikoshi) was refurbished. A new shrine office was also constructed, including a "Mikoshi Hall and Festival Museum." == Kotohira Shrine Grand Festival == The annual grand festival is held over three days from August 9 (evening festival) to August 11 (main festival with portable shrine procession). The festival includes taiko drumming, performances with golden staves, floats, and traditional hand dances. Four festival districts, established during the {{ill|Taishō period|ja|大正時代}}, participate: "First Festival District," "Third Festival District," "Eastern Festival District," and "Western Festival District." On the night of August 9, a competition titled "Sound and Strength Performance" features floats and hand dances, while August 10 showcases taiko drumming and golden staves. The festival was designated an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Asset of Hokkaido|ja|無形民俗文化財}} in 2020 (Reiwa 2). == Address == * Kotobira Town 1-4, Nemuro, Hokkaido == External Links == * [https://www.nemuro-kotohira.com/ Official Website of Kotohira Shrine] * [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/top.html Hokkaido Shrine Association] * [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/16/16001.html Kotohira Shrine Information] * [http://www.city.nemuro.hokkaido.jp/section/kanko/event_cale/konpira/konpira.html Nemuro City Event Information: Kotohira Shrine Festival] {{ill-broken-link|date=August 2023}} * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.co.jp/HeartLand/3922/ |title=Nemuro Topics (Kotohira Shrine Festival images and videos) |date=April 27, 1999}} * [http://www.nemuro-kankou.com/events/kotohira.html Nemuro Tourist Association: Kotohira Shrine Grand Festival] {{ill-broken-link|date=August 2023}} * Main Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|43|20|37.64|N|145|35|14.90|E}}}} | location = Kotobira Town 1-4, {{ill|Nemuro|ja|根室市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}} | Position = {{ill-coordinates|43|20|37.64|N|145|35|14.90|E|inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命}} | Classification = Former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} | Established = 1806 (Bunka 3) | MainHall = Irimoya-zukuri (hip-and-gable style) | Alternatename = | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = August 9–11 | Rituals = | Map = {{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=43|lat_min=20|lat_sec=37.6|lon_deg=145|lon_min=35|lon_sec=14.9|label=Kotohira
Shrine|position=left|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|AlternativeMap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社), also known as Konpira Shrine, is located in {{ill|Nemuro|ja|根室市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}. == Deities == * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命}} * The shrine also holds sacred objects from shrines located on the {{ill|Northern Territories|ja|北方地域}} of {{ill|Taraku Island|ja|多楽島}}, {{ill|Shibotsu Island|ja|志発島}}, {{ill|Suisho Island|ja|水晶島}}, {{ill|Shikotan|ja|色丹島}}, and {{ill|Kunashiri|ja|国後島}}, conducting festivals on their respective anniversaries. == History == Kotohira Shrine was established in 1806 (Bunka 3) in {{ill|Nemuro Province|ja|根室国}}, {{ill|Nemuro District|ja|根室郡}} by {{ill|Kahē Takadaya|ja|高田屋嘉兵衛}}. It was relocated to its current site in 1881 (Meiji 14). The shrine was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} in 1881 and promoted to {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} in 1919 (Taishō 8). In 2005 (Heisei 18), the shrine celebrated its 200th anniversary, during which the sacred palanquin (mikoshi) was refurbished. A new shrine office was also constructed, including a "Mikoshi Hall and Festival Museum." == Kotohira Shrine Grand Festival == The annual grand festival is held over three days from August 9 (evening festival) to August 11 (main festival with portable shrine procession). The festival includes taiko drumming, performances with golden staves, floats, and traditional hand dances. Four festival districts, established during the {{ill|Taishō period|ja|大正時代}}, participate: "First Festival District," "Third Festival District," "Eastern Festival District," and "Western Festival District." On the night of August 9, a competition titled "Sound and Strength Performance" features floats and hand dances, while August 10 showcases taiko drumming and golden staves. The festival was designated an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Asset of Hokkaido|ja|無形民俗文化財}} in 2020 (Reiwa 2). == Address == * Kotobira Town 1-4, Nemuro, Hokkaido == External Links == *
