Main Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|43|20|37.64|N|145|35|14.90|E}}}} | location = Kotobira Town 1-4, {{ill|Nemuro|ja|根室市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}} | Position = {{ill-coordinates|43|20|37.64|N|145|35|14.90|E|inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命}} | Classification = Former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} | Established = 1806 (Bunka 3) | MainHall = Irimoya-zukuri (hip-and-gable style) | Alternatename = | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = August 9–11 | Rituals = | Map = {{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=43|lat_min=20|lat_sec=37.6|lon_deg=145|lon_min=35|lon_sec=14.9|label=Kotohira
Shrine|position=left|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|AlternativeMap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社), also known as Konpira Shrine, is located in {{ill|Nemuro|ja|根室市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}. == Deities == * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命}} * The shrine also holds sacred objects from shrines located on the {{ill|Northern Territories|ja|北方地域}} of {{ill|Taraku Island|ja|多楽島}}, {{ill|Shibotsu Island|ja|志発島}}, {{ill|Suisho Island|ja|水晶島}}, {{ill|Shikotan|ja|色丹島}}, and {{ill|Kunashiri|ja|国後島}}, conducting festivals on their respective anniversaries. == History == Kotohira Shrine was established in 1806 (Bunka 3) in {{ill|Nemuro Province|ja|根室国}}, {{ill|Nemuro District|ja|根室郡}} by {{ill|Kahē Takadaya|ja|高田屋嘉兵衛}}. It was relocated to its current site in 1881 (Meiji 14). The shrine was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} in 1881 and promoted to {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} in 1919 (Taishō 8). In 2005 (Heisei 18), the shrine celebrated its 200th anniversary, during which the sacred palanquin (mikoshi) was refurbished. A new shrine office was also constructed, including a "Mikoshi Hall and Festival Museum." == Kotohira Shrine Grand Festival == The annual grand festival is held over three days from August 9 (evening festival) to August 11 (main festival with portable shrine procession). The festival includes taiko drumming, performances with golden staves, floats, and traditional hand dances. Four festival districts, established during the {{ill|Taishō period|ja|大正時代}}, participate: "First Festival District," "Third Festival District," "Eastern Festival District," and "Western Festival District." On the night of August 9, a competition titled "Sound and Strength Performance" features floats and hand dances, while August 10 showcases taiko drumming and golden staves. The festival was designated an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Asset of Hokkaido|ja|無形民俗文化財}} in 2020 (Reiwa 2). == Address == * Kotobira Town 1-4, Nemuro, Hokkaido == External Links == * [https://www.nemuro-kotohira.com/ Official Website of Kotohira Shrine] * [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/top.html Hokkaido Shrine Association] * [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/16/16001.html Kotohira Shrine Information] * [http://www.city.nemuro.hokkaido.jp/section/kanko/event_cale/konpira/konpira.html Nemuro City Event Information: Kotohira Shrine Festival] {{ill-broken-link|date=August 2023}} * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.co.jp/HeartLand/3922/ |title=Nemuro Topics (Kotohira Shrine Festival images and videos) |date=April 27, 1999}} * [http://www.nemuro-kankou.com/events/kotohira.html Nemuro Tourist Association: Kotohira Shrine Grand Festival] {{ill-broken-link|date=August 2023}} * Main Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|43|20|37.64|N|145|35|14.90|E}}}} | location = Kotobira Town 1-4, {{ill|Nemuro|ja|根室市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}} | Position = {{ill-coordinates|43|20|37.64|N|145|35|14.90|E|inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命}} | Classification = Former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} | Established = 1806 (Bunka 3) | MainHall = Irimoya-zukuri (hip-and-gable style) | Alternatename = | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = August 9–11 | Rituals = | Map = {{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=43|lat_min=20|lat_sec=37.6|lon_deg=145|lon_min=35|lon_sec=14.9|label=Kotohira
Shrine|position=left|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|AlternativeMap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社), also known as Konpira Shrine, is located in {{ill|Nemuro|ja|根室市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}. == Deities == * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命}} * The shrine also holds sacred objects from shrines located on the {{ill|Northern Territories|ja|北方地域}} of {{ill|Taraku Island|ja|多楽島}}, {{ill|Shibotsu Island|ja|志発島}}, {{ill|Suisho Island|ja|水晶島}}, {{ill|Shikotan|ja|色丹島}}, and {{ill|Kunashiri|ja|国後島}}, conducting festivals on their respective anniversaries. == History == Kotohira Shrine was established in 1806 (Bunka 3) in {{ill|Nemuro Province|ja|根室国}}, {{ill|Nemuro District|ja|根室郡}} by {{ill|Kahē Takadaya|ja|高田屋嘉兵衛}}. It was relocated to its current site in 1881 (Meiji 14). The shrine was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} in 1881 and promoted to {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} in 1919 (Taishō 8). In 2005 (Heisei 18), the shrine celebrated its 200th anniversary, during which the sacred palanquin (mikoshi) was refurbished. A new shrine office was also constructed, including a "Mikoshi Hall and Festival Museum." == Kotohira Shrine Grand Festival == The annual grand festival is held over three days from August 9 (evening festival) to August 11 (main festival with portable shrine procession). The festival includes taiko drumming, performances with golden staves, floats, and traditional hand dances. Four festival districts, established during the {{ill|Taishō period|ja|大正時代}}, participate: "First Festival District," "Third Festival District," "Eastern Festival District," and "Western Festival District." On the night of August 9, a competition titled "Sound and Strength Performance" features floats and hand dances, while August 10 showcases taiko drumming and golden staves. The festival was designated an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Asset of Hokkaido|ja|無形民俗文化財}} in 2020 (Reiwa 2). == Address == * Kotobira Town 1-4, Nemuro, Hokkaido == External Links == * Kotohira Shrine Information] * [http://www.city.nemuro.hokkaido.jp/section/kanko/event_cale/konpira/konpira.html Nemuro City Event Information: Kotohira Shrine Festival] {{ill-broken-link|date=August Main Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|43|20|37.64|N|145|35|14.90|E}}}} | location = Kotobira Town 1-4, {{ill|Nemuro|ja|根室市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}} | Position = {{ill-coordinates|43|20|37.64|N|145|35|14.90|E|inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命}} | Classification = Former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} | Established = 1806 (Bunka 3) | MainHall = Irimoya-zukuri (hip-and-gable style) | Alternatename = | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = August 9–11 | Rituals = | Map = {{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=43|lat_min=20|lat_sec=37.6|lon_deg=145|lon_min=35|lon_sec=14.9|label=Kotohira
Shrine|position=left|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|AlternativeMap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社), also known as Konpira Shrine, is located in {{ill|Nemuro|ja|根室市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}. == Deities == * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命}} * The shrine also holds sacred objects from shrines located on the {{ill|Northern Territories|ja|北方地域}} of {{ill|Taraku Island|ja|多楽島}}, {{ill|Shibotsu Island|ja|志発島}}, {{ill|Suisho Island|ja|水晶島}}, {{ill|Shikotan|ja|色丹島}}, and {{ill|Kunashiri|ja|国後島}}, conducting festivals on their respective anniversaries. == History == Kotohira Shrine was established in 1806 (Bunka 3) in {{ill|Nemuro Province|ja|根室国}}, {{ill|Nemuro District|ja|根室郡}} by {{ill|Kahē Takadaya|ja|高田屋嘉兵衛}}. It was relocated to its current site in 1881 (Meiji 14). The shrine was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} in 1881 and promoted to {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} in 1919 (Taishō 8). In 2005 (Heisei 18), the shrine celebrated its 200th anniversary, during which the sacred palanquin (mikoshi) was refurbished. A new shrine office was also constructed, including a "Mikoshi Hall and Festival Museum." == Kotohira Shrine Grand Festival == The annual grand festival is held over three days from August 9 (evening festival) to August 11 (main festival with portable shrine procession). The festival includes taiko drumming, performances with golden staves, floats, and traditional hand dances. Four festival districts, established during the {{ill|Taishō period|ja|大正時代}}, participate: "First Festival District," "Third Festival District," "Eastern Festival District," and "Western Festival District." On the night of August 9, a competition titled "Sound and Strength Performance" features floats and hand dances, while August 10 showcases taiko drumming and golden staves. The festival was designated an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Asset of Hokkaido|ja|無形民俗文化財}} in 2020 (Reiwa 2). == Address == * Kotobira Town 1-4, Nemuro, Hokkaido == External Links == * [https://www.nemuro-kotohira.com/ Official Website of Kotohira Shrine] * [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/top.html Hokkaido Shrine Association] * [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/16/16001.html Kotohira Shrine Information] * [http://www.city.nemuro.hokkaido.jp/section/kanko/event_cale/konpira/konpira.html Nemuro City Event Information: Kotohira Shrine Festival] {{ill-broken-link|date=August 2023}} * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.co.jp/HeartLand/3922/ |title=Nemuro Topics (Kotohira Shrine Festival images and videos) |date=April 27, 1999}} * [http://www.nemuro-kankou.com/events/kotohira.html Nemuro Tourist Association: Kotohira Shrine Grand Festival] {{ill-broken-link|date=August 2023}} * Main Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|43|20|37.64|N|145|35|14.90|E}}}} | location = Kotobira Town 1-4, {{ill|Nemuro|ja|根室市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}} | Position = {{ill-coordinates|43|20|37.64|N|145|35|14.90|E|inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命}} | Classification = Former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} | Established = 1806 (Bunka 3) | MainHall = Irimoya-zukuri (hip-and-gable style) | Alternatename = | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = August 9–11 | Rituals = | Map = {{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=43|lat_min=20|lat_sec=37.6|lon_deg=145|lon_min=35|lon_sec=14.9|label=Kotohira
Shrine|position=left|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|AlternativeMap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社), also known as Konpira Shrine, is located in {{ill|Nemuro|ja|根室市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}. == Deities == * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命}} * The shrine also holds sacred objects from shrines located on the {{ill|Northern Territories|ja|北方地域}} of {{ill|Taraku Island|ja|多楽島}}, {{ill|Shibotsu Island|ja|志発島}}, {{ill|Suisho Island|ja|水晶島}}, {{ill|Shikotan|ja|色丹島}}, and {{ill|Kunashiri|ja|国後島}}, conducting festivals on their respective anniversaries. == History == Kotohira Shrine was established in 1806 (Bunka 3) in {{ill|Nemuro Province|ja|根室国}}, {{ill|Nemuro District|ja|根室郡}} by {{ill|Kahē Takadaya|ja|高田屋嘉兵衛}}. It was relocated to its current site in 1881 (Meiji 14). The shrine was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} in 1881 and promoted to {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} in 1919 (Taishō 8). In 2005 (Heisei 18), the shrine celebrated its 200th anniversary, during which the sacred palanquin (mikoshi) was refurbished. A new shrine office was also constructed, including a "Mikoshi Hall and Festival Museum." == Kotohira Shrine Grand Festival == The annual grand festival is held over three days from August 9 (evening festival) to August 11 (main festival with portable shrine procession). The festival includes taiko drumming, performances with golden staves, floats, and traditional hand dances. Four festival districts, established during the {{ill|Taishō period|ja|大正時代}}, participate: "First Festival District," "Third Festival District," "Eastern Festival District," and "Western Festival District." On the night of August 9, a competition titled "Sound and Strength Performance" features floats and hand dances, while August 10 showcases taiko drumming and golden staves. The festival was designated an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Asset of Hokkaido|ja|無形民俗文化財}} in 2020 (Reiwa 2). == Address == * Kotobira Town 1-4, Nemuro, Hokkaido == External Links == * Kotohira Shrine Information] * [http://www.city.nemuro.hokkaido.jp/section/kanko/event_cale/konpira/konpira.html Nemuro City Event Information: Kotohira Shrine Festival] {{ill-broken-link|date=August It was relocated to its current site in 1881 (Meiji 14). The shrine was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} in 1881 and promoted to {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} in 1919 (Taishō 8). In 2005 (Heisei 18), the shrine celebrated its 200th anniversary, during which the sacred palanquin (mikoshi) was refurbished. A new shrine office was also constructed, including a "Mikoshi Hall and Festival Museum." == Kotohira Shrine Grand Festival == The annual grand festival is held over three days from August 9 (evening festival) to August 11 (main festival with portable shrine procession). The festival includes taiko drumming, performances with golden staves, floats, and traditional hand dances. Four festival districts, established during the {{ill|Taishō period|ja|大正時代}}, participate: "First Festival District," "Third Festival District," "Eastern Festival District," and "Western Festival District." On the night of August 9, a competition titled "Sound and Strength Performance" features floats and hand dances, while August 10 showcases taiko drumming and golden staves. The festival was designated an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Asset of Hokkaido|ja|無形民俗文化財}} in 2020 (Reiwa 2). == Address == * Kotobira Town 1-4, Nemuro, Hokkaido == External Links == * Main Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|43|20|37.64|N|145|35|14.90|E}}}} | location = Kotobira Town 1-4, {{ill|Nemuro|ja|根室市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}} | Position = {{ill-coordinates|43|20|37.64|N|145|35|14.90|E|inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命}} | Classification = Former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} | Established = 1806 (Bunka 3) | MainHall = Irimoya-zukuri (hip-and-gable style) | Alternatename = | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = August 9–11 | Rituals = | Map = {{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=43|lat_min=20|lat_sec=37.6|lon_deg=145|lon_min=35|lon_sec=14.9|label=Kotohira
Shrine|position=left|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|AlternativeMap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社), also known as Konpira Shrine, is located in {{ill|Nemuro|ja|根室市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}. == Deities == * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命}} * The shrine also holds sacred objects from shrines located on the {{ill|Northern Territories|ja|北方地域}} of {{ill|Taraku Island|ja|多楽島}}, {{ill|Shibotsu Island|ja|志発島}}, {{ill|Suisho Island|ja|水晶島}}, {{ill|Shikotan|ja|色丹島}}, and {{ill|Kunashiri|ja|国後島}}, conducting festivals on their respective anniversaries. == History == Kotohira Shrine was established in 1806 (Bunka 3) in {{ill|Nemuro Province|ja|根室国}}, {{ill|Nemuro District|ja|根室郡}} by {{ill|Kahē Takadaya|ja|高田屋嘉兵衛}}. It was relocated to its current site in 1881 (Meiji 14). The shrine was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} in 1881 and promoted to {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} in 1919 (Taishō 8). In 2005 (Heisei 18), the shrine celebrated its 200th anniversary, during which the sacred palanquin (mikoshi) was refurbished. A new shrine office was also constructed, including a "Mikoshi Hall and Festival Museum." == Kotohira Shrine Grand Festival == The annual grand festival is held over three days from August 9 (evening festival) to August 11 (main festival with portable shrine procession). The festival includes taiko drumming, performances with golden staves, floats, and traditional hand dances. Four festival districts, established during the {{ill|Taishō period|ja|大正時代}}, participate: "First Festival District," "Third Festival District," "Eastern Festival District," and "Western Festival District." On the night of August 9, a competition titled "Sound and Strength Performance" features floats and hand dances, while August 10 showcases taiko drumming and golden staves. The festival was designated an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Asset of Hokkaido|ja|無形民俗文化財}} in 2020 (Reiwa 2). == Address == * Kotobira Town 1-4, Nemuro, Hokkaido == External Links == * Kotohira Shrine Information] * [http://www.city.nemuro.hokkaido.jp/section/kanko/event_cale/konpira/konpira.html Nemuro City Event Information: Kotohira Shrine Festival] {{ill-broken-link|date=August 2023}} * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.co.jp/HeartLand/3922/ |title=Nemuro Topics (Kotohira Shrine Festival images and videos) |date=April 27, 1999}} * Main Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|43|20|37.64|N|145|35|14.90|E}}}} | location = Kotobira Town 1-4, {{ill|Nemuro|ja|根室市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}} | Position = {{ill-coordinates|43|20|37.64|N|145|35|14.90|E|inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命}} | Classification = Former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} | Established = 1806 (Bunka 3) | MainHall = Irimoya-zukuri (hip-and-gable style) | Alternatename = | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = August 9–11 | Rituals = | Map = {{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=43|lat_min=20|lat_sec=37.6|lon_deg=145|lon_min=35|lon_sec=14.9|label=Kotohira
Shrine|position=left|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|AlternativeMap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社), also known as Konpira Shrine, is located in {{ill|Nemuro|ja|根室市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}. == Deities == * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命}} * The shrine also holds sacred objects from shrines located on the {{ill|Northern Territories|ja|北方地域}} of {{ill|Taraku Island|ja|多楽島}}, {{ill|Shibotsu Island|ja|志発島}}, {{ill|Suisho Island|ja|水晶島}}, {{ill|Shikotan|ja|色丹島}}, and {{ill|Kunashiri|ja|国後島}}, conducting festivals on their respective anniversaries. == History == Kotohira Shrine was established in 1806 (Bunka 3) in {{ill|Nemuro Province|ja|根室国}}, {{ill|Nemuro District|ja|根室郡}} by {{ill|Kahē Takadaya|ja|高田屋嘉兵衛}}. It was relocated to its current site in 1881 (Meiji 14). The shrine was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} in 1881 and promoted to {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} in 1919 (Taishō 8). In 2005 (Heisei 18), the shrine celebrated its 200th anniversary, during which the sacred palanquin (mikoshi) was refurbished. A new shrine office was also constructed, including a "Mikoshi Hall and Festival Museum." == Kotohira Shrine Grand Festival == The annual grand festival is held over three days from August 9 (evening festival) to August 11 (main festival with portable shrine procession). The festival includes taiko drumming, performances with golden staves, floats, and traditional hand dances. Four festival districts, established during the {{ill|Taishō period|ja|大正時代}}, participate: "First Festival District," "Third Festival District," "Eastern Festival District," and "Western Festival District." On the night of August 9, a competition titled "Sound and Strength Performance" features floats and hand dances, while August 10 showcases taiko drumming and golden staves. The festival was designated an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Asset of Hokkaido|ja|無形民俗文化財}} in 2020 (Reiwa 2). == Address == * Kotobira Town 1-4, Nemuro, Hokkaido == External Links == * [https://www.nemuro-kotohira.com/ Official Website of Kotohira Shrine] * [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/top.html Hokkaido Shrine Association] * [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/16/16001.html Kotohira Shrine Information] * [http://www.city.nemuro.hokkaido.jp/section/kanko/event_cale/konpira/konpira.html Nemuro City Event Information: Kotohira Shrine Festival] {{ill-broken-link|date=August 2023}} * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.co.jp/HeartLand/3922/ |title=Nemuro Topics (Kotohira Shrine Festival images and videos) |date=April 27, 1999}} * Shrine|position=left|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|AlternativeMap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社), also known as Konpira Shrine, is located in {{ill|Nemuro|ja|根室市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}. == Deities == * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命}} * The shrine also holds sacred objects from shrines located on the {{ill|Northern Territories|ja|北方地域}} of {{ill|Taraku Island|ja|多楽島}}, {{ill|Shibotsu Island|ja|志発島}}, {{ill|Suisho Island|ja|水晶島}}, {{ill|Shikotan|ja|色丹島}}, and {{ill|Kunashiri|ja|国後島}}, conducting festivals on their respective anniversaries. == History == Kotohira Shrine was established in 1806 (Bunka 3) in {{ill|Nemuro Province|ja|根室国}}, {{ill|Nemuro District|ja|根室郡}} by {{ill|Kahē Takadaya|ja|高田屋嘉兵衛}}. It was relocated to its current site in 1881 (Meiji 14). The shrine was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} in 1881 and promoted to {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} in 1919 (Taishō 8). In 2005 (Heisei 18), the shrine celebrated its 200th anniversary, during which the sacred palanquin (mikoshi) was refurbished. A new shrine office was also constructed, including a "Mikoshi Hall and Festival Museum." == Kotohira Shrine Grand Festival == The annual grand festival is held over three days from August 9 (evening festival) to August 11 (main festival with portable shrine procession). The festival includes taiko drumming, performances with golden staves, floats, and traditional hand dances. Four festival districts, established during the {{ill|Taishō period|ja|大正時代}}, participate: "First Festival District," "Third Festival District," "Eastern Festival District," and "Western Festival District." On the night of August 9, a competition titled "Sound and Strength Performance" features floats and hand dances, while August 10 showcases taiko drumming and golden staves. The festival was designated an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Asset of Hokkaido|ja|無形民俗文化財}} in 2020 (Reiwa 2). == Address == * Kotobira Town 1-4, Nemuro, Hokkaido == External Links == * On the night of August 9, a competition titled "Sound and Strength Performance" features floats and hand dances, while August 10 showcases taiko drumming and golden staves. [[ja:金刀比羅神社 (根室市)]]

金刀比羅神社_(根室市)&action=edit [[Category:Link complete]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)-qq]] [[Category:Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)-qq]] [[Category:Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)-qq]] [[Category:Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)-qq]] [[Category:Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)-qq]] [[Category:Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)-qq]] [[Category:Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)-qq]] [[Category:Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)-qq]] [[Category:Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)-qq]] [[Category:Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)-qq]] [[Category:Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)-qq]] [[Category:Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)]][[Category:K]][[ja:金刀比羅神社 (根室市)]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kotohira Shrine (Nemuro City).jpg|270px]]
Main Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|43|20|37.64|N|145|35|14.90|E}}}} | location = Kotobira Town 1-4, {{ill|Nemuro|ja|根室市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}} | Position = {{ill-coordinates|43|20|37.64|N|145|35|14.90|E|inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命}} | Classification = Former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} | Established = 1806 (Bunka 3) | MainHall = Irimoya-zukuri (hip-and-gable style) | Alternatename = | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = August 9–11 | Rituals = | Map = {{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=43|lat_min=20|lat_sec=37.6|lon_deg=145|lon_min=35|lon_sec=14.9|label=Kotohira
Shrine|position=left|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|AlternativeMap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社), also known as Konpira Shrine, is located in {{ill|Nemuro|ja|根室市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}. == Deities == * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命}} * The shrine also holds sacred objects from shrines located on the {{ill|Northern Territories|ja|北方地域}} of {{ill|Taraku Island|ja|多楽島}}, {{ill|Shibotsu Island|ja|志発島}}, {{ill|Suisho Island|ja|水晶島}}, {{ill|Shikotan|ja|色丹島}}, and {{ill|Kunashiri|ja|国後島}}, conducting festivals on their respective anniversaries. == History == Kotohira Shrine was established in 1806 (Bunka 3) in {{ill|Nemuro Province|ja|根室国}}, {{ill|Nemuro District|ja|根室郡}} by {{ill|Kahē Takadaya|ja|高田屋嘉兵衛}}. It was relocated to its current site in 1881 (Meiji 14). The shrine was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} in 1881 and promoted to {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} in 1919 (Taishō 8). In 2005 (Heisei 18), the shrine celebrated its 200th anniversary, during which the sacred palanquin (mikoshi) was refurbished. A new shrine office was also constructed, including a "Mikoshi Hall and Festival Museum." == Kotohira Shrine Grand Festival == The annual grand festival is held over three days from August 9 (evening festival) to August 11 (main festival with portable shrine procession). The festival includes taiko drumming, performances with golden staves, floats, and traditional hand dances. Four festival districts, established during the {{ill|Taishō period|ja|大正時代}}, participate: "First Festival District," "Third Festival District," "Eastern Festival District," and "Western Festival District." On the night of August 9, a competition titled "Sound and Strength Performance" features floats and hand dances, while August 10 showcases taiko drumming and golden staves. The festival was designated an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Asset of Hokkaido|ja|無形民俗文化財}} in 2020 (Reiwa 2). == Address == * Kotobira Town 1-4, Nemuro, Hokkaido == External Links == * [https://www.nemuro-kotohira.com/ Official Website of Kotohira Shrine] * [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/top.html Hokkaido Shrine Association] * [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/16/16001.html Kotohira Shrine Information] * [http://www.city.nemuro.hokkaido.jp/section/kanko/event_cale/konpira/konpira.html Nemuro City Event Information: Kotohira Shrine Festival] {{ill-broken-link|date=August 2023}} * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.co.jp/HeartLand/3922/ |title=Nemuro Topics (Kotohira Shrine Festival images and videos) |date=April 27, 1999}} * [http://www.nemuro-kankou.com/events/kotohira.html Nemuro Tourist Association: Kotohira Shrine Grand Festival] {{ill-broken-link|date=August 2023}} * Main Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|43|20|37.64|N|145|35|14.90|E}}}} | location = Kotobira Town 1-4, {{ill|Nemuro|ja|根室市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}} | Position = {{ill-coordinates|43|20|37.64|N|145|35|14.90|E|inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命}} | Classification = Former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} | Established = 1806 (Bunka 3) | MainHall = Irimoya-zukuri (hip-and-gable style) | Alternatename = | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = August 9–11 | Rituals = | Map = {{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=43|lat_min=20|lat_sec=37.6|lon_deg=145|lon_min=35|lon_sec=14.9|label=Kotohira
Shrine|position=left|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|AlternativeMap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社), also known as Konpira Shrine, is located in {{ill|Nemuro|ja|根室市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}. == Deities == * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命}} * The shrine also holds sacred objects from shrines located on the {{ill|Northern Territories|ja|北方地域}} of {{ill|Taraku Island|ja|多楽島}}, {{ill|Shibotsu Island|ja|志発島}}, {{ill|Suisho Island|ja|水晶島}}, {{ill|Shikotan|ja|色丹島}}, and {{ill|Kunashiri|ja|国後島}}, conducting festivals on their respective anniversaries. == History == Kotohira Shrine was established in 1806 (Bunka 3) in {{ill|Nemuro Province|ja|根室国}}, {{ill|Nemuro District|ja|根室郡}} by {{ill|Kahē Takadaya|ja|高田屋嘉兵衛}}. It was relocated to its current site in 1881 (Meiji 14). The shrine was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} in 1881 and promoted to {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} in 1919 (Taishō 8). In 2005 (Heisei 18), the shrine celebrated its 200th anniversary, during which the sacred palanquin (mikoshi) was refurbished. A new shrine office was also constructed, including a "Mikoshi Hall and Festival Museum." == Kotohira Shrine Grand Festival == The annual grand festival is held over three days from August 9 (evening festival) to August 11 (main festival with portable shrine procession). The festival includes taiko drumming, performances with golden staves, floats, and traditional hand dances. Four festival districts, established during the {{ill|Taishō period|ja|大正時代}}, participate: "First Festival District," "Third Festival District," "Eastern Festival District," and "Western Festival District." On the night of August 9, a competition titled "Sound and Strength Performance" features floats and hand dances, while August 10 showcases taiko drumming and golden staves. The festival was designated an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Asset of Hokkaido|ja|無形民俗文化財}} in 2020 (Reiwa 2). == Address == * Kotobira Town 1-4, Nemuro, Hokkaido == External Links == * Kotohira Shrine Information] * [http://www.city.nemuro.hokkaido.jp/section/kanko/event_cale/konpira/konpira.html Nemuro City Event Information: Kotohira Shrine Festival] {{ill-broken-link|date=August In 2005 (Heisei 18), the shrine celebrated its 200th anniversary, during which the sacred palanquin (mikoshi) was refurbished. A new shrine office was also constructed, including a "Mikoshi Hall and Festival Museum." == Kotohira Shrine Grand Festival == The annual grand festival is held over three days from August 9 (evening festival) to August 11 (main festival with portable shrine procession). Main Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|43|20|37.64|N|145|35|14.90|E}}}} | location = Kotobira Town 1-4, {{ill|Nemuro|ja|根室市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}} | Position = {{ill-coordinates|43|20|37.64|N|145|35|14.90|E|inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命}} | Classification = Former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} | Established = 1806 (Bunka 3) | MainHall = Irimoya-zukuri (hip-and-gable style) | Alternatename = | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = August 9–11 | Rituals = | Map = {{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=43|lat_min=20|lat_sec=37.6|lon_deg=145|lon_min=35|lon_sec=14.9|label=Kotohira
Shrine|position=left|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|AlternativeMap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社), also known as Konpira Shrine, is located in {{ill|Nemuro|ja|根室市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}. == Deities == * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命}} * The shrine also holds sacred objects from shrines located on the {{ill|Northern Territories|ja|北方地域}} of {{ill|Taraku Island|ja|多楽島}}, {{ill|Shibotsu Island|ja|志発島}}, {{ill|Suisho Island|ja|水晶島}}, {{ill|Shikotan|ja|色丹島}}, and {{ill|Kunashiri|ja|国後島}}, conducting festivals on their respective anniversaries. == History == Kotohira Shrine was established in 1806 (Bunka 3) in {{ill|Nemuro Province|ja|根室国}}, {{ill|Nemuro District|ja|根室郡}} by {{ill|Kahē Takadaya|ja|高田屋嘉兵衛}}. It was relocated to its current site in 1881 (Meiji 14). The shrine was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} in 1881 and promoted to {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} in 1919 (Taishō 8). In 2005 (Heisei 18), the shrine celebrated its 200th anniversary, during which the sacred palanquin (mikoshi) was refurbished. A new shrine office was also constructed, including a "Mikoshi Hall and Festival Museum." == Kotohira Shrine Grand Festival == The annual grand festival is held over three days from August 9 (evening festival) to August 11 (main festival with portable shrine procession). The festival includes taiko drumming, performances with golden staves, floats, and traditional hand dances. Four festival districts, established during the {{ill|Taishō period|ja|大正時代}}, participate: "First Festival District," "Third Festival District," "Eastern Festival District," and "Western Festival District." On the night of August 9, a competition titled "Sound and Strength Performance" features floats and hand dances, while August 10 showcases taiko drumming and golden staves. The festival was designated an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Asset of Hokkaido|ja|無形民俗文化財}} in 2020 (Reiwa 2). == Address == * Kotobira Town 1-4, Nemuro, Hokkaido == External Links == * Kotohira Shrine Information] * [http://www.city.nemuro.hokkaido.jp/section/kanko/event_cale/konpira/konpira.html Nemuro City Event Information: Kotohira Shrine Festival] {{ill-broken-link|date=August 2023}} * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.co.jp/HeartLand/3922/ |title=Nemuro Topics (Kotohira Shrine Festival images and videos) |date=April 27, 1999}} * 金刀比羅神社_(根室市)&action=edit [[Category:Link complete]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)-qq]] [[Category:Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)-qq]] [[Category:Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)-qq]] [[Category:Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)-qq]] [[Category:Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)-qq]] [[Category:Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)-qq]] [[Category:Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)-qq]] [[Category:Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)-qq]] [[Category:Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)-qq]] [[Category:Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)-qq]] [[Category:Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)-qq]] [[Category:Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)]][[Category:K]][[ja:金刀比羅神社 (根室市)]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kotohira Shrine (Nemuro City).jpg|270px]]
Main Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|43|20|37.64|N|145|35|14.90|E}}}} | location = Kotobira Town 1-4, {{ill|Nemuro|ja|根室市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}} | Position = {{ill-coordinates|43|20|37.64|N|145|35|14.90|E|inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命}} | Classification = Former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} | Established = 1806 (Bunka 3) | MainHall = Irimoya-zukuri (hip-and-gable style) | Alternatename = | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = August 9–11 | Rituals = | Map = {{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=43|lat_min=20|lat_sec=37.6|lon_deg=145|lon_min=35|lon_sec=14.9|label=Kotohira
Shrine|position=left|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|AlternativeMap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社), also known as Konpira Shrine, is located in {{ill|Nemuro|ja|根室市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}. == Deities == * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命}} * The shrine also holds sacred objects from shrines located on the {{ill|Northern Territories|ja|北方地域}} of {{ill|Taraku Island|ja|多楽島}}, {{ill|Shibotsu Island|ja|志発島}}, {{ill|Suisho Island|ja|水晶島}}, {{ill|Shikotan|ja|色丹島}}, and {{ill|Kunashiri|ja|国後島}}, conducting festivals on their respective anniversaries. == History == Kotohira Shrine was established in 1806 (Bunka 3) in {{ill|Nemuro Province|ja|根室国}}, {{ill|Nemuro District|ja|根室郡}} by {{ill|Kahē Takadaya|ja|高田屋嘉兵衛}}. It was relocated to its current site in 1881 (Meiji 14). The shrine was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} in 1881 and promoted to {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} in 1919 (Taishō 8). In 2005 (Heisei 18), the shrine celebrated its 200th anniversary, during which the sacred palanquin (mikoshi) was refurbished. A new shrine office was also constructed, including a "Mikoshi Hall and Festival Museum." == Kotohira Shrine Grand Festival == The annual grand festival is held over three days from August 9 (evening festival) to August 11 (main festival with portable shrine procession). The festival includes taiko drumming, performances with golden staves, floats, and traditional hand dances. Four festival districts, established during the {{ill|Taishō period|ja|大正時代}}, participate: "First Festival District," "Third Festival District," "Eastern Festival District," and "Western Festival District." On the night of August 9, a competition titled "Sound and Strength Performance" features floats and hand dances, while August 10 showcases taiko drumming and golden staves. The festival was designated an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Asset of Hokkaido|ja|無形民俗文化財}} in 2020 (Reiwa 2). == Address == * Kotobira Town 1-4, Nemuro, Hokkaido == External Links == * [https://www.nemuro-kotohira.com/ Official Website of Kotohira Shrine] * [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/top.html Hokkaido Shrine Association] * [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/16/16001.html Kotohira Shrine Information] * [http://www.city.nemuro.hokkaido.jp/section/kanko/event_cale/konpira/konpira.html Nemuro City Event Information: Kotohira Shrine Festival] {{ill-broken-link|date=August 2023}} * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.co.jp/HeartLand/3922/ |title=Nemuro Topics (Kotohira Shrine Festival images and videos) |date=April 27, 1999}} * [http://www.nemuro-kankou.com/events/kotohira.html Nemuro Tourist Association: Kotohira Shrine Grand Festival] {{ill-broken-link|date=August 2023}} * Main Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|43|20|37.64|N|145|35|14.90|E}}}} | location = Kotobira Town 1-4, {{ill|Nemuro|ja|根室市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}} | Position = {{ill-coordinates|43|20|37.64|N|145|35|14.90|E|inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命}} | Classification = Former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} | Established = 1806 (Bunka 3) | MainHall = Irimoya-zukuri (hip-and-gable style) | Alternatename = | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = August 9–11 | Rituals = | Map = {{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=43|lat_min=20|lat_sec=37.6|lon_deg=145|lon_min=35|lon_sec=14.9|label=Kotohira
Shrine|position=left|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|AlternativeMap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社), also known as Konpira Shrine, is located in {{ill|Nemuro|ja|根室市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}. == Deities == * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命}} * The shrine also holds sacred objects from shrines located on the {{ill|Northern Territories|ja|北方地域}} of {{ill|Taraku Island|ja|多楽島}}, {{ill|Shibotsu Island|ja|志発島}}, {{ill|Suisho Island|ja|水晶島}}, {{ill|Shikotan|ja|色丹島}}, and {{ill|Kunashiri|ja|国後島}}, conducting festivals on their respective anniversaries. == History == Kotohira Shrine was established in 1806 (Bunka 3) in {{ill|Nemuro Province|ja|根室国}}, {{ill|Nemuro District|ja|根室郡}} by {{ill|Kahē Takadaya|ja|高田屋嘉兵衛}}. It was relocated to its current site in 1881 (Meiji 14). The shrine was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} in 1881 and promoted to {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} in 1919 (Taishō 8). In 2005 (Heisei 18), the shrine celebrated its 200th anniversary, during which the sacred palanquin (mikoshi) was refurbished. A new shrine office was also constructed, including a "Mikoshi Hall and Festival Museum." == Kotohira Shrine Grand Festival == The annual grand festival is held over three days from August 9 (evening festival) to August 11 (main festival with portable shrine procession). The festival includes taiko drumming, performances with golden staves, floats, and traditional hand dances. Four festival districts, established during the {{ill|Taishō period|ja|大正時代}}, participate: "First Festival District," "Third Festival District," "Eastern Festival District," and "Western Festival District." On the night of August 9, a competition titled "Sound and Strength Performance" features floats and hand dances, while August 10 showcases taiko drumming and golden staves. The festival was designated an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Asset of Hokkaido|ja|無形民俗文化財}} in 2020 (Reiwa 2). == Address == * Kotobira Town 1-4, Nemuro, Hokkaido == External Links == * 金刀比羅神社_(根室市)&action=edit [[Category:Link complete]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)-qq]] [[Category:Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)-qq]] [[Category:Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)-qq]] [[Category:Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)-qq]] [[Category:Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)-qq]] [[Category:Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)-qq]] [[Category:Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)-qq]] [[Category:Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)-qq]] [[Category:Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)-qq]] [[Category:Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)-qq]] [[Category:Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)-qq]] [[Category:Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)]][[Category:K]][[ja:金刀比羅神社 (根室市)]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kotohira Shrine (Nemuro City).jpg|270px]]
Main Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|43|20|37.64|N|145|35|14.90|E}}}} | location = Kotobira Town 1-4, {{ill|Nemuro|ja|根室市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}} | Position = {{ill-coordinates|43|20|37.64|N|145|35|14.90|E|inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命}} | Classification = Former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} | Established = 1806 (Bunka 3) | MainHall = Irimoya-zukuri (hip-and-gable style) | Alternatename = | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = August 9–11 | Rituals = | Map = {{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=43|lat_min=20|lat_sec=37.6|lon_deg=145|lon_min=35|lon_sec=14.9|label=Kotohira
Shrine|position=left|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|AlternativeMap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社), also known as Konpira Shrine, is located in {{ill|Nemuro|ja|根室市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}. == Deities == * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命}} * The shrine also holds sacred objects from shrines located on the {{ill|Northern Territories|ja|北方地域}} of {{ill|Taraku Island|ja|多楽島}}, {{ill|Shibotsu Island|ja|志発島}}, {{ill|Suisho Island|ja|水晶島}}, {{ill|Shikotan|ja|色丹島}}, and {{ill|Kunashiri|ja|国後島}}, conducting festivals on their respective anniversaries. == History == Kotohira Shrine was established in 1806 (Bunka 3) in {{ill|Nemuro Province|ja|根室国}}, {{ill|Nemuro District|ja|根室郡}} by {{ill|Kahē Takadaya|ja|高田屋嘉兵衛}}. It was relocated to its current site in 1881 (Meiji 14). The shrine was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} in 1881 and promoted to {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} in 1919 (Taishō 8). In 2005 (Heisei 18), the shrine celebrated its 200th anniversary, during which the sacred palanquin (mikoshi) was refurbished. A new shrine office was also constructed, including a "Mikoshi Hall and Festival Museum." == Kotohira Shrine Grand Festival == The annual grand festival is held over three days from August 9 (evening festival) to August 11 (main festival with portable shrine procession). The festival includes taiko drumming, performances with golden staves, floats, and traditional hand dances. Four festival districts, established during the {{ill|Taishō period|ja|大正時代}}, participate: "First Festival District," "Third Festival District," "Eastern Festival District," and "Western Festival District." On the night of August 9, a competition titled "Sound and Strength Performance" features floats and hand dances, while August 10 showcases taiko drumming and golden staves. The festival was designated an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Asset of Hokkaido|ja|無形民俗文化財}} in 2020 (Reiwa 2). == Address == * Kotobira Town 1-4, Nemuro, Hokkaido == External Links == * [https://www.nemuro-kotohira.com/ Official Website of Kotohira Shrine] * [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/top.html Hokkaido Shrine Association] * [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/16/16001.html Kotohira Shrine Information] * [http://www.city.nemuro.hokkaido.jp/section/kanko/event_cale/konpira/konpira.html Nemuro City Event Information: Kotohira Shrine Festival] {{ill-broken-link|date=August 2023}} * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.co.jp/HeartLand/3922/ |title=Nemuro Topics (Kotohira Shrine Festival images and videos) |date=April 27, 1999}} * [http://www.nemuro-kankou.com/events/kotohira.html Nemuro Tourist Association: Kotohira Shrine Grand Festival] {{ill-broken-link|date=August 2023}} * 金刀比羅神社_(根室市)&action=edit [[Category:Link complete]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)-qq]] [[Category:Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)-qq]] [[Category:Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)-qq]] [[Category:Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)-qq]] [[Category:Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)-qq]] [[Category:Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)-qq]] [[Category:Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)-qq]] [[Category:Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)-qq]] [[Category:Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)-qq]] [[Category:Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)-qq]] [[Category:Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)-qq]] [[Category:Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)]][[Category:K]][[ja:金刀比羅神社 (根室市)]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kotohira Shrine (Nemuro City).jpg|270px]]
Main Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|43|20|37.64|N|145|35|14.90|E}}}} | location = Kotobira Town 1-4, {{ill|Nemuro|ja|根室市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}} | Position = {{ill-coordinates|43|20|37.64|N|145|35|14.90|E|inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命}} | Classification = Former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} | Established = 1806 (Bunka 3) | MainHall = Irimoya-zukuri (hip-and-gable style) | Alternatename = | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = August 9–11 | Rituals = | Map = {{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=43|lat_min=20|lat_sec=37.6|lon_deg=145|lon_min=35|lon_sec=14.9|label=Kotohira
Shrine|position=left|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|AlternativeMap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社), also known as Konpira Shrine, is located in {{ill|Nemuro|ja|根室市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}. == Deities == * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命}} * The shrine also holds sacred objects from shrines located on the {{ill|Northern Territories|ja|北方地域}} of {{ill|Taraku Island|ja|多楽島}}, {{ill|Shibotsu Island|ja|志発島}}, {{ill|Suisho Island|ja|水晶島}}, {{ill|Shikotan|ja|色丹島}}, and {{ill|Kunashiri|ja|国後島}}, conducting festivals on their respective anniversaries. == History == Kotohira Shrine was established in 1806 (Bunka 3) in {{ill|Nemuro Province|ja|根室国}}, {{ill|Nemuro District|ja|根室郡}} by {{ill|Kahē Takadaya|ja|高田屋嘉兵衛}}. It was relocated to its current site in 1881 (Meiji 14). The shrine was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} in 1881 and promoted to {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} in 1919 (Taishō 8). In 2005 (Heisei 18), the shrine celebrated its 200th anniversary, during which the sacred palanquin (mikoshi) was refurbished. A new shrine office was also constructed, including a "Mikoshi Hall and Festival Museum." == Kotohira Shrine Grand Festival == The annual grand festival is held over three days from August 9 (evening festival) to August 11 (main festival with portable shrine procession). The festival includes taiko drumming, performances with golden staves, floats, and traditional hand dances. Four festival districts, established during the {{ill|Taishō period|ja|大正時代}}, participate: "First Festival District," "Third Festival District," "Eastern Festival District," and "Western Festival District." On the night of August 9, a competition titled "Sound and Strength Performance" features floats and hand dances, while August 10 showcases taiko drumming and golden staves. The festival was designated an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Asset of Hokkaido|ja|無形民俗文化財}} in 2020 (Reiwa 2). == Address == * Kotobira Town 1-4, Nemuro, Hokkaido == External Links == * [https://www.nemuro-kotohira.com/ Official Website of Kotohira Shrine] * [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/top.html Hokkaido Shrine Association] * [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/16/16001.html Kotohira Shrine Information] * [http://www.city.nemuro.hokkaido.jp/section/kanko/event_cale/konpira/konpira.html Nemuro City Event Information: Kotohira Shrine Festival] {{ill-broken-link|date=August 2023}} * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.co.jp/HeartLand/3922/ |title=Nemuro Topics (Kotohira Shrine Festival images and videos) |date=April 27, 1999}} * [http://www.nemuro-kankou.com/events/kotohira.html Nemuro Tourist Association: Kotohira Shrine Grand Festival] {{ill-broken-link|date=August 2023}} * Main Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|43|20|37.64|N|145|35|14.90|E}}}} | location = Kotobira Town 1-4, {{ill|Nemuro|ja|根室市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}} | Position = {{ill-coordinates|43|20|37.64|N|145|35|14.90|E|inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命}} | Classification = Former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} | Established = 1806 (Bunka 3) | MainHall = Irimoya-zukuri (hip-and-gable style) | Alternatename = | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = August 9–11 | Rituals = | Map = {{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=43|lat_min=20|lat_sec=37.6|lon_deg=145|lon_min=35|lon_sec=14.9|label=Kotohira
Shrine|position=left|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|AlternativeMap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社), also known as Konpira Shrine, is located in {{ill|Nemuro|ja|根室市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}. == Deities == * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命}} * The shrine also holds sacred objects from shrines located on the {{ill|Northern Territories|ja|北方地域}} of {{ill|Taraku Island|ja|多楽島}}, {{ill|Shibotsu Island|ja|志発島}}, {{ill|Suisho Island|ja|水晶島}}, {{ill|Shikotan|ja|色丹島}}, and {{ill|Kunashiri|ja|国後島}}, conducting festivals on their respective anniversaries. == History == Kotohira Shrine was established in 1806 (Bunka 3) in {{ill|Nemuro Province|ja|根室国}}, {{ill|Nemuro District|ja|根室郡}} by {{ill|Kahē Takadaya|ja|高田屋嘉兵衛}}. It was relocated to its current site in 1881 (Meiji 14). The shrine was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} in 1881 and promoted to {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} in 1919 (Taishō 8). In 2005 (Heisei 18), the shrine celebrated its 200th anniversary, during which the sacred palanquin (mikoshi) was refurbished. A new shrine office was also constructed, including a "Mikoshi Hall and Festival Museum." == Kotohira Shrine Grand Festival == The annual grand festival is held over three days from August 9 (evening festival) to August 11 (main festival with portable shrine procession). The festival includes taiko drumming, performances with golden staves, floats, and traditional hand dances. Four festival districts, established during the {{ill|Taishō period|ja|大正時代}}, participate: "First Festival District," "Third Festival District," "Eastern Festival District," and "Western Festival District." On the night of August 9, a competition titled "Sound and Strength Performance" features floats and hand dances, while August 10 showcases taiko drumming and golden staves. The festival was designated an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Asset of Hokkaido|ja|無形民俗文化財}} in 2020 (Reiwa 2). == Address == * Kotobira Town 1-4, Nemuro, Hokkaido == External Links == * Main Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|43|20|37.64|N|145|35|14.90|E}}}} | location = Kotobira Town 1-4, {{ill|Nemuro|ja|根室市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}} | Position = {{ill-coordinates|43|20|37.64|N|145|35|14.90|E|inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命}} | Classification = Former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} | Established = 1806 (Bunka 3) | MainHall = Irimoya-zukuri (hip-and-gable style) | Alternatename = | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = August 9–11 | Rituals = | Map = {{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=43|lat_min=20|lat_sec=37.6|lon_deg=145|lon_min=35|lon_sec=14.9|label=Kotohira
Shrine|position=left|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|AlternativeMap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社), also known as Konpira Shrine, is located in {{ill|Nemuro|ja|根室市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}. == Deities == * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命}} * The shrine also holds sacred objects from shrines located on the {{ill|Northern Territories|ja|北方地域}} of {{ill|Taraku Island|ja|多楽島}}, {{ill|Shibotsu Island|ja|志発島}}, {{ill|Suisho Island|ja|水晶島}}, {{ill|Shikotan|ja|色丹島}}, and {{ill|Kunashiri|ja|国後島}}, conducting festivals on their respective anniversaries. == History == Kotohira Shrine was established in 1806 (Bunka 3) in {{ill|Nemuro Province|ja|根室国}}, {{ill|Nemuro District|ja|根室郡}} by {{ill|Kahē Takadaya|ja|高田屋嘉兵衛}}. It was relocated to its current site in 1881 (Meiji 14). The shrine was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} in 1881 and promoted to {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} in 1919 (Taishō 8). In 2005 (Heisei 18), the shrine celebrated its 200th anniversary, during which the sacred palanquin (mikoshi) was refurbished. A new shrine office was also constructed, including a "Mikoshi Hall and Festival Museum." == Kotohira Shrine Grand Festival == The annual grand festival is held over three days from August 9 (evening festival) to August 11 (main festival with portable shrine procession). The festival includes taiko drumming, performances with golden staves, floats, and traditional hand dances. Four festival districts, established during the {{ill|Taishō period|ja|大正時代}}, participate: "First Festival District," "Third Festival District," "Eastern Festival District," and "Western Festival District." On the night of August 9, a competition titled "Sound and Strength Performance" features floats and hand dances, while August 10 showcases taiko drumming and golden staves. The festival was designated an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Asset of Hokkaido|ja|無形民俗文化財}} in 2020 (Reiwa 2). == Address == * Kotobira Town 1-4, Nemuro, Hokkaido == External Links == * [https://www.nemuro-kotohira.com/ Official Website of Kotohira Shrine] * [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/top.html Hokkaido Shrine Association] * [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/16/16001.html Kotohira Shrine Information] * [http://www.city.nemuro.hokkaido.jp/section/kanko/event_cale/konpira/konpira.html Nemuro City Event Information: Kotohira Shrine Festival] {{ill-broken-link|date=August 2023}} * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.co.jp/HeartLand/3922/ |title=Nemuro Topics (Kotohira Shrine Festival images and videos) |date=April 27, 1999}} * [http://www.nemuro-kankou.com/events/kotohira.html Nemuro Tourist Association: Kotohira Shrine Grand Festival] {{ill-broken-link|date=August 2023}} * 金刀比羅神社_(根室市)&action=edit [[Category:Link complete]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)-qq]] [[Category:Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)-qq]] [[Category:Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)-qq]] [[Category:Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)-qq]] [[Category:Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)-qq]] [[Category:Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)-qq]] [[Category:Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)-qq]] [[Category:Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)-qq]] [[Category:Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)-qq]] [[Category:Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)-qq]] [[Category:Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)-qq]] [[Category:Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)]][[Category:K]][[ja:金刀比羅神社 (根室市)]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kotohira Shrine (Nemuro City).jpg|270px]]
Main Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|43|20|37.64|N|145|35|14.90|E}}}} | location = Kotobira Town 1-4, {{ill|Nemuro|ja|根室市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}} | Position = {{ill-coordinates|43|20|37.64|N|145|35|14.90|E|inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命}} | Classification = Former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} | Established = 1806 (Bunka 3) | MainHall = Irimoya-zukuri (hip-and-gable style) | Alternatename = | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = August 9–11 | Rituals = | Map = {{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=43|lat_min=20|lat_sec=37.6|lon_deg=145|lon_min=35|lon_sec=14.9|label=Kotohira
Shrine|position=left|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|AlternativeMap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社), also known as Konpira Shrine, is located in {{ill|Nemuro|ja|根室市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}. == Deities == * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命}} * The shrine also holds sacred objects from shrines located on the {{ill|Northern Territories|ja|北方地域}} of {{ill|Taraku Island|ja|多楽島}}, {{ill|Shibotsu Island|ja|志発島}}, {{ill|Suisho Island|ja|水晶島}}, {{ill|Shikotan|ja|色丹島}}, and {{ill|Kunashiri|ja|国後島}}, conducting festivals on their respective anniversaries. == History == Kotohira Shrine was established in 1806 (Bunka 3) in {{ill|Nemuro Province|ja|根室国}}, {{ill|Nemuro District|ja|根室郡}} by {{ill|Kahē Takadaya|ja|高田屋嘉兵衛}}. It was relocated to its current site in 1881 (Meiji 14). The shrine was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} in 1881 and promoted to {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} in 1919 (Taishō 8). In 2005 (Heisei 18), the shrine celebrated its 200th anniversary, during which the sacred palanquin (mikoshi) was refurbished. A new shrine office was also constructed, including a "Mikoshi Hall and Festival Museum." == Kotohira Shrine Grand Festival == The annual grand festival is held over three days from August 9 (evening festival) to August 11 (main festival with portable shrine procession). The festival includes taiko drumming, performances with golden staves, floats, and traditional hand dances. Four festival districts, established during the {{ill|Taishō period|ja|大正時代}}, participate: "First Festival District," "Third Festival District," "Eastern Festival District," and "Western Festival District." On the night of August 9, a competition titled "Sound and Strength Performance" features floats and hand dances, while August 10 showcases taiko drumming and golden staves. The festival was designated an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Asset of Hokkaido|ja|無形民俗文化財}} in 2020 (Reiwa 2). == Address == * Kotobira Town 1-4, Nemuro, Hokkaido == External Links == * [https://www.nemuro-kotohira.com/ Official Website of Kotohira Shrine] * [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/top.html Hokkaido Shrine Association] * [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/16/16001.html Kotohira Shrine Information] * [http://www.city.nemuro.hokkaido.jp/section/kanko/event_cale/konpira/konpira.html Nemuro City Event Information: Kotohira Shrine Festival] {{ill-broken-link|date=August 2023}} * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.co.jp/HeartLand/3922/ |title=Nemuro Topics (Kotohira Shrine Festival images and videos) |date=April 27, 1999}} * [http://www.nemuro-kankou.com/events/kotohira.html Nemuro Tourist Association: Kotohira Shrine Grand Festival] {{ill-broken-link|date=August 2023}} * Main Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|43|20|37.64|N|145|35|14.90|E}}}} | location = Kotobira Town 1-4, {{ill|Nemuro|ja|根室市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}} | Position = {{ill-coordinates|43|20|37.64|N|145|35|14.90|E|inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命}} | Classification = Former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} | Established = 1806 (Bunka 3) | MainHall = Irimoya-zukuri (hip-and-gable style) | Alternatename = | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = August 9–11 | Rituals = | Map = {{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=43|lat_min=20|lat_sec=37.6|lon_deg=145|lon_min=35|lon_sec=14.9|label=Kotohira
Shrine|position=left|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|AlternativeMap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社), also known as Konpira Shrine, is located in {{ill|Nemuro|ja|根室市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}. == Deities == * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命}} * The shrine also holds sacred objects from shrines located on the {{ill|Northern Territories|ja|北方地域}} of {{ill|Taraku Island|ja|多楽島}}, {{ill|Shibotsu Island|ja|志発島}}, {{ill|Suisho Island|ja|水晶島}}, {{ill|Shikotan|ja|色丹島}}, and {{ill|Kunashiri|ja|国後島}}, conducting festivals on their respective anniversaries. == History == Kotohira Shrine was established in 1806 (Bunka 3) in {{ill|Nemuro Province|ja|根室国}}, {{ill|Nemuro District|ja|根室郡}} by {{ill|Kahē Takadaya|ja|高田屋嘉兵衛}}. It was relocated to its current site in 1881 (Meiji 14). The shrine was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} in 1881 and promoted to {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} in 1919 (Taishō 8). In 2005 (Heisei 18), the shrine celebrated its 200th anniversary, during which the sacred palanquin (mikoshi) was refurbished. A new shrine office was also constructed, including a "Mikoshi Hall and Festival Museum." == Kotohira Shrine Grand Festival == The annual grand festival is held over three days from August 9 (evening festival) to August 11 (main festival with portable shrine procession). The festival includes taiko drumming, performances with golden staves, floats, and traditional hand dances. Four festival districts, established during the {{ill|Taishō period|ja|大正時代}}, participate: "First Festival District," "Third Festival District," "Eastern Festival District," and "Western Festival District." On the night of August 9, a competition titled "Sound and Strength Performance" features floats and hand dances, while August 10 showcases taiko drumming and golden staves. The festival was designated an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Asset of Hokkaido|ja|無形民俗文化財}} in 2020 (Reiwa 2). == Address == * Kotobira Town 1-4, Nemuro, Hokkaido == External Links == * 金刀比羅神社_(根室市)&action=edit [[Category:Link complete]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq deprecated open]][[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq deprecated open]][[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq deprecated open]][[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq deprecated open]][[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq deprecated open]][[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq deprecated open]][[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq deprecated open]][[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq deprecated open]][[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq deprecated open]][[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq deprecated open]][[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq deprecated open]][[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)]][[Category:K]][[ja:金刀比羅神社 (根室市)]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kotohira Shrine (Nemuro City).jpg|270px]]
Main Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|43|20|37.64|N|145|35|14.90|E}}}} | location = Kotobira Town 1-4, {{ill|Nemuro|ja|根室市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}} | Position = {{ill-coordinates|43|20|37.64|N|145|35|14.90|E|inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命}} | Classification = Former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} | Established = 1806 (Bunka 3) | MainHall = Irimoya-zukuri (hip-and-gable style) | Alternatename = | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = August 9–11 | Rituals = | Map = {{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=43|lat_min=20|lat_sec=37.6|lon_deg=145|lon_min=35|lon_sec=14.9|label=Kotohira
Shrine|position=left|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|AlternativeMap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社), also known as Konpira Shrine, is located in {{ill|Nemuro|ja|根室市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}. == Deities == * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命}} * The shrine also holds sacred objects from shrines located on the {{ill|Northern Territories|ja|北方地域}} of {{ill|Taraku Island|ja|多楽島}}, {{ill|Shibotsu Island|ja|志発島}}, {{ill|Suisho Island|ja|水晶島}}, {{ill|Shikotan|ja|色丹島}}, and {{ill|Kunashiri|ja|国後島}}, conducting festivals on their respective anniversaries. == History == Kotohira Shrine was established in 1806 (Bunka 3) in {{ill|Nemuro Province|ja|根室国}}, {{ill|Nemuro District|ja|根室郡}} by {{ill|Kahē Takadaya|ja|高田屋嘉兵衛}}. It was relocated to its current site in 1881 (Meiji 14). The shrine was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} in 1881 and promoted to {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} in 1919 (Taishō 8). In 2005 (Heisei 18), the shrine celebrated its 200th anniversary, during which the sacred palanquin (mikoshi) was refurbished. A new shrine office was also constructed, including a "Mikoshi Hall and Festival Museum." == Kotohira Shrine Grand Festival == The annual grand festival is held over three days from August 9 (evening festival) to August 11 (main festival with portable shrine procession). The festival includes taiko drumming, performances with golden staves, floats, and traditional hand dances. Four festival districts, established during the {{ill|Taishō period|ja|大正時代}}, participate: "First Festival District," "Third Festival District," "Eastern Festival District," and "Western Festival District." On the night of August 9, a competition titled "Sound and Strength Performance" features floats and hand dances, while August 10 showcases taiko drumming and golden staves. The festival was designated an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Asset of Hokkaido|ja|無形民俗文化財}} in 2020 (Reiwa 2). == Address == * Kotobira Town 1-4, Nemuro, Hokkaido == External Links == * [https://www.nemuro-kotohira.com/ Official Website of Kotohira Shrine] * [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/top.html Hokkaido Shrine Association] * [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/16/16001.html Kotohira Shrine Information] * [http://www.city.nemuro.hokkaido.jp/section/kanko/event_cale/konpira/konpira.html Nemuro City Event Information: Kotohira Shrine Festival] {{ill-broken-link|date=August 2023}} * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.co.jp/HeartLand/3922/ |title=Nemuro Topics (Kotohira Shrine Festival images and videos) |date=April 27, 1999}} * (Nemuro)]][[Category:K]][[ja:金刀比羅神社 (根室市)]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kotohira Shrine (Nemuro City).jpg|270px]]
Main Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|43|20|37.64|N|145|35|14.90|E}}}} | location = Kotobira Town 1-4, {{ill|Nemuro|ja|根室市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}} | Position = {{ill-coordinates|43|20|37.64|N|145|35|14.90|E|inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命}} | Classification = Former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} | Established = 1806 (Bunka 3) | MainHall = Irimoya-zukuri (hip-and-gable style) | Alternatename = | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = August 9–11 | Rituals = | Map = {{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=43|lat_min=20|lat_sec=37.6|lon_deg=145|lon_min=35|lon_sec=14.9|label=Kotohira
Shrine|position=left|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|AlternativeMap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社), also known as Konpira Shrine, is located in {{ill|Nemuro|ja|根室市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}. == Deities == * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命}} * The shrine also holds sacred objects from shrines located on the {{ill|Northern Territories|ja|北方地域}} of {{ill|Taraku Island|ja|多楽島}}, {{ill|Shibotsu Island|ja|志発島}}, {{ill|Suisho Island|ja|水晶島}}, {{ill|Shikotan|ja|色丹島}}, and {{ill|Kunashiri|ja|国後島}}, conducting festivals on their respective anniversaries. == History == Kotohira Shrine was established in 1806 (Bunka 3) in {{ill|Nemuro Province|ja|根室国}}, {{ill|Nemuro District|ja|根室郡}} by {{ill|Kahē Takadaya|ja|高田屋嘉兵衛}}. It was relocated to its current site in 1881 (Meiji 14). The shrine was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} in 1881 and promoted to {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} in 1919 (Taishō 8). In 2005 (Heisei 18), the shrine celebrated its 200th anniversary, during which the sacred palanquin (mikoshi) was refurbished. A new shrine office was also constructed, including a "Mikoshi Hall and Festival Museum." == Kotohira Shrine Grand Festival == The annual grand festival is held over three days from August 9 (evening festival) to August 11 (main festival with portable shrine procession). The festival includes taiko drumming, performances with golden staves, floats, and traditional hand dances. Four festival districts, established during the {{ill|Taishō period|ja|大正時代}}, participate: "First Festival District," "Third Festival District," "Eastern Festival District," and "Western Festival District." On the night of August 9, a competition titled "Sound and Strength Performance" features floats and hand dances, while August 10 showcases taiko drumming and golden staves. The festival was designated an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Asset of Hokkaido|ja|無形民俗文化財}} in 2020 (Reiwa 2). == Address == * Kotobira Town 1-4, Nemuro, Hokkaido == External Links == * [https://www.nemuro-kotohira.com/ Official Website of Kotohira Shrine] * [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/top.html Hokkaido Shrine Association] * [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/16/16001.html Kotohira Shrine Information] * [http://www.city.nemuro.hokkaido.jp/section/kanko/event_cale/konpira/konpira.html Nemuro City Event Information: Kotohira Shrine Festival] {{ill-broken-link|date=August 2023}} * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.co.jp/HeartLand/3922/ |title=Nemuro Topics (Kotohira Shrine Festival images and videos) |date=April 27, 1999}} *
