{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kotohira Shrine (Nemuro City).jpg|270px]]
Main Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|43|20|37.64|N|145|35|14.90|E}}}} | location = Kotobira Town 1-4, {{ill|Nemuro|ja|根室市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}} | Position = {{ill-coordinates|43|20|37.64|N|145|35|14.90|E|inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命}} | Classification = Former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} | Established = 1806 (Bunka 3) | MainHall = Irimoya-zukuri (hip-and-gable style) | Alternatename = | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = August 9–11 | Rituals = | Map = {{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=43|lat_min=20|lat_sec=37.6|lon_deg=145|lon_min=35|lon_sec=14.9|label=Kotohira
Shrine|position=left|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|AlternativeMap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社), also known as Konpira Shrine, is located in {{ill|Nemuro|ja|根室市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}. == Deities == * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命}} * The shrine also holds sacred objects from shrines located on the {{ill|Northern Territories|ja|北方地域}} of {{ill|Taraku Island|ja|多楽島}}, {{ill|Shibotsu Island|ja|志発島}}, {{ill|Suisho Island|ja|水晶島}}, {{ill|Shikotan|ja|色丹島}}, and {{ill|Kunashiri|ja|国後島}}, conducting festivals on their respective anniversaries. == History == Kotohira Shrine was established in 1806 (Bunka 3) in {{ill|Nemuro Province|ja|根室国}}, {{ill|Nemuro District|ja|根室郡}} by {{ill|Kahē Takadaya|ja|高田屋嘉兵衛}}. It was relocated to its current site in 1881 (Meiji 14). The shrine was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} in 1881 and promoted to {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} in 1919 (Taishō 8). In 2005 (Heisei 18), the shrine celebrated its 200th anniversary, during which the sacred palanquin (mikoshi) was refurbished. A new shrine office was also constructed, including a "Mikoshi Hall and Festival Museum." == Kotohira Shrine Grand Festival == The annual grand festival is held over three days from August 9 (evening festival) to August 11 (main festival with portable shrine procession). The festival includes taiko drumming, performances with golden staves, floats, and traditional hand dances. Four festival districts, established during the {{ill|Taishō period|ja|大正時代}}, participate: "First Festival District," "Third Festival District," "Eastern Festival District," and "Western Festival District." On the night of August 9, a competition titled "Sound and Strength Performance" features floats and hand dances, while August 10 showcases taiko drumming and golden staves. The festival was designated an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Asset of Hokkaido|ja|無形民俗文化財}} in 2020 (Reiwa 2). == Address == * Kotobira Town 1-4, Nemuro, Hokkaido == External Links == * Kotohira Shrine Information] * [http://www.city.nemuro.hokkaido.jp/section/kanko/event_cale/konpira/konpira.html Nemuro City Event Information: Kotohira Shrine Festival] {{ill-broken-link|date=August 2023}} * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.co.jp/HeartLand/3922/ |title=Nemuro Topics (Kotohira Shrine Festival images and videos) |date=April 27, 1999}} * 6]][[Category: tier 6]][[Category:11]][[sn:Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)]][[Category:K]][[ja:金刀比羅神社 (根室市)]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kotohira Shrine (Nemuro City).jpg|270px]]
Main Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|43|20|37.64|N|145|35|14.90|E}}}} | location = Kotobira Town 1-4, {{ill|Nemuro|ja|根室市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}} | Position = {{ill-coordinates|43|20|37.64|N|145|35|14.90|E|inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命}} | Classification = Former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} | Established = 1806 (Bunka 3) | MainHall = Irimoya-zukuri (hip-and-gable style) | Alternatename = | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = August 9–11 | Rituals = | Map = {{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=43|lat_min=20|lat_sec=37.6|lon_deg=145|lon_min=35|lon_sec=14.9|label=Kotohira
Shrine|position=left|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|AlternativeMap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社), also known as Konpira Shrine, is located in {{ill|Nemuro|ja|根室市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}. == Deities == * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命}} * The shrine also holds sacred objects from shrines located on the {{ill|Northern Territories|ja|北方地域}} of {{ill|Taraku Island|ja|多楽島}}, {{ill|Shibotsu Island|ja|志発島}}, {{ill|Suisho Island|ja|水晶島}}, {{ill|Shikotan|ja|色丹島}}, and {{ill|Kunashiri|ja|国後島}}, conducting festivals on their respective anniversaries. == History == Kotohira Shrine was established in 1806 (Bunka 3) in {{ill|Nemuro Province|ja|根室国}}, {{ill|Nemuro District|ja|根室郡}} by {{ill|Kahē Takadaya|ja|高田屋嘉兵衛}}. It was relocated to its current site in 1881 (Meiji 14). The shrine was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} in 1881 and promoted to {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} in 1919 (Taishō 8). In 2005 (Heisei 18), the shrine celebrated its 200th anniversary, during which the sacred palanquin (mikoshi) was refurbished. A new shrine office was also constructed, including a "Mikoshi Hall and Festival Museum." == Kotohira Shrine Grand Festival == The annual grand festival is held over three days from August 9 (evening festival) to August 11 (main festival with portable shrine procession). The festival includes taiko drumming, performances with golden staves, floats, and traditional hand dances. Four festival districts, established during the {{ill|Taishō period|ja|大正時代}}, participate: "First Festival District," "Third Festival District," "Eastern Festival District," and "Western Festival District." On the night of August 9, a competition titled "Sound and Strength Performance" features floats and hand dances, while August 10 showcases taiko drumming and golden staves. The festival was designated an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Asset of Hokkaido|ja|無形民俗文化財}} in 2020 (Reiwa 2). == Address == * Kotobira Town 1-4, Nemuro, Hokkaido == External Links == * Kotohira Shrine Information] * [http://www.city.nemuro.hokkaido.jp/section/kanko/event_cale/konpira/konpira.html Nemuro City Event Information: Kotohira Shrine Festival] {{ill-broken-link|date=August 2023}} * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.co.jp/HeartLand/3922/ |title=Nemuro Topics (Kotohira Shrine Festival images and videos) |date=April 27, 1999}} * 6]][[Category: tier 6]][[Category:11]][[sn:Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)]][[Category:K]][[ja:金刀比羅神社 (根室市)]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kotohira Shrine (Nemuro City).jpg|270px]]
Main Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|43|20|37.64|N|145|35|14.90|E}}}} | location = Kotobira Town 1-4, {{ill|Nemuro|ja|根室市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}} | Position = {{ill-coordinates|43|20|37.64|N|145|35|14.90|E|inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命}} | Classification = Former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} | Established = 1806 (Bunka 3) | MainHall = Irimoya-zukuri (hip-and-gable style) | Alternatename = | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = August 9–11 | Rituals = | Map = {{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=43|lat_min=20|lat_sec=37.6|lon_deg=145|lon_min=35|lon_sec=14.9|label=Kotohira
Shrine|position=left|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|AlternativeMap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社), also known as Konpira Shrine, is located in {{ill|Nemuro|ja|根室市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}. == Deities == * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命}} * The shrine also holds sacred objects from shrines located on the {{ill|Northern Territories|ja|北方地域}} of {{ill|Taraku Island|ja|多楽島}}, {{ill|Shibotsu Island|ja|志発島}}, {{ill|Suisho Island|ja|水晶島}}, {{ill|Shikotan|ja|色丹島}}, and {{ill|Kunashiri|ja|国後島}}, conducting festivals on their respective anniversaries. == History == Kotohira Shrine was established in 1806 (Bunka 3) in {{ill|Nemuro Province|ja|根室国}}, {{ill|Nemuro District|ja|根室郡}} by {{ill|Kahē Takadaya|ja|高田屋嘉兵衛}}. It was relocated to its current site in 1881 (Meiji 14). The shrine was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} in 1881 and promoted to {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} in 1919 (Taishō 8). In 2005 (Heisei 18), the shrine celebrated its 200th anniversary, during which the sacred palanquin (mikoshi) was refurbished. A new shrine office was also constructed, including a "Mikoshi Hall and Festival Museum." == Kotohira Shrine Grand Festival == The annual grand festival is held over three days from August 9 (evening festival) to August 11 (main festival with portable shrine procession). The festival includes taiko drumming, performances with golden staves, floats, and traditional hand dances. Four festival districts, established during the {{ill|Taishō period|ja|大正時代}}, participate: "First Festival District," "Third Festival District," "Eastern Festival District," and "Western Festival District." On the night of August 9, a competition titled "Sound and Strength Performance" features floats and hand dances, while August 10 showcases taiko drumming and golden staves. The festival was designated an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Asset of Hokkaido|ja|無形民俗文化財}} in 2020 (Reiwa 2). == Address == * Kotobira Town 1-4, Nemuro, Hokkaido == External Links == * Kotohira Shrine Information] * [http://www.city.nemuro.hokkaido.jp/section/kanko/event_cale/konpira/konpira.html Nemuro City Event Information: Kotohira Shrine Festival] {{ill-broken-link|date=August 6]][[Category: tier 6]][[Category:11]][[sn:Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)]][[Category:K]][[ja:金刀比羅神社 (根室市)]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kotohira Shrine (Nemuro City).jpg|270px]]
Main Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|43|20|37.64|N|145|35|14.90|E}}}} | location = Kotobira Town 1-4, {{ill|Nemuro|ja|根室市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}} | Position = {{ill-coordinates|43|20|37.64|N|145|35|14.90|E|inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命}} | Classification = Former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} | Established = 1806 (Bunka 3) | MainHall = Irimoya-zukuri (hip-and-gable style) | Alternatename = | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = August 9–11 | Rituals = | Map = {{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=43|lat_min=20|lat_sec=37.6|lon_deg=145|lon_min=35|lon_sec=14.9|label=Kotohira
Shrine|position=left|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|AlternativeMap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社), also known as Konpira Shrine, is located in {{ill|Nemuro|ja|根室市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}. == Deities == * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命}} * The shrine also holds sacred objects from shrines located on the {{ill|Northern Territories|ja|北方地域}} of {{ill|Taraku Island|ja|多楽島}}, {{ill|Shibotsu Island|ja|志発島}}, {{ill|Suisho Island|ja|水晶島}}, {{ill|Shikotan|ja|色丹島}}, and {{ill|Kunashiri|ja|国後島}}, conducting festivals on their respective anniversaries. == History == Kotohira Shrine was established in 1806 (Bunka 3) in {{ill|Nemuro Province|ja|根室国}}, {{ill|Nemuro District|ja|根室郡}} by {{ill|Kahē Takadaya|ja|高田屋嘉兵衛}}. It was relocated to its current site in 1881 (Meiji 14). The shrine was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} in 1881 and promoted to {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} in 1919 (Taishō 8). In 2005 (Heisei 18), the shrine celebrated its 200th anniversary, during which the sacred palanquin (mikoshi) was refurbished. A new shrine office was also constructed, including a "Mikoshi Hall and Festival Museum." == Kotohira Shrine Grand Festival == The annual grand festival is held over three days from August 9 (evening festival) to August 11 (main festival with portable shrine procession). The festival includes taiko drumming, performances with golden staves, floats, and traditional hand dances. Four festival districts, established during the {{ill|Taishō period|ja|大正時代}}, participate: "First Festival District," "Third Festival District," "Eastern Festival District," and "Western Festival District." On the night of August 9, a competition titled "Sound and Strength Performance" features floats and hand dances, while August 10 showcases taiko drumming and golden staves. The festival was designated an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Asset of Hokkaido|ja|無形民俗文化財}} in 2020 (Reiwa 2). == Address == * Kotobira Town 1-4, Nemuro, Hokkaido == External Links == * [https://www.nemuro-kotohira.com/ Official Website of Kotohira Shrine] * [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/top.html Hokkaido Shrine Association] * [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/16/16001.html Kotohira Shrine Information] * [http://www.city.nemuro.hokkaido.jp/section/kanko/event_cale/konpira/konpira.html Nemuro City Event Information: Kotohira Shrine Festival] {{ill-broken-link|date=August 2023}} * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.co.jp/HeartLand/3922/ |title=Nemuro Topics (Kotohira Shrine Festival images and videos) |date=April 27, 1999}} * [http://www.nemuro-kankou.com/events/kotohira.html Nemuro Tourist Association: Kotohira Shrine Grand Festival] {{ill-broken-link|date=August 2023}} * [http://crabnavi.ninpou.jp/nemuro-kotohira/top.html Nemuro Tourism and Festivals: Kotohira Shrine Festival and Sightseeing] {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]][[Category: tier [[Category:11]] [[Category:Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)]][[Category:K]][[ja:金刀比羅神社 (根室市)]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kotohira Shrine (Nemuro City).jpg|270px]]
Main Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|43|20|37.64|N|145|35|14.90|E}}}} | location = Kotobira Town 1-4, {{ill|Nemuro|ja|根室市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}} | Position = {{ill-coordinates|43|20|37.64|N|145|35|14.90|E|inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命}} | Classification = Former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} | Established = 1806 (Bunka 3) | MainHall = Irimoya-zukuri (hip-and-gable style) | Alternatename = | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = August 9–11 | Rituals = | Map = {{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=43|lat_min=20|lat_sec=37.6|lon_deg=145|lon_min=35|lon_sec=14.9|label=Kotohira
Shrine|position=left|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|AlternativeMap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社), also known as Konpira Shrine, is located in {{ill|Nemuro|ja|根室市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}. == Deities == * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命}} * The shrine also holds sacred objects from shrines located on the {{ill|Northern Territories|ja|北方地域}} of {{ill|Taraku Island|ja|多楽島}}, {{ill|Shibotsu Island|ja|志発島}}, {{ill|Suisho Island|ja|水晶島}}, {{ill|Shikotan|ja|色丹島}}, and {{ill|Kunashiri|ja|国後島}}, conducting festivals on their respective anniversaries. == History == Kotohira Shrine was established in 1806 (Bunka 3) in {{ill|Nemuro Province|ja|根室国}}, {{ill|Nemuro District|ja|根室郡}} by {{ill|Kahē Takadaya|ja|高田屋嘉兵衛}}. It was relocated to its current site in 1881 (Meiji 14). The shrine was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} in 1881 and promoted to {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} in 1919 (Taishō 8). In 2005 (Heisei 18), the shrine celebrated its 200th anniversary, during which the sacred palanquin (mikoshi) was refurbished. A new shrine office was also constructed, including a "Mikoshi Hall and Festival Museum." == Kotohira Shrine Grand Festival == The annual grand festival is held over three days from August 9 (evening festival) to August 11 (main festival with portable shrine procession). The festival includes taiko drumming, performances with golden staves, floats, and traditional hand dances. Four festival districts, established during the {{ill|Taishō period|ja|大正時代}}, participate: "First Festival District," "Third Festival District," "Eastern Festival District," and "Western Festival District." On the night of August 9, a competition titled "Sound and Strength Performance" features floats and hand dances, while August 10 showcases taiko drumming and golden staves. The festival was designated an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Asset of Hokkaido|ja|無形民俗文化財}} in 2020 (Reiwa 2). == Address == * Kotobira Town 1-4, Nemuro, Hokkaido == External Links == * Kotohira Shrine Information] * [http://www.city.nemuro.hokkaido.jp/section/kanko/event_cale/konpira/konpira.html Nemuro City Event Information: Kotohira Shrine Festival] {{ill-broken-link|date=August revision.text:
金刀比羅神社_(根室市)&action=edit {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kotohira Shrine (Nemuro City).jpg|270px]]
Main Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|43|20|37.64|N|145|35|14.90|E}}}} | location = Kotobira Town 1-4, {{ill|Nemuro|ja|根室市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}} | Position = {{ill-coordinates|43|20|37.64|N|145|35|14.90|E|inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命}} | Classification = Former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} | Established = 1806 (Bunka 3) | MainHall = Irimoya-zukuri (hip-and-gable style) | Alternatename = | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = August 9–11 | Rituals = | Map = {{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=43|lat_min=20|lat_sec=37.6|lon_deg=145|lon_min=35|lon_sec=14.9|label=Kotohira
Shrine|position=left|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|AlternativeMap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社), also known as Konpira Shrine, is located in {{ill|Nemuro|ja|根室市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}. == Deities == * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命}} * The shrine also holds sacred objects from shrines located on the {{ill|Northern Territories|ja|北方地域}} of {{ill|Taraku Island|ja|多楽島}}, {{ill|Shibotsu Island|ja|志発島}}, {{ill|Suisho Island|ja|水晶島}}, {{ill|Shikotan|ja|色丹島}}, and {{ill|Kunashiri|ja|国後島}}, conducting festivals on their respective anniversaries. == History == Kotohira Shrine was established in 1806 (Bunka 3) in {{ill|Nemuro Province|ja|根室国}}, {{ill|Nemuro District|ja|根室郡}} by {{ill|Kahē Takadaya|ja|高田屋嘉兵衛}}. It was relocated to its current site in 1881 (Meiji 14). The shrine was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} in 1881 and promoted to {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} in 1919 (Taishō 8). In 2005 (Heisei 18), the shrine celebrated its 200th anniversary, during which the sacred palanquin (mikoshi) was refurbished. A new shrine office was also constructed, including a "Mikoshi Hall and Festival Museum." == Kotohira Shrine Grand Festival == The annual grand festival is held over three days from August 9 (evening festival) to August 11 (main festival with portable shrine procession). The festival includes taiko drumming, performances with golden staves, floats, and traditional hand dances. Four festival districts, established during the {{ill|Taishō period|ja|大正時代}}, participate: "First Festival District," "Third Festival District," "Eastern Festival District," and "Western Festival District." On the night of August 9, a competition titled "Sound and Strength Performance" features floats and hand dances, while August 10 showcases taiko drumming and golden staves. The festival was designated an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Asset of Hokkaido|ja|無形民俗文化財}} in 2020 (Reiwa 2). == Address == * Kotobira Town 1-4, Nemuro, Hokkaido == External Links == * [https://www.nemuro-kotohira.com/ Official Website of Kotohira Shrine] * [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/top.html Hokkaido Shrine Association] * [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/16/16001.html Kotohira Shrine Information] * [http://www.city.nemuro.hokkaido.jp/section/kanko/event_cale/konpira/konpira.html Nemuro City Event Information: Kotohira Shrine Festival] {{ill-broken-link|date=August 2023}} * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.co.jp/HeartLand/3922/ |title=Nemuro Topics (Kotohira Shrine Festival images and videos) |date=April 27, 1999}} * [http://www.nemuro-kankou.com/events/kotohira.html Nemuro Tourist Association: Kotohira Shrine Grand Festival] {{ill-broken-link|date=August 2023}} * [http://crabnavi.ninpou.jp/nemuro-kotohira/top.html Nemuro Tourism and Festivals: Kotohira Shrine Festival and Sightseeing] {{Shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category: tier 6]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)]] [[Category:K]] [[Category:Kotohira shrines]] [[Category:Shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:Nemuro Province|Shrines]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines of Hokkaido]] [[Category:Buildings in Nemuro]] [[Category:History of Nemuro]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)]] [[ja:金刀比羅神社 (根室市)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)}}

金刀比羅神社_(根室市)&action=edit {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kotohira Shrine (Nemuro City).jpg|270px]]
Main Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|43|20|37.64|N|145|35|14.90|E}}}} | location = Kotobira Town 1-4, {{ill|Nemuro|ja|根室市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道|12=draft}} | Position = {{ill-coordinates|43|20|37.64|N|145|35|14.90|E|inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命|12=draft}} | Classification = Former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|12=draft}} | Established = 1806 (Bunka 3) | MainHall = Irimoya-zukuri (hip-and-gable style) | Alternatename = | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = August 9–11 | Rituals = | Map = {{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=43|lat_min=20|lat_sec=37.6|lon_deg=145|lon_min=35|lon_sec=14.9|label=Kotohira
Shrine|position=left|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|AlternativeMap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社), also known as Konpira Shrine, is located in {{ill|Nemuro|ja|根室市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道|12=draft}}. == Deities == * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命|12=draft}} * The shrine also holds sacred objects from shrines located on the {{ill|Northern Territories|ja|北方地域|12=draft}} of {{ill|Taraku Island|ja|多楽島|12=draft}}, {{ill|Shibotsu Island|ja|志発島|12=draft}}, {{ill|Suisho Island|ja|水晶島|12=draft}}, {{ill|Shikotan|ja|色丹島|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Kunashiri|ja|国後島|12=draft}}, conducting festivals on their respective anniversaries. == History == Kotohira Shrine was established in 1806 (Bunka 3) in {{ill|Nemuro Province|ja|根室国|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nemuro District|ja|根室郡|12=draft}} by {{ill|Kahē Takadaya|ja|高田屋嘉兵衛|12=draft}}. It was relocated to its current site in 1881 (Meiji 14). The shrine was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft}} in 1881 and promoted to {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|12=draft}} in 1919 (Taishō 8). In 2005 (Heisei 18), the shrine celebrated its 200th anniversary, during which the sacred palanquin (mikoshi) was refurbished. A new shrine office was also constructed, including a "Mikoshi Hall and Festival Museum." == Kotohira Shrine Grand Festival == The annual grand festival is held over three days from August 9 (evening festival) to August 11 (main festival with portable shrine procession). The festival includes taiko drumming, performances with golden staves, floats, and traditional hand dances. Four festival districts, established during the {{ill|Taishō period|ja|大正時代|12=draft}}, participate: "First Festival District," "Third Festival District," "Eastern Festival District," and "Western Festival District." On the night of August 9, a competition titled "Sound and Strength Performance" features floats and hand dances, while August 10 showcases taiko drumming and golden staves. The festival was designated an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Asset of Hokkaido|ja|無形民俗文化財|12=draft}} in 2020 (Reiwa 2). == Address == * Kotobira Town 1-4, Nemuro, Hokkaido == External Links == * [https://www.nemuro-kotohira.com/ Official Website of Kotohira Shrine] * [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/top.html Hokkaido Shrine Association] * [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/16/16001.html Kotohira Shrine Information] * [http://www.city.nemuro.hokkaido.jp/section/kanko/event_cale/konpira/konpira.html Nemuro City Event Information: Kotohira Shrine Festival] {{ill-broken-link|date=August 2023}} * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.co.jp/HeartLand/3922/ |title=Nemuro Topics (Kotohira Shrine Festival images and videos) |date=April 27, 1999}} * [http://www.nemuro-kankou.com/events/kotohira.html Nemuro Tourist Association: Kotohira Shrine Grand Festival] {{ill-broken-link|date=August 2023}} * [http://crabnavi.ninpou.jp/nemuro-kotohira/top.html Nemuro Tourism and Festivals: Kotohira Shrine Festival and Sightseeing] {{Shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)]] [[Category:K]] [[Category:Kotohira shrines]] [[Category:Shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:Nemuro Province|Shrines]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines of Hokkaido]] [[Category:Buildings in Nemuro]] [[Category:History of Nemuro]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)]] [[ja:金刀比羅神社 (根室市)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)}}

Shrine|position=left|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|AlternativeMap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社), also known as Konpira Shrine, is located in {{ill|Nemuro|ja|根室市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}. == Deities == * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命}} * The shrine also holds sacred objects from shrines located on the {{ill|Northern Territories|ja|北方地域}} of {{ill|Taraku Island|ja|多楽島}}, {{ill|Shibotsu Island|ja|志発島}}, {{ill|Suisho Island|ja|水晶島}}, {{ill|Shikotan|ja|色丹島}}, and {{ill|Kunashiri|ja|国後島}}, conducting festivals on their respective anniversaries. == History == Kotohira Shrine was established in 1806 (Bunka 3) in {{ill|Nemuro Province|ja|根室国}}, {{ill|Nemuro District|ja|根室郡}} by {{ill|Kahē Takadaya|ja|高田屋嘉兵衛}}. It was relocated to its current site in 1881 (Meiji 14). The shrine was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} in 1881 and promoted to {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} in 1919 (Taishō 8). In 2005 (Heisei 18), the shrine celebrated its 200th anniversary, during which the sacred palanquin (mikoshi) was refurbished. A new shrine office was also constructed, including a "Mikoshi Hall and Festival Museum." == Kotohira Shrine Grand Festival == The annual grand festival is held over three days from August 9 (evening festival) to August 11 (main festival with portable shrine procession). The festival includes taiko drumming, performances with golden staves, floats, and traditional hand dances. Four festival districts, established during the {{ill|Taishō period|ja|大正時代}}, participate: "First Festival District," "Third Festival District," "Eastern Festival District," and "Western Festival District." On the night of August 9, a competition titled "Sound and Strength Performance" features floats and hand dances, while August 10 showcases taiko drumming and golden staves. The festival was designated an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Asset of Hokkaido|ja|無形民俗文化財}} in 2020 (Reiwa 2). == Address == * Kotobira Town 1-4, Nemuro, Hokkaido == External Links == * [https://www.nemuro-kotohira.com/ Official Website of Kotohira Shrine] * [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/top.html Hokkaido Shrine Association] * [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/16/16001.html Kotohira Shrine Information] * [http://www.city.nemuro.hokkaido.jp/section/kanko/event_cale/konpira/konpira.html Nemuro City Event Information: Kotohira Shrine Festival] {{ill-broken-link|date=August 2023}} * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.co.jp/HeartLand/3922/ |title=Nemuro Topics (Kotohira Shrine Festival images and videos) |date=April 27, 1999}} * [http://www.nemuro-kankou.com/events/kotohira.html Nemuro Tourist Association: Kotohira Shrine Grand Festival] {{ill-broken-link|date=August 2023}} * [http://crabnavi.ninpou.jp/nemuro-kotohira/top.html Nemuro Tourism and Festivals: Kotohira Shrine Festival and Sightseeing] {{Shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)]] [[Category:K]] [[Category:Kotohira shrines]] [[Category:Shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:Nemuro Province|Shrines]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines of Hokkaido]] [[Category:Buildings in Nemuro]] [[Category:History of Nemuro]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)]] [[ja:金刀比羅神社 (根室市)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)}}

revision.comment: 45 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:金刀比羅神社 (根室市) up to rev 101314516
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 金刀比羅神社 |画像=[[ファイル:Kotohira Shrine (Nemuro City).jpg|270px]]
拝殿 {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|43|20|37.64|N|145|35|14.90|E}}}} |所在地 = [[北海道]][[根室市]]琴平町1-4 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|43|20|37.64|N|145|35|14.90|E|display=title,inline}} |祭神 = [[大物主神]]・[[事代主|事代主神]]・[[ウカノミタマ|倉稲魂命]] |社格 = 旧[[県社]] |創建 = [[1806年]]（文化3年） |本殿 = 入母屋流造 |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 8月9〜11日 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=43|lat_min=20|lat_sec=37.6|lon_deg=145|lon_min=35|lon_sec=14.9|label=金刀比羅
神社|position=left|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|AlternativeMap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} }} '''金刀比羅神社'''（ことひらじんじゃ、こんぴらじんじゃ）は、[[北海道]][[根室市]]にある[[神社]]である。 == 祭神 == *[[大物主神]]・[[事代主|事代主神]]・[[ウカノミタマ|倉稲魂命]] *[[北方地域|北方領土]]の[[多楽島]]・[[志発島]]・[[水晶島]]・[[色丹島]]・[[国後島]]にあった神社の御神体も預かっており，各社の例祭日に祭典が行われる。 == 由緒 == [[1806年]]（[[文化 (元号)|文化]]3年）[[根室国]][[根室郡]]で[[高田屋嘉兵衛]]によって創建され、[[1881年]]（[[明治]]14年）現在地に[[遷座]]した。同年に郷社、[[1919年]]（[[大正]]8年）に[[県社]]に列格している。[[2005年]]（[[平成]]18年）には「金刀比羅神社御創祀200年」を迎え、御神輿が御色直しを行い、さらに社務所を新築し「神輿殿・お祭り資料館」を併設した。 == 金刀比羅神社例大祭 == 毎年8月9日（宵宮祭）、10日（本祭・御神輿渡御）、11日（御神輿渡御）の3日間に渡って、金刀比羅神社例大祭が行なわれる。 先太鼓・金棒・[[山車]]・手古舞の奉納を行なう祭典区は[[大正時代]]に置かれ、「第一祭典区」「第三祭典区」「東部祭典区」「西部祭典区」の4つからなる。9日の夜には「音と力の競演」と題して山車・手古舞の部が、10日には先太鼓・金棒の部が行なわれる。 2020年（令和2年）北海道[[民俗文化財|無形民俗文化財]]に指定された。 == 所在地 == *北海道根室市琴平町1-4 == 外部リンク == ・[https://www.nemuro-kotohira.com/ 金刀比羅神社　公式ウェブサイト] ・[http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/top.html 北海道神社庁] *[http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/16/16001.html 金刀比羅神社] *[http://www.city.nemuro.hokkaido.jp/section/kanko/event_cale/konpira/konpira.html 根室市役所（ねむろのイベント情報：金刀比羅神社例大祭）]{{リンク切れ|date=2023年8月}} *{{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.co.jp/HeartLand/3922/ |title=ねむトピ（金刀比羅神社例大祭の画像、動画が載っているサイト） |date=19990427034754}} *[http://www.nemuro-kankou.com/events/kotohira.html 根室市観光協会:金刀比羅神社例大祭]{{リンク切れ|date=2023年8月}} *[http://crabnavi.ninpou.jp/nemuro-kotohira/top.html ねむろの観光とおまつり（根室金刀比羅神社例大祭と観光名所を紹介したサイト）] {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ことひらしんしや}} [[Category:金刀比羅神社]] [[Category:北海道の神社]] [[Category:根室国|社]] [[Category:北海道の旧県社]] [[Category:根室市の建築物]] [[Category:根室市の歴史]]

拝殿 {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|43|20|37.64|N|145|35|14.90|E}}}} |所在地 = [[北海道]][[根室市]]琴平町1-4 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|43|20|37.64|N|145|35|14.90|E|display=title,inline}} |祭神 = [[大物主神]]・[[事代主|事代主神]]・[[ウカノミタマ|倉稲魂命]] |社格 = 旧[[県社]] |創建 = [[1806年]]（文化3年） |本殿 = 入母屋流造 |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 8月9〜11日 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=43|lat_min=20|lat_sec=37.6|lon_deg=145|lon_min=35|lon_sec=14.9|label=金刀比羅
神社|position=left|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|AlternativeMap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} }} '''金刀比羅神社'''（ことひらじんじゃ、こんぴらじんじゃ）は、[[北海道]][[根室市]]にある[[神社]]である。 == 祭神 == *[[大物主神]]・[[事代主|事代主神]]・[[ウカノミタマ|倉稲魂命]] *[[北方地域|北方領土]]の[[多楽島]]・[[志発島]]・[[水晶島]]・[[色丹島]]・[[国後島]]にあった神社の御神体も預かっており，各社の例祭日に祭典が行われる。 == 由緒 == [[1806年]]（[[文化 (元号)|文化]]3年）[[根室国]][[根室郡]]で[[高田屋嘉兵衛]]によって創建され、[[1881年]]（[[明治]]14年）現在地に[[遷座]]した。同年に郷社、[[1919年]]（[[大正]]8年）に[[県社]]に列格している。[[2005年]]（[[平成]]18年）には「金刀比羅神社御創祀200年」を迎え、御神輿が御色直しを行い、さらに社務所を新築し「神輿殿・お祭り資料館」を併設した。 == 金刀比羅神社例大祭 == 毎年8月9日（宵宮祭）、10日（本祭・御神輿渡御）、11日（御神輿渡御）の3日間に渡って、金刀比羅神社例大祭が行なわれる。 先太鼓・金棒・[[山車]]・手古舞の奉納を行なう祭典区は[[大正時代]]に置かれ、「第一祭典区」「第三祭典区」「東部祭典区」「西部祭典区」の4つからなる。9日の夜には「音と力の競演」と題して山車・手古舞の部が、10日には先太鼓・金棒の部が行なわれる。 2020年（令和2年）北海道[[民俗文化財|無形民俗文化財]]に指定された。 == 所在地 == *北海道根室市琴平町1-4 == 外部リンク == ・[https://www.nemuro-kotohira.com/ 金刀比羅神社　公式ウェブサイト] ・[http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/top.html 北海道神社庁] *[http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/16/16001.html 金刀比羅神社] *[http://www.city.nemuro.hokkaido.jp/section/kanko/event_cale/konpira/konpira.html 根室市役所（ねむろのイベント情報：金刀比羅神社例大祭）]{{リンク切れ|date=2023年8月}} *{{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.co.jp/HeartLand/3922/ |title=ねむトピ（金刀比羅神社例大祭の画像、動画が載っているサイト） |date=19990427034754}} *[http://www.nemuro-kankou.com/events/kotohira.html 根室市観光協会:金刀比羅神社例大祭]{{リンク切れ|date=2023年8月}} *[http://crabnavi.ninpou.jp/nemuro-kotohira/top.html ねむろの観光とおまつり（根室金刀比羅神社例大祭と観光名所を紹介したサイト）] {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ことひらしんしや}} [[Category:金刀比羅神社]] [[Category:北海道の神社]] [[Category:根室国|社]] [[Category:北海道の旧県社]] [[Category:根室市の建築物]] [[Category:根室市の歴史]]

金刀比羅神社_(根室市)&action=edit {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kotohira Shrine (Nemuro City).jpg|270px]]
Main Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|43|20|37.64|N|145|35|14.90|E}}}} | location = Kotobira Town 1-4, {{ill|Nemuro|ja|根室市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}} | Position = {{ill-coordinates|43|20|37.64|N|145|35|14.90|E|inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命}} | Classification = Former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} | Established = 1806 (Bunka 3) | MainHall = Irimoya-zukuri (hip-and-gable style) | Alternatename = | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = August 9–11 | Rituals = | Map = {{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=43|lat_min=20|lat_sec=37.6|lon_deg=145|lon_min=35|lon_sec=14.9|label=Kotohira
Shrine|position=left|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|AlternativeMap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社), also known as Konpira Shrine, is located in {{ill|Nemuro|ja|根室市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}. == Deities == * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命}} * The shrine also holds sacred objects from shrines located on the {{ill|Northern Territories|ja|北方地域}} of {{ill|Taraku Island|ja|多楽島}}, {{ill|Shibotsu Island|ja|志発島}}, {{ill|Suisho Island|ja|水晶島}}, {{ill|Shikotan|ja|色丹島}}, and {{ill|Kunashiri|ja|国後島}}, conducting festivals on their respective anniversaries. == History == Kotohira Shrine was established in 1806 (Bunka 3) in {{ill|Nemuro Province|ja|根室国}}, {{ill|Nemuro District|ja|根室郡}} by {{ill|Kahē Takadaya|ja|高田屋嘉兵衛}}. It was relocated to its current site in 1881 (Meiji 14). The shrine was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} in 1881 and promoted to {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} in 1919 (Taishō 8). In 2005 (Heisei 18), the shrine celebrated its 200th anniversary, during which the sacred palanquin (mikoshi) was refurbished. A new shrine office was also constructed, including a "Mikoshi Hall and Festival Museum." == Kotohira Shrine Grand Festival == The annual grand festival is held over three days from August 9 (evening festival) to August 11 (main festival with portable shrine procession). The festival includes taiko drumming, performances with golden staves, floats, and traditional hand dances. Four festival districts, established during the {{ill|Taishō period|ja|大正時代}}, participate: "First Festival District," "Third Festival District," "Eastern Festival District," and "Western Festival District." On the night of August 9, a competition titled "Sound and Strength Performance" features floats and hand dances, while August 10 showcases taiko drumming and golden staves. The festival was designated an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Asset of Hokkaido|ja|無形民俗文化財}} in 2020 (Reiwa 2). == Address == * Kotobira Town 1-4, Nemuro, Hokkaido == External Links == * [https://www.nemuro-kotohira.com/ Official Website of Kotohira Shrine] * [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/top.html Hokkaido Shrine Association] * [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/16/16001.html Kotohira Shrine Information] * [http://www.city.nemuro.hokkaido.jp/section/kanko/event_cale/konpira/konpira.html Nemuro City Event Information: Kotohira Shrine Festival] {{ill-broken-link|date=August 2023}} * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.co.jp/HeartLand/3922/ |title=Nemuro Topics (Kotohira Shrine Festival images and videos) |date=April 27, 1999}} * [http://www.nemuro-kankou.com/events/kotohira.html Nemuro Tourist Association: Kotohira Shrine Grand Festival] {{ill-broken-link|date=August 2023}} * [http://crabnavi.ninpou.jp/nemuro-kotohira/top.html Nemuro Tourism and Festivals: Kotohira Shrine Festival and Sightseeing] {{Shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)]] [[Category:K]] [[Category:Kotohira shrines]] [[Category:Shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:Nemuro Province|Shrines]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines of Hokkaido]] [[Category:Buildings in Nemuro]] [[Category:History of Nemuro]] }} [[sn:Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)]] [[ja:金刀比羅神社 (根室市)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)}} {{translated page|ja|金刀比羅神社 (根室市)|version=101314516|comment=Imported full ja history}}

The festival was designated an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Asset of Hokkaido|ja|無形民俗文化財|qq=draft}} in 2020 (Reiwa 2). == Address == * Kotobira Town 1-4, Nemuro, Hokkaido == External Links == * [https://www.nemuro-kotohira.com/ Official Website of Kotohira Shrine] * [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/top.html Hokkaido Shrine Association] * [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/16/16001.html Kotohira Shrine Information] * [http://www.city.nemuro.hokkaido.jp/section/kanko/event_cale/konpira/konpira.html Nemuro City Event Information: Kotohira Shrine Festival] {{ill-broken-link|date=August 2023}} * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.co.jp/HeartLand/3922/ |title=Nemuro Topics (Kotohira Shrine Festival images and videos) |date=April 27, 1999}} * [http://www.nemuro-kankou.com/events/kotohira.html Nemuro Tourist Association: Kotohira Shrine Grand Festival] {{ill-broken-link|date=August 2023}} * [http://crabnavi.ninpou.jp/nemuro-kotohira/top.html Nemuro Tourism and Festivals: Kotohira Shrine Festival and Sightseeing] {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|金刀比羅神社 (根室市)|version=101314516|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)]] [[Category:K]] [[Category:Kotohira shrines]] [[Category:Shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:Nemuro Province|Shrines]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines of Hokkaido]] [[Category:Buildings in Nemuro]] [[Category:History of Nemuro]] }} [[sn:Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)]] [[ja:金刀比羅神社 (根室市)]]

金刀比羅神社_(根室市)&action=edit {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kotohira Shrine (Nemuro City).jpg|270px]]
Main Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|43|20|37.64|N|145|35|14.90|E}}}} | location = Kotobira Town 1-4, {{ill|Nemuro|ja|根室市|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道|qq=draft}} | Position = {{ill-coordinates|43|20|37.64|N|145|35|14.90|E|inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命|qq=draft}} | Classification = Former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft}} | Established = 1806 (Bunka 3) | MainHall = Irimoya-zukuri (hip-and-gable style) | Alternatename = | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = August 9–11 | Rituals = | Map = {{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=43|lat_min=20|lat_sec=37.6|lon_deg=145|lon_min=35|lon_sec=14.9|label=Kotohira
Shrine|position=left|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|AlternativeMap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社), also known as Konpira Shrine, is located in {{ill|Nemuro|ja|根室市|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道|qq=draft}}. == Deities == * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命|qq=draft}} * The shrine also holds sacred objects from shrines located on the {{ill|Northern Territories|ja|北方地域|qq=draft}} of {{ill|Taraku Island|ja|多楽島|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Shibotsu Island|ja|志発島|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Suisho Island|ja|水晶島|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Shikotan|ja|色丹島|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Kunashiri|ja|国後島|qq=draft}}, conducting festivals on their respective anniversaries. == History == Kotohira Shrine was established in 1806 (Bunka 3) in {{ill|Nemuro Province|ja|根室国|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Nemuro District|ja|根室郡|qq=draft}} by {{ill|Kahē Takadaya|ja|高田屋嘉兵衛|qq=draft}}. It was relocated to its current site in 1881 (Meiji 14). The shrine was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft}} in 1881 and promoted to {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft}} in 1919 (Taishō 8). In 2005 (Heisei 18), the shrine celebrated its 200th anniversary, during which the sacred palanquin (mikoshi) was refurbished. A new shrine office was also constructed, including a "Mikoshi Hall and Festival Museum." == Kotohira Shrine Grand Festival == The annual grand festival is held over three days from August 9 (evening festival) to August 11 (main festival with portable shrine procession). The festival includes taiko drumming, performances with golden staves, floats, and traditional hand dances. Four festival districts, established during the {{ill|Taishō period|ja|大正時代|qq=draft}}, participate: "First Festival District," "Third Festival District," "Eastern Festival District," and "Western Festival District." On the night of August 9, a competition titled "Sound and Strength Performance" features floats and hand dances, while August 10 showcases taiko drumming and golden staves. The festival was designated an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Asset of Hokkaido|ja|無形民俗文化財|qq=draft}} in 2020 (Reiwa 2). == Address == * Kotobira Town 1-4, Nemuro, Hokkaido == External Links == * [https://www.nemuro-kotohira.com/ Official Website of Kotohira Shrine] * [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/top.html Hokkaido Shrine Association] * [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/16/16001.html Kotohira Shrine Information] * [http://www.city.nemuro.hokkaido.jp/section/kanko/event_cale/konpira/konpira.html Nemuro City Event Information: Kotohira Shrine Festival] {{ill-broken-link|date=August 2023}} * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.co.jp/HeartLand/3922/ |title=Nemuro Topics (Kotohira Shrine Festival images and videos) |date=April 27, 1999}} * [http://www.nemuro-kankou.com/events/kotohira.html Nemuro Tourist Association: Kotohira Shrine Grand Festival] {{ill-broken-link|date=August 2023}} * [http://crabnavi.ninpou.jp/nemuro-kotohira/top.html Nemuro Tourism and Festivals: Kotohira Shrine Festival and Sightseeing] {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|金刀比羅神社 (根室市)|version=101314516|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)]] [[Category:K]] [[Category:Kotohira shrines]] [[Category:Shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:Nemuro Province|Shrines]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:Buildings in Nemuro]] [[Category:History of Nemuro]] }} [[sn:Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)]] [[ja:金刀比羅神社 (根室市)]]

It was relocated to its current site in 1881 (Meiji 14). The shrine was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}} in 1881 and promoted to {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=}} in 1919 (Taishō 8). The festival includes taiko drumming, performances with golden staves, floats, and traditional hand dances. Four festival districts, established during the {{ill|Taishō period|ja|大正時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishō period|qq=}}, participate: "First Festival District," "Third Festival District," "Eastern Festival District," and "Western Festival District." The festival was designated an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Asset of Hokkaido|ja|無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Intangible Folk Cultural Asset of Hokkaido|qq=}} in 2020 (Reiwa 2). == Address == * Kotobira Town 1-4, Nemuro, Hokkaido == External Links == * [https://www.nemuro-kotohira.com/ Official Website of Kotohira Shrine] * [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/top.html Hokkaido Shrine Association] * [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/16/16001.html Kotohira Shrine Information] * [http://www.city.nemuro.hokkaido.jp/section/kanko/event_cale/konpira/konpira.html Nemuro City Event Information: Kotohira Shrine Festival] {{ill-broken-link|date=August 2023}} * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.co.jp/HeartLand/3922/ |title=Nemuro Topics (Kotohira Shrine Festival images and videos) |date=April 27, 1999}} * [http://www.nemuro-kankou.com/events/kotohira.html Nemuro Tourist Association: Kotohira Shrine Grand Festival] {{ill-broken-link|date=August 2023}} * [http://crabnavi.ninpou.jp/nemuro-kotohira/top.html Nemuro Tourism and Festivals: Kotohira Shrine Festival and Sightseeing] {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|金刀比羅神社 (根室市)|version=101314516|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)]] [[Category:K]] [[Category:Kotohira shrines]] [[Category:Shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:Nemuro Province|Shrines]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:Buildings in Nemuro]] [[Category:History of Nemuro]] }} [[sn:Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)]] [[ja:金刀比羅神社 (根室市)]] ==Merged second translation== {{infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|image=Kotohira Shrine (Nemuro City).jpg | name = Kotohira Shrine | image = [[File:Kotohira Shrine (Nemuro City).jpg|270px]]
Worship Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|43|20|37.64|N|145|35|14.90|E}}}} | location = 1-4 Kotohira-chō, Nemuro-shi, Hokkaidō | coordinates = {{Coord|43|20|37.64|N|145|35|14.90|E|display=title,inline}} | deity = [[Ōmononushi no Kami]] • [[Kotoshironushi no Kami]] • [[Ukanomitama no Kami]] | type = Former [[Ken-sha|Prefectural Shrine]] | founded = [[1806]] (Bunka 3) | Main Building Style = Irimoya-zukuri | festival = August 9–11 | map = {{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=43|lat_min=20|lat_sec=37.6|lon_deg=145|lon_min=35|lon_sec=14.9|label=Kotohira
Shrine|position=left|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|Alternativemap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} }} {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Nemuro City)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (根室市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine (Nemuro City)|qq=}} is a {{ill|Kotohira Shrines|lt=Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrines|qq=}} located in {{ill|Japan|simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan|qq=}}{{Cite web|date=2017-08-07 |title=Nemuro Kotohira Shrine |url=https://nemuro-hokkaido.com/facility/nemuro-kotohira-shrine/ |access-date=2023-10-17 |website=NEMURO |language=ja|qq=draft}}. It worships {{ill|Konpira Gongen|simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Gongen|qq=}}. It is one of the easternmost shrines in Japan. {{ill|Kotohira Shrines|ja|金刀比羅神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrines|qq=}} are [[shinto shrine]]s dedicated to [[Ōmononushi]]. Due to [[Shinbutsu-shūgō]] they often also worship [[Konpira Gongen]] They are part of the famous Inahachikonten Shrines (稲八金天神社) of the [[Shrine Consolidation Policy]] alongside [[Inari Shrine]], [[Hachiman Shrine]], {{ill|Kotohira Shrines|ja|金刀比羅神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrines|qq=}}, and [[Tenmangū|Tenmangu Shrine]]. == History == The shrine's roots can be traced back to 1806 when Kahei Takadaya, a pioneer of northern sea fishing, paid homage to it while overseeing the fishing grounds. Initially located elsewhere, the shrine was moved to its present location in 1881.{{Cite web |date=2018-06-15 |title=Kotohira Shrine |url=https://map.uu-hokkaido.jp/e/nemuro-kotohira-shrine/ |access-date=2023-10-17 |website=Sightseeing information in Hokkaido |language=en-US}} == Attractions == Upon visiting the shrine, devotees and tourists alike are encouraged to explore the surrounding buildings and facilities. A notable feature within the shrine complex is the festival Museum (Omatsuri Shiryokan). This museum houses various documents and displays, among which the luxurious portable shrine, or Omikoshi, stands out. Visitors can also find the Goshuin, a letter bearing the shogun’s scarlet seal. ==festival== The festival goes from August 9 to August 11. It is a major festival of Hokkkaido. It has a major [[mikoshi]] that requires 60 people to carry.{{Cite web |last=Calendar |first=Japanese Traditional festival |date=2023-10-17 |title=Konhira Shrine annual Grand festival |url=https://ohmatsuri.com/en/matsuri-list/konhira-shrine-annual-grand-festival |access-date=2023-10-17 |website=ohmatsuri.com |language=en}} ==Overview== '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社, Kotohira Jinja, also Konpira Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Nemuro, Hokkaidō]]. == Deities == *[[Ōmononushi no Kami]] • [[Kotoshironushi no Kami]] • [[Ukanomitama no Kami]] *The shrine also houses the sacred objects of shrines from the [[Northern Territories]], including Taraku Island, Shibotsu Island, Suisho Island, Shikotan Island, and Kunashiri Island, and festivals are held on the respective dates of these shrines. == History == The shrine was founded in [[1806]] (Bunka 3) by [[Takadaya Kahei]] in the Nemuro District of Nemuro Province. In [[1881]] (Meiji 14), it was relocated to its current location. The shrine was designated as a village shrine the same year and was promoted to a prefectural shrine in [[1919]] (Taishō 8). In [[2005]] (Heisei 18), the shrine celebrated its 200th anniversary, during which the mikoshi (portable shrine) was refurbished and a new shrine office was built, including the Mikoshi Hall and festival Museum. == Kotohira Shrine festival == The Kotohira Shrine festival is held over three days from August 9 (Eve festival), August 10 (Main festival, Mikoshi Procession), and August 11 (Mikoshi Procession). The festival districts that offer dedications, including drum and kanabō (iron club) performances, and parade floats, were established during the Taishō era and consist of four districts: "First festival District," "Third festival District," "Eastern festival District," and "Western festival District." On the night of the 9th, a performance titled "Sound and Strength Competition" features the parade floats and tekomai (hand drum dance). On the 10th, performances of drums and kanabō are held. == location == *1-4 Kotohira-chō, Nemuro-shi, Hokkaidō == External Links == *[https://www.nemuro-kotohira.com/ Official Website of Kotohira Shrine] *[http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/top.html Hokkaido Shrine Association] *[http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/16/16001.html Kotohira Shrine] *[http://www.city.nemuro.hokkaido.jp/section/kanko/event_cale/konpira/konpira.html Nemuro City Office (Nemuro Event Information: Kotohira Shrine festival)]{{Dead link|date=August 2023}} *{{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.co.jp/HeartLand/3922/ |title=Nemuro Topics (images and videos of Kotohira Shrine festival) |date=19990427034754}} *[http://www.nemuro-kankou.com/events/kotohira.html Nemuro City Tourist Association: Kotohira Shrine festival]{{Dead link|date=August 2023}} *[http://crabnavi.ninpou.jp/nemuro-kotohira/top.html Nemuro Tourism and festival (Site introducing Kotohira Shrine festival and tourist attractions)] {{shinto-stub}} ==References== {{Reflist}} {{translated page|ja|金刀比羅神社 (根室市)|version=101314516|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kotohira shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:Nemuro Province|Shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Nemuro, Hokkaido]] [[Category:History of Nemuro, Hokkaido]] [[Category:Shinbutsu shūgō]] [[Category:Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Kotohira Shrine (Nemuro City)]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:金刀比羅神社 (根室市)]] [[en:Kotohira Shrine (Nemuro City)]] [[sn:Kotohira Shrine (Nemuro City)]] [[Category:Merged pages]]

Main Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|43|20|37.64|N|145|35|14.90|E}}}} | location = Kotobira Town 1-4, {{ill|Nemuro|ja|根室市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nemuro|qq=}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokkaido|qq=}} | Position = {{ill-coordinates|43|20|37.64|N|145|35|14.90|E|inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=}} | Classification = Former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=}} | Established = 1806 (Bunka 3) | MainHall = Irimoya-zukuri (hip-and-gable style) | Alternatename = | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = August 9–11 | Rituals = | Map = {{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=43|lat_min=20|lat_sec=37.6|lon_deg=145|lon_min=35|lon_sec=14.9|label=Kotohira
Shrine|position=left|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|AlternativeMap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社), also known as Konpira Shrine, is located in {{ill|Nemuro|ja|根室市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nemuro|qq=}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokkaido|qq=}}. == Deities == * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=}} * The shrine also holds sacred objects from shrines located on the {{ill|Northern Territories|ja|北方地域|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Northern Territories|qq=}} of {{ill|Taraku Island|ja|多楽島|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taraku Island|qq=}}, {{ill|Shibotsu Island|ja|志発島|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibotsu Island|qq=}}, {{ill|Suisho Island|ja|水晶島|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suisho Island|qq=}}, {{ill|Shikotan|ja|色丹島|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikotan|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kunashiri|ja|国後島|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunashiri|qq=}}, conducting festivals on their respective anniversaries. == History == Kotohira Shrine was established in 1806 (Bunka 3) in {{ill|Nemuro Province|ja|根室国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nemuro Province|qq=}}, {{ill|Nemuro District|ja|根室郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nemuro District|qq=}} by {{ill|Kahē Takadaya|ja|高田屋嘉兵衛|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kahē Takadaya|qq=}}. It was relocated to its current site in 1881 (Meiji 14). The shrine was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}} in 1881 and promoted to {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=}} in 1919 (Taishō 8). In 2005 (Heisei 18), the shrine celebrated its 200th anniversary, during which the sacred palanquin (mikoshi) was refurbished. A new shrine office was also constructed, including a "Mikoshi Hall and Festival Museum." == Kotohira Shrine Grand Festival == The annual grand festival is held over three days from August 9 (evening festival) to August 11 (main festival with portable shrine procession). The festival includes taiko drumming, performances with golden staves, floats, and traditional hand dances. Four festival districts, established during the {{ill|Taishō period|ja|大正時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishō period|qq=}}, participate: "First Festival District," "Third Festival District," "Eastern Festival District," and "Western Festival District." On the night of August 9, a competition titled "Sound and Strength Performance" features floats and hand dances, while August 10 showcases taiko drumming and golden staves. The festival was designated an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Asset of Hokkaido|ja|無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Intangible Folk Cultural Asset of Hokkaido|qq=}} in 2020 (Reiwa 2). == Address == * Kotobira Town 1-4, Nemuro, Hokkaido == External Links == * [https://www.nemuro-kotohira.com/ Official Website of Kotohira Shrine] * [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/top.html Hokkaido Shrine Association] * [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/16/16001.html Kotohira Shrine Information] * [http://www.city.nemuro.hokkaido.jp/section/kanko/event_cale/konpira/konpira.html Nemuro City Event Information: Kotohira Shrine Festival] {{ill-broken-link|date=August 2023}} * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.co.jp/HeartLand/3922/ |title=Nemuro Topics (Kotohira Shrine Festival images and videos) |date=April 27, 1999}} * [http://www.nemuro-kankou.com/events/kotohira.html Nemuro Tourist Association: Kotohira Shrine Grand Festival] {{ill-broken-link|date=August 2023}} * [http://crabnavi.ninpou.jp/nemuro-kotohira/top.html Nemuro Tourism and Festivals: Kotohira Shrine Festival and Sightseeing] {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|金刀比羅神社 (根室市)|version=101314516|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)]] [[Category:K]] [[Category:Kotohira shrines]] [[Category:Shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:Nemuro Province|Shrines]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:Buildings in Nemuro]] [[Category:History of Nemuro]] }} [[sn:Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)]] [[ja:金刀比羅神社 (根室市)]] ==Merged second translation== {{infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|image=Kotohira Shrine (Nemuro City).jpg | name = Kotohira Shrine | image = [[File:Kotohira Shrine (Nemuro City).jpg|270px]]
Worship Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|43|20|37.64|N|145|35|14.90|E}}}} | location = 1-4 Kotohira-chō, Nemuro-shi, Hokkaidō | coordinates = {{Coord|43|20|37.64|N|145|35|14.90|E|display=title,inline}} | deity = [[Ōmononushi no Kami]] • [[Kotoshironushi no Kami]] • [[Ukanomitama no Kami]] | type = Former [[Ken-sha|Prefectural Shrine]] | founded = [[1806]] (Bunka 3) | Main Building Style = Irimoya-zukuri | festival = August 9–11 | map = {{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=43|lat_min=20|lat_sec=37.6|lon_deg=145|lon_min=35|lon_sec=14.9|label=Kotohira
Shrine|position=left|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|Alternativemap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} }} {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Nemuro City)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (根室市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine (Nemuro City)|qq=}} is a {{ill|Kotohira Shrines|lt=Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrines|qq=}} located in {{ill|Japan|simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan|qq=}}{{Cite web|date=2017-08-07 |title=Nemuro Kotohira Shrine |url=https://nemuro-hokkaido.com/facility/nemuro-kotohira-shrine/ |access-date=2023-10-17 |website=NEMURO |language=ja|qq=draft}}. It worships {{ill|Konpira Gongen|simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Gongen|qq=}}. It is one of the easternmost shrines in Japan. {{ill|Kotohira Shrines|ja|金刀比羅神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrines|qq=}} are [[shinto shrine]]s dedicated to [[Ōmononushi]]. Due to [[Shinbutsu-shūgō]] they often also worship [[Konpira Gongen]] They are part of the famous Inahachikonten Shrines (稲八金天神社) of the [[Shrine Consolidation Policy]] alongside [[Inari Shrine]], [[Hachiman Shrine]], {{ill|Kotohira Shrines|ja|金刀比羅神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrines|qq=}}, and [[Tenmangū|Tenmangu Shrine]]. == History == The shrine's roots can be traced back to 1806 when Kahei Takadaya, a pioneer of northern sea fishing, paid homage to it while overseeing the fishing grounds. Initially located elsewhere, the shrine was moved to its present location in 1881.{{Cite web |date=2018-06-15 |title=Kotohira Shrine |url=https://map.uu-hokkaido.jp/e/nemuro-kotohira-shrine/ |access-date=2023-10-17 |website=Sightseeing information in Hokkaido |language=en-US}} == Attractions == Upon visiting the shrine, devotees and tourists alike are encouraged to explore the surrounding buildings and facilities. A notable feature within the shrine complex is the festival Museum (Omatsuri Shiryokan). This museum houses various documents and displays, among which the luxurious portable shrine, or Omikoshi, stands out. Visitors can also find the Goshuin, a letter bearing the shogun’s scarlet seal. ==festival== The festival goes from August 9 to August 11. It is a major festival of Hokkkaido. It has a major [[mikoshi]] that requires 60 people to carry.{{Cite web |last=Calendar |first=Japanese Traditional festival |date=2023-10-17 |title=Konhira Shrine annual Grand festival |url=https://ohmatsuri.com/en/matsuri-list/konhira-shrine-annual-grand-festival |access-date=2023-10-17 |website=ohmatsuri.com |language=en}} ==Overview== '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社, Kotohira Jinja, also Konpira Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Nemuro, Hokkaidō]]. == Deities == *[[Ōmononushi no Kami]] • [[Kotoshironushi no Kami]] • [[Ukanomitama no Kami]] *The shrine also houses the sacred objects of shrines from the [[Northern Territories]], including Taraku Island, Shibotsu Island, Suisho Island, Shikotan Island, and Kunashiri Island, and festivals are held on the respective dates of these shrines. == History == The shrine was founded in [[1806]] (Bunka 3) by [[Takadaya Kahei]] in the Nemuro District of Nemuro Province. In [[1881]] (Meiji 14), it was relocated to its current location. The shrine was designated as a village shrine the same year and was promoted to a prefectural shrine in [[1919]] (Taishō 8). In [[2005]] (Heisei 18), the shrine celebrated its 200th anniversary, during which the mikoshi (portable shrine) was refurbished and a new shrine office was built, including the Mikoshi Hall and festival Museum. == Kotohira Shrine festival == The Kotohira Shrine festival is held over three days from August 9 (Eve festival), August 10 (Main festival, Mikoshi Procession), and August 11 (Mikoshi Procession). The festival districts that offer dedications, including drum and kanabō (iron club) performances, and parade floats, were established during the Taishō era and consist of four districts: "First festival District," "Third festival District," "Eastern festival District," and "Western festival District." On the night of the 9th, a performance titled "Sound and Strength Competition" features the parade floats and tekomai (hand drum dance). On the 10th, performances of drums and kanabō are held. == location == *1-4 Kotohira-chō, Nemuro-shi, Hokkaidō == External Links == *[https://www.nemuro-kotohira.com/ Official Website of Kotohira Shrine] *[http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/top.html Hokkaido Shrine Association] *[http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/16/16001.html Kotohira Shrine] *[http://www.city.nemuro.hokkaido.jp/section/kanko/event_cale/konpira/konpira.html Nemuro City Office (Nemuro Event Information: Kotohira Shrine festival)]{{Dead link|date=August 2023}} *{{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.co.jp/HeartLand/3922/ |title=Nemuro Topics (images and videos of Kotohira Shrine festival) |date=19990427034754}} *[http://www.nemuro-kankou.com/events/kotohira.html Nemuro City Tourist Association: Kotohira Shrine festival]{{Dead link|date=August 2023}} *[http://crabnavi.ninpou.jp/nemuro-kotohira/top.html Nemuro Tourism and festival (Site introducing Kotohira Shrine festival and tourist attractions)] {{shinto-stub}} ==References== {{Reflist}} {{translated page|ja|金刀比羅神社 (根室市)|version=101314516|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kotohira shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:Nemuro Province|Shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Nemuro, Hokkaido]] [[Category:History of Nemuro, Hokkaido]] [[Category:Shinbutsu shūgō]] [[Category:Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Kotohira Shrine (Nemuro City)]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:金刀比羅神社 (根室市)]] [[en:Kotohira Shrine (Nemuro City)]] [[sn:Kotohira Shrine (Nemuro City)]] [[Category:Merged pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)]][[Category:qq]]
