It worships {{ill|Konpira Gongen|simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Gongen|qq=}}. It is one of the easternmost shrines in Japan. {{ill|Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira Shrines|ja|金刀比羅神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrines|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} are {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}}s dedicated to [[Ōmononushi]]. Due to [[Shinbutsu-shūgō]] they often also worship [[Konpira Gongen]] They are part of the famous Inahachikonten Shrines (稲八金天神社) of the [[Shrine Consolidation Policy]] alongside {{ill|Inari shrine|en|Inari shrine|WD=Q514480|lt=Inari shrine}}, [[Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]], {{ill|Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira Shrines|ja|金刀比羅神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrines|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, and [[Tenmangū|Tenmangu Shrine]]. == History == The shrine's roots can be traced back to 1806 when Kahei Takadaya, a pioneer of northern sea fishing, paid homage to it while overseeing the fishing grounds. Initially located elsewhere, the shrine was moved to its present location in 1881.{{Cite web |date=2018-06-15 |title=Kotohira Shrine |url=https://map.uu-hokkaido.jp/e/nemuro-kotohira-shrine/ |access-date=2023-10-17 |website=Sightseeing information in Hokkaido |language=en-US}} == Attractions == Upon visiting the shrine, devotees and tourists alike are encouraged to explore the surrounding buildings and facilities. A notable feature within the shrine complex is the festival Museum (Omatsuri Shiryokan). This museum houses various documents and displays, among which the luxurious portable shrine, or Omikoshi, stands out. Visitors can also find the Goshuin, a letter bearing the shogun’s scarlet seal. ==festival== The festival goes from August 9 to August 11. It is a major festival of Hokkkaido. It has a major [[mikoshi]] that requires 60 people to carry.{{Cite web |last=Calendar |first=Japanese Traditional festival |date=2023-10-17 |title=Konhira Shrine annual Grand festival |url=https://ohmatsuri.com/en/matsuri-list/konhira-shrine-annual-grand-festival |access-date=2023-10-17 |website=ohmatsuri.com |language=en}} ==Overview== '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社, Kotohira Jinja, also Konpira Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in [[:en:Nemuro, Hokkaidō|Nemuro, Hokkaidō]]. == Deities == *[[Ōmononushi no Kami]] • [[Kotoshironushi no Kami]] • [[Ukanomitama no Kami]] *The shrine also houses the sacred objects of shrines from the {{ill|Northern Territories|en|Northern Territories|WD=Q1217330|lt=Northern Territories}}, including Taraku Island, Shibotsu Island, Suisho Island, Shikotan Island, and Kunashiri Island, and festivals are held on the respective dates of these shrines. == History == The shrine was founded in {{ill|1806|en|1806|WD=Q6903|lt=1806}} (Bunka 3) by {{ill|Takadaya Kahei|en|Takadaya Kahei|WD=Q4450102|lt=Takadaya Kahei}} in the Nemuro District of Nemuro Province. In {{ill|1881|en|1881|WD=Q7808|lt=1881}} (Meiji 14), it was relocated to its current location. The shrine was designated as a village shrine the same year and was promoted to a prefectural shrine in {{ill|1919|en|1919|WD=Q2157|lt=1919}} (Taishō 8). In {{ill|2005|en|2005|WD=Q2019|lt=2005}} (Heisei 18), the shrine celebrated its 200th anniversary, during which the mikoshi (portable shrine) was refurbished and a new shrine office was built, including the Mikoshi Hall and festival Museum. == Kotohira Shrine festival == The Kotohira Shrine festival is held over three days from August 9 (Eve festival), August 10 (Main festival, Mikoshi Procession), and August 11 (Mikoshi Procession). The festival districts that offer dedications, including drum and kanabō (iron club) performances, and parade floats, were established during the Taishō era and consist of four districts: "First festival District," "Third festival District," "Eastern festival District," and "Western festival District." On the night of the 9th, a performance titled "Sound and Strength Competition" features the parade floats and tekomai (hand drum dance). On the 10th, performances of drums and kanabō are held. == location == *1-4 Kotohira-chō, Nemuro-shi, Hokkaidō == External Links == *[https://www.nemuro-kotohira.com/ Official Website of Kotohira Shrine] *[http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/top.html Hokkaido Shrine Association] *[http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/16/16001.html Kotohira Shrine] *[http://www.city.nemuro.hokkaido.jp/section/kanko/event_cale/konpira/konpira.html Nemuro City Office (Nemuro Event Information: Kotohira Shrine festival)]{{Dead link|date=August 2023}} *{{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.co.jp/HeartLand/3922/ |title=Nemuro Topics (images and videos of Kotohira Shrine festival) |date=19990427034754}} *[http://www.nemuro-kankou.com/events/kotohira.html Nemuro City Tourist Association: Kotohira Shrine festival]{{Dead link|date=August 2023}} *[http://crabnavi.ninpou.jp/nemuro-kotohira/top.html Nemuro Tourism and festival (Site introducing Kotohira Shrine festival and tourist attractions)] {{shinto-stub}} ==References== {{Reflist}} {{translated page|ja|金刀比羅神社 (根室市)|version=101314516|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:金刀比羅神社 (根室市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11646492}} [[Category:Buildings in Nemuro]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:History of Nemuro]] [[Category:K]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Kotohira shrines]] [[Category:Nemuro Province]] [[Category:Nemuro Province|Shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha merges]] [[Category:Shinbutsu shūgō]] [[Category:Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages]]

[[ja:金刀比羅神社 (根室市)]] [[ja:金刀比羅神社 (根室市)]] [[ja:金刀比羅神社 (根室市)]] 金刀比羅神社_(根室市)&action=edit {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Kotohira Shrine (Nemuro City).jpg | location = Kotobira Town 1-4, {{ill|Nemuro, Hokkaido|en|Nemuro, Hokkaido|WD=Q502205|lt=Nemuro}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|en|Hokkaido|WD=Q35581|lt=Hokkaido}} | Position = {{ill-coordinates|43|20|37.64|N|145|35|14.90|E|inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Ōmononushi|WD=Q10936701}} | Classification = Former {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | Established = 1806 (Bunka 3) | MainHall = Irimoya-zukuri (hip-and-gable style) | Alternatename = | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = August 9–11 | Rituals = | Map = {{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=43|lat_min=20|lat_sec=37.6|lon_deg=145|lon_min=35|lon_sec=14.9|label=Kotohira
Shrine|position=left|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|AlternativeMap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} | location = {{ill|Nemuro|WD=Q502205}} | coordinates = {{Coord|43.343789|N|145.587472|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社), also known as Konpira Shrine, is located in {{ill|Nemuro, Hokkaido|en|Nemuro, Hokkaido|WD=Q502205|lt=Nemuro}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|en|Hokkaido|WD=Q35581|lt=Hokkaido}}. == Deities == * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}} * The shrine also holds sacred objects from shrines located on the {{ill|Northern Territories|ja|北方地域|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Northern Territories|1=South Kuril Islands|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11401819}} of {{ill|Polonsky Island|en|Polonsky Island|WD=Q904875|lt=Taraku Island}}, {{ill|Zelyony Island (Kuril Islands)|en|Zelyony Island (Kuril Islands)|WD=Q904898|lt=Shibotsu Island}}, [[:en:Tanfiliev Island|Suisho Island]], {{ill|Shikotan|en|Shikotan|WD=Q1043128|lt=Shikotan}}, and {{ill|Kunashir|en|Kunashir|WD=Q847285|lt=Kunashiri}}, conducting festivals on their respective anniversaries. == History == Kotohira Shrine was established in 1806 (Bunka 3) in {{ill|Nemuro Province|en|Nemuro Province|WD=Q1063001|lt=Nemuro Province}}, {{ill|Nemuro District|ja|根室郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nemuro District|1=Nemuro district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11110970}} by {{ill|Takadaya Kahei|en|Takadaya Kahei|WD=Q4450102|lt=Kahē Takadaya}}. It was relocated to its current site in 1881 (Meiji 14). The shrine was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in 1881 and promoted to {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in 1919 (Taishō 8). In 2005 (Heisei 18), the shrine celebrated its 200th anniversary, during which the sacred palanquin (mikoshi) was refurbished. A new shrine office was also constructed, including a "Mikoshi Hall and Festival Museum." == Kotohira Shrine Grand Festival == The annual grand festival is held over three days from August 9 (evening festival) to August 11 (main festival with portable shrine procession). The festival includes taiko drumming, performances with golden staves, floats, and traditional hand dances. Four festival districts, established during the {{ill|Taishō period|ja|大正時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishō period|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大正|1=Taishō period|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q329174}}, participate: "First Festival District," "Third Festival District," "Eastern Festival District," and "Western Festival District." On the night of August 9, a competition titled "Sound and Strength Performance" features floats and hand dances, while August 10 showcases taiko drumming and golden staves. The festival was designated an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Asset of Hokkaido|ja|無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Intangible Folk Cultural Asset of Hokkaido|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 民俗文化財|1=Folk Cultural Property of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24895900}} in 2020 (Reiwa 2). == Address == * Kotobira Town 1-4, Nemuro, Hokkaido == External Links == * [https://www.nemuro-kotohira.com/ Official Website of Kotohira Shrine] * [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/top.html Hokkaido Shrine Association] * [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/16/16001.html Kotohira Shrine Information] * [http://www.city.nemuro.hokkaido.jp/section/kanko/event_cale/konpira/konpira.html Nemuro City Event Information: Kotohira Shrine Festival] {{ill-broken-link|date=August 2023}} * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.co.jp/HeartLand/3922/ |title=Nemuro Topics (Kotohira Shrine Festival images and videos) |date=April 27, 1999}} * [http://www.nemuro-kankou.com/events/kotohira.html Nemuro Tourist Association: Kotohira Shrine Grand Festival] {{ill-broken-link|date=August 2023}} * [http://crabnavi.ninpou.jp/nemuro-kotohira/top.html Nemuro Tourism and Festivals: Kotohira Shrine Festival and Sightseeing] {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|金刀比羅神社 (根室市)|version=101314516|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:金刀比羅神社 (根室市)]] ==Merged second translation== {{infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|image=Kotohira Shrine (Nemuro City).jpg | name = Kotohira Shrine | image = [[File:Kotohira Shrine (Nemuro City).jpg|270px]]

Shrine|position=left|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|Alternativemap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} }} {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (根室市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine (Nemuro City)|1=Nemuro Kotohira Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata}} is a {{ill|Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira Shrines|lt=Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrines|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in {{ill|Japan|simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan|qq=}}{{Cite web|date=2017-08-07 |title=Nemuro Kotohira Shrine |url=https://nemuro-hokkaido.com/facility/nemuro-kotohira-shrine/ |access-date=2023-10-17 |website=NEMURO |language=ja|qq=draft}}. It worships {{ill|Konpira Gongen|simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Gongen|qq=}}. It is one of the easternmost shrines in Japan. {{ill|Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira Shrines|ja|金刀比羅神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrines|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} are {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}}s dedicated to [[Ōmononushi]]. Due to [[Shinbutsu-shūgō]] they often also worship [[Konpira Gongen]] They are part of the famous Inahachikonten Shrines (稲八金天神社) of the [[Shrine Consolidation Policy]] alongside {{ill|Inari shrine|en|Inari shrine|WD=Q514480|lt=Inari shrine}}, [[Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]], {{ill|Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira Shrines|ja|金刀比羅神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrines|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, and [[Tenmangū|Tenmangu Shrine]]. == History == The shrine's roots can be traced back to 1806 when Kahei Takadaya, a pioneer of northern sea fishing, paid homage to it while overseeing the fishing grounds. Initially located elsewhere, the shrine was moved to its present location in 1881.{{Cite web |date=2018-06-15 |title=Kotohira Shrine |url=https://map.uu-hokkaido.jp/e/nemuro-kotohira-shrine/ |access-date=2023-10-17 |website=Sightseeing information in Hokkaido |language=en-US}} == Attractions == Upon visiting the shrine, devotees and tourists alike are encouraged to explore the surrounding buildings and facilities. A notable feature within the shrine complex is the festival Museum (Omatsuri Shiryokan). This museum houses various documents and displays, among which the luxurious portable shrine, or Omikoshi, stands out. Visitors can also find the Goshuin, a letter bearing the shogun’s scarlet seal. ==festival== The festival goes from August 9 to August 11. It is a major festival of Hokkkaido. It has a major [[mikoshi]] that requires 60 people to carry.{{Cite web |last=Calendar |first=Japanese Traditional festival |date=2023-10-17 |title=Konhira Shrine annual Grand festival |url=https://ohmatsuri.com/en/matsuri-list/konhira-shrine-annual-grand-festival |access-date=2023-10-17 |website=ohmatsuri.com |language=en}} ==Overview== '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社, Kotohira Jinja, also Konpira Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in [[:en:Nemuro, Hokkaidō|Nemuro, Hokkaidō]]. == Deities == *[[Ōmononushi no Kami]] • [[Kotoshironushi no Kami]] • [[Ukanomitama no Kami]] *The shrine also houses the sacred objects of shrines from the {{ill|Northern Territories|en|Northern Territories|WD=Q1217330|lt=Northern Territories}}, including Taraku Island, Shibotsu Island, Suisho Island, Shikotan Island, and Kunashiri Island, and festivals are held on the respective dates of these shrines. == History == The shrine was founded in {{ill|1806|en|1806|WD=Q6903|lt=1806}} (Bunka 3) by {{ill|Takadaya Kahei|en|Takadaya Kahei|WD=Q4450102|lt=Takadaya Kahei}} in the Nemuro District of Nemuro Province. In {{ill|1881|en|1881|WD=Q7808|lt=1881}} (Meiji 14), it was relocated to its current location. The shrine was designated as a village shrine the same year and was promoted to a prefectural shrine in {{ill|1919|en|1919|WD=Q2157|lt=1919}} (Taishō 8). In {{ill|2005|en|2005|WD=Q2019|lt=2005}} (Heisei 18), the shrine celebrated its 200th anniversary, during which the mikoshi (portable shrine) was refurbished and a new shrine office was built, including the Mikoshi Hall and festival Museum. == Kotohira Shrine festival == The Kotohira Shrine festival is held over three days from August 9 (Eve festival), August 10 (Main festival, Mikoshi Procession), and August 11 (Mikoshi Procession). The festival districts that offer dedications, including drum and kanabō (iron club) performances, and parade floats, were established during the Taishō era and consist of four districts: "First festival District," "Third festival District," "Eastern festival District," and "Western festival District." On the night of the 9th, a performance titled "Sound and Strength Competition" features the parade floats and tekomai (hand drum dance). On the 10th, performances of drums and kanabō are held. == location == *1-4 Kotohira-chō, Nemuro-shi, Hokkaidō == External Links == *[https://www.nemuro-kotohira.com/ Official Website of Kotohira Shrine] *[http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/top.html Hokkaido Shrine Association] *[http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/16/16001.html Kotohira Shrine] *[http://www.city.nemuro.hokkaido.jp/section/kanko/event_cale/konpira/konpira.html Nemuro City Office (Nemuro Event Information: Kotohira Shrine festival)]{{Dead link|date=August 2023}} *{{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.co.jp/HeartLand/3922/ |title=Nemuro Topics (images and videos of Kotohira Shrine festival) |date=19990427034754}} *[http://www.nemuro-kankou.com/events/kotohira.html Nemuro City Tourist Association: Kotohira Shrine festival]{{Dead link|date=August 2023}} *[http://crabnavi.ninpou.jp/nemuro-kotohira/top.html Nemuro Tourism and festival (Site introducing Kotohira Shrine festival and tourist attractions)] {{shinto-stub}} ==References== {{Reflist}} {{translated page|ja|金刀比羅神社 (根室市)|version=101314516|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:金刀比羅神社 (根室市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11646492}} [[Category:Buildings in Nemuro]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:History of Nemuro]] [[Category:K]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Kotohira shrines]] [[Category:Nemuro Province]] [[Category:Nemuro Province|Shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha merges]] [[Category:Shinbutsu shūgō]] [[Category:Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages]]

{{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to festivals on their respective anniversaries. == History == Kotohira Shrine was established in 1806 (Bunka 3) in {{ill|Nemuro Province|en|Nemuro Province|WD=Q1063001|lt=Nemuro Province|1=Nemuro Province}}, {{ill|Nemuro District|ja|根室郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nemuro District|1=Nemuro district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11110970}} by {{ill|Takadaya Kahei|en|Takadaya Kahei|WD=Q4450102|lt=Kahē Takadaya|1=Takadaya Kahei}}. It was relocated to its current site in 1881 (Meiji 14). The shrine was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in 1881 and promoted to {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in 1919 (Taishō 8). In 2005 (Heisei 18), the shrine celebrated its 200th anniversary, during which the sacred palanquin (mikoshi) was refurbished. A new shrine office was also constructed, including a "Mikoshi Hall and Festival Museum." == Kotohira Shrine Grand Festival == The annual grand festival is held over three days from August 9 (evening festival) to August 11 (main festival with portable shrine procession). The festival includes taiko drumming, performances with golden staves, floats, and traditional hand dances. Four festival districts, established during the {{ill|Taishō period|ja|大正時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishō period|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大正|1=Taishō era|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q329174}}, participate: "First Festival District," "Third Festival District," "Eastern Festival District," and "Western Festival District." On the night of August 9, a competition titled "Sound and Strength Performance" features floats and hand dances, while August 10 showcases taiko drumming and golden staves. The festival was designated an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Asset of Hokkaido|ja|無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Intangible Folk Cultural Asset of Hokkaido|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 民俗文化財|1=Folk Cultural Property|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24895900}} in 2020 (Reiwa 2). == Address == * Kotobira Town 1-4, Nemuro, Hokkaido == External Links == * [https://www.nemuro-kotohira.com/ Official Website of Kotohira Shrine] * [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/top.html Hokkaido Shrine Association] * [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/16/16001.html Kotohira Shrine Information] * [http://www.city.nemuro.hokkaido.jp/section/kanko/event_cale/konpira/konpira.html Nemuro City Event Information: Kotohira Shrine Festival] {{ill-broken-link|date=August 2023}} * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.co.jp/HeartLand/3922/ |title=Nemuro Topics (Kotohira Shrine Festival images and videos) |date=April 27, 1999}} * [http://www.nemuro-kankou.com/events/kotohira.html Nemuro Tourist Association: Kotohira Shrine Grand Festival] {{ill-broken-link|date=August 2023}} * [http://crabnavi.ninpou.jp/nemuro-kotohira/top.html Nemuro Tourism and Festivals: Kotohira Shrine Festival and Sightseeing] {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|金刀比羅神社 (根室市)|version=101314516|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:金刀比羅神社 (根室市)]] ==Merged second translation== {{infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|image=Kotohira Shrine (Nemuro City).jpg | name = Kotohira Shrine | image = [[File:Kotohira Shrine (Nemuro City).jpg|270px]]
Worship Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|43|20|37.64|N|145|35|14.90|E}}}} | location = 1-4 Kotohira-chō, Nemuro-shi, Hokkaidō | coordinates = {{Coord|43|20|37.64|N|145|35|14.90|E|display=title,inline}} | deity = [[Ōmononushi no Kami]] • [[Kotoshironushi no Kami]] • [[Ukanomitama no Kami]] | type = Former [[Ken-sha|Prefectural Shrine]] | founded = {{ill|1806|en|1806|WD=Q6903|lt=1806|1=1806}} (Bunka 3) | Main Building Style = Irimoya-zukuri | festival = August 9–11 | map = {{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=43|lat_min=20|lat_sec=37.6|lon_deg=145|lon_min=35|lon_sec=14.9|label=Kotohira
Shrine|position=left|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|Alternativemap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} }} {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (根室市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine (Nemuro City)|1=Nemuro Kotohira Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata}} is a {{ill|Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira Shrines|lt=Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrines|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in {{ill|Japan|simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan|qq=}}{{Cite web|date=2017-08-07 |title=Nemuro Kotohira Shrine |url=https://nemuro-hokkaido.com/facility/nemuro-kotohira-shrine/ |access-date=2023-10-17 |website=NEMURO |language=ja|qq=draft}}. It worships {{ill|Konpira Gongen|simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Gongen|qq=}}. It is one of the easternmost shrines in Japan. {{ill|Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira Shrines|ja|金刀比羅神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrines|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} are {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}}s dedicated to [[Ōmononushi]]. Due to [[Shinbutsu-shūgō]] they often also worship [[Konpira Gongen]] They are part of the famous Inahachikonten Shrines (稲八金天神社) of the [[Shrine Consolidation Policy]] alongside {{ill|Inari shrine|en|Inari shrine|WD=Q514480|lt=Inari shrine|1=Inari shrine}}, [[Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]], {{ill|Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira Shrines|ja|金刀比羅神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrines|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, and [[Tenmangū|Tenmangu Shrine]]. == History == The shrine's roots can be traced back to 1806 when Kahei Takadaya, a pioneer of northern sea fishing, paid homage to it while overseeing the fishing grounds. Initially located elsewhere, the shrine was moved to its present location in 1881.{{Cite web |date=2018-06-15 |title=Kotohira Shrine |url=https://map.uu-hokkaido.jp/e/nemuro-kotohira-shrine/ |access-date=2023-10-17 |website=Sightseeing information in Hokkaido |language=en-US}} == Attractions == Upon visiting the shrine, devotees and tourists alike are encouraged to explore the surrounding buildings and facilities. A notable feature within the shrine complex is the festival Museum (Omatsuri Shiryokan). This museum houses various documents and displays, among which the luxurious portable shrine, or Omikoshi, stands out. Visitors can also find the Goshuin, a letter bearing the shogun’s scarlet seal. ==festival== The festival goes from August 9 to August 11. It is a major festival of Hokkkaido. It has a major [[mikoshi]] that requires 60 people to carry.{{Cite web |last=Calendar |first=Japanese Traditional festival |date=2023-10-17 |title=Konhira Shrine annual Grand festival |url=https://ohmatsuri.com/en/matsuri-list/konhira-shrine-annual-grand-festival |access-date=2023-10-17 |website=ohmatsuri.com |language=en}} ==Overview== '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社, Kotohira Jinja, also Konpira Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in [[:en:Nemuro, Hokkaidō|Nemuro, Hokkaidō]]. == Deities == *[[Ōmononushi no Kami]] • [[Kotoshironushi no Kami]] • [[Ukanomitama no Kami]] *The shrine also houses the sacred objects of shrines from the {{ill|Northern Territories|en|Northern Territories|WD=Q1217330|lt=Northern Territories|1=Northern Territories}}, including Taraku Island, Shibotsu Island, Suisho Island, Shikotan Island, and Kunashiri Island, and festivals are held on the respective dates of these shrines. == History == The shrine was founded in {{ill|1806|en|1806|WD=Q6903|lt=1806|1=1806}} (Bunka 3) by {{ill|Takadaya Kahei|en|Takadaya Kahei|WD=Q4450102|lt=Takadaya Kahei|1=Takadaya Kahei}} in the Nemuro District of Nemuro Province. In {{ill|1881|en|1881|WD=Q7808|lt=1881|1=1881}} (Meiji 14), it was relocated to its current location. The shrine was designated as a village shrine the same year and was promoted to a prefectural shrine in {{ill|1919|en|1919|WD=Q2157|lt=1919|1=1919}} (Taishō 8). In {{ill|2005|en|2005|WD=Q2019|lt=2005|1=2005}} (Heisei 18), the shrine celebrated its 200th anniversary, during which the mikoshi (portable shrine) was refurbished and a new shrine office was built, including the Mikoshi Hall and festival Museum. == Kotohira Shrine festival == The Kotohira Shrine festival is held over three days from August 9 (Eve festival), August 10 (Main festival, Mikoshi Procession), and August 11 (Mikoshi Procession). The festival districts that offer dedications, including drum and kanabō (iron club) performances, and parade floats, were established during the Taishō era and consist of four districts: "First festival District," "Third festival District," "Eastern festival District," and "Western festival District." On the night of the 9th, a performance titled "Sound and Strength Competition" features the parade floats and tekomai (hand drum dance). On the 10th, performances of drums and kanabō are held. == location == *1-4 Kotohira-chō, Nemuro-shi, Hokkaidō == External Links == *[https://www.nemuro-kotohira.com/ Official Website of Kotohira Shrine] *[http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/top.html Hokkaido Shrine Association] *[http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/16/16001.html Kotohira Shrine] *[http://www.city.nemuro.hokkaido.jp/section/kanko/event_cale/konpira/konpira.html Nemuro City Office (Nemuro Event Information: Kotohira Shrine festival)]{{Dead link|date=August 2023}} *{{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.co.jp/HeartLand/3922/ |title=Nemuro Topics (images and videos of Kotohira Shrine festival) |date=19990427034754}} *[http://www.nemuro-kankou.com/events/kotohira.html Nemuro City Tourist Association: Kotohira Shrine festival]{{Dead link|date=August 2023}} *[http://crabnavi.ninpou.jp/nemuro-kotohira/top.html Nemuro Tourism and festival (Site introducing Kotohira Shrine festival and tourist attractions)] {{shinto-stub}} ==References== {{Reflist}} {{translated page|ja|金刀比羅神社 (根室市)|version=101314516|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:金刀比羅神社 (根室市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11646492}} [[Category:Buildings in Nemuro]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:History of Nemuro]] [[Category:K]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Kotohira shrines]] [[Category:Nemuro Province]] [[Category:Nemuro Province|Shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha merges]] [[Category:Shinbutsu shūgō]] [[Category:Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages]]

Shrine|position=left|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|AlternativeMap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} | location = {{ill|Nemuro|WD=Q502205|1=Nemuro, Hokkaido}} | coordinates = {{Coord|43.343789|N|145.587472|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社), also known as Konpira Shrine, is located in {{ill|Nemuro, Hokkaido|en|Nemuro, Hokkaido|WD=Q502205|lt=Nemuro|1=Nemuro, Hokkaido}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|en|Hokkaido|WD=Q35581|lt=Hokkaido|1=Hokkaido}}. == Deities == * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}} * The shrine also holds sacred objects from shrines located on the {{ill|Northern Territories|ja|北方地域|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Northern Territories|1=South Kuril Islands|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11401819}} of {{ill|Polonsky Island|en|Polonsky Island|WD=Q904875|lt=Taraku Island|1=Polonsky Island}}, {{ill|Zelyony Island (Kuril Islands)|en|Zelyony Island (Kuril Islands)|WD=Q904898|lt=Shibotsu Island|1=Zelyony Island (Kuril Islands)}}, [[:en:Tanfiliev Island|Suisho Island]], {{ill|Shikotan|en|Shikotan|WD=Q1043128|lt=Shikotan|1=Shikotan}}, and {{ill|Kunashir|en|Kunashir|WD=Q847285|lt=Kunashiri|1=Kunashir}}, conducting festivals on their respective anniversaries. == History == Kotohira Shrine was established in 1806 (Bunka 3) in {{ill|Nemuro Province|en|Nemuro Province|WD=Q1063001|lt=Nemuro Province|1=Nemuro Province}}, {{ill|Nemuro District|ja|根室郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nemuro District|1=Nemuro district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11110970}} by {{ill|Takadaya Kahei|en|Takadaya Kahei|WD=Q4450102|lt=Kahē Takadaya|1=Takadaya Kahei}}. It was relocated to its current site in 1881 (Meiji 14). The shrine was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in 1881 and promoted to {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in 1919 (Taishō 8). In 2005 (Heisei 18), the shrine celebrated its 200th anniversary, during which the sacred palanquin (mikoshi) was refurbished. A new shrine office was also constructed, including a "Mikoshi Hall and Festival Museum." == Kotohira Shrine Grand Festival == The annual grand festival is held over three days from August 9 (evening festival) to August 11 (main festival with portable shrine procession). The festival includes taiko drumming, performances with golden staves, floats, and traditional hand dances. Four festival districts, established during the {{ill|Taishō period|ja|大正時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishō period|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大正|1=Taishō era|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q329174}}, participate: "First Festival District," "Third Festival District," "Eastern Festival District," and "Western Festival District." On the night of August 9, a competition titled "Sound and Strength Performance" features floats and hand dances, while August 10 showcases taiko drumming and golden staves. The festival was designated an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Asset of Hokkaido|ja|無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Intangible Folk Cultural Asset of Hokkaido|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 民俗文化財|1=Folk Cultural Property|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24895900}} in 2020 (Reiwa 2). == Address == * Kotobira Town 1-4, Nemuro, Hokkaido == External Links == * [https://www.nemuro-kotohira.com/ Official Website of Kotohira Shrine] * [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/top.html Hokkaido Shrine Association] * [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/16/16001.html Kotohira Shrine Information] * [http://www.city.nemuro.hokkaido.jp/section/kanko/event_cale/konpira/konpira.html Nemuro City Event Information: Kotohira Shrine Festival] {{ill-broken-link|date=August 2023}} * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.co.jp/HeartLand/3922/ |title=Nemuro Topics (Kotohira Shrine Festival images and videos) |date=April 27, 1999}} * [http://www.nemuro-kankou.com/events/kotohira.html Nemuro Tourist Association: Kotohira Shrine Grand Festival] {{ill-broken-link|date=August 2023}} * [http://crabnavi.ninpou.jp/nemuro-kotohira/top.html Nemuro Tourism and Festivals: Kotohira Shrine Festival and Sightseeing] {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|金刀比羅神社 (根室市)|version=101314516|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:金刀比羅神社 (根室市)]] [[Category:Shikinaisha merges]] ==Merged second translation== {{infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|image=Kotohira Shrine (Nemuro City).jpg | name = Kotohira Shrine | image = [[File:Kotohira Shrine (Nemuro City).jpg|270px]]
Worship Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|43|20|37.64|N|145|35|14.90|E}}}} | location = 1-4 Kotohira-chō, Nemuro-shi, Hokkaidō | coordinates = {{Coord|43|20|37.64|N|145|35|14.90|E|display=title,inline}} | deity = [[Ōmononushi no Kami]] • [[Kotoshironushi no Kami]] • [[Ukanomitama no Kami]] | type = Former [[Ken-sha|Prefectural Shrine]] | founded = {{ill|1806|en|1806|WD=Q6903|lt=1806|1=1806}} (Bunka 3) | Main Building Style = Irimoya-zukuri | festival = August 9–11 | map = {{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=43|lat_min=20|lat_sec=37.6|lon_deg=145|lon_min=35|lon_sec=14.9|label=Kotohira
Shrine|position=left|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|Alternativemap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} }} {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (根室市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine (Nemuro City)|1=Nemuro Kotohira Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata}} is a {{ill|Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira Shrines|lt=Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrines|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in {{ill|Japan|simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan|qq=}}{{Cite web|date=2017-08-07 |title=Nemuro Kotohira Shrine |url=https://nemuro-hokkaido.com/facility/nemuro-kotohira-shrine/ |access-date=2023-10-17 |website=NEMURO |language=ja|qq=draft}}. It worships {{ill|Konpira Gongen|simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Gongen|qq=}}. It is one of the easternmost shrines in Japan. {{ill|Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira Shrines|ja|金刀比羅神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrines|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} are {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}}s dedicated to [[Ōmononushi]]. Due to [[Shinbutsu-shūgō]] they often also worship [[Konpira Gongen]] They are part of the famous Inahachikonten Shrines (稲八金天神社) of the [[Shrine Consolidation Policy]] alongside {{ill|Inari shrine|en|Inari shrine|WD=Q514480|lt=Inari shrine|1=Inari shrine}}, [[Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]], {{ill|Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira Shrines|ja|金刀比羅神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrines|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, and [[Tenmangū|Tenmangu Shrine]]. == History == The shrine's roots can be traced back to 1806 when Kahei Takadaya, a pioneer of northern sea fishing, paid homage to it while overseeing the fishing grounds. Initially located elsewhere, the shrine was moved to its present location in 1881.{{Cite web |date=2018-06-15 |title=Kotohira Shrine |url=https://map.uu-hokkaido.jp/e/nemuro-kotohira-shrine/ |access-date=2023-10-17 |website=Sightseeing information in Hokkaido |language=en-US}} == Attractions == Upon visiting the shrine, devotees and tourists alike are encouraged to explore the surrounding buildings and facilities. A notable feature within the shrine complex is the festival Museum (Omatsuri Shiryokan). This museum houses various documents and displays, among which the luxurious portable shrine, or Omikoshi, stands out. Visitors can also find the Goshuin, a letter bearing the shogun’s scarlet seal. ==festival== The festival goes from August 9 to August 11. It is a major festival of Hokkkaido. It has a major [[mikoshi]] that requires 60 people to carry.{{Cite web |last=Calendar |first=Japanese Traditional festival |date=2023-10-17 |title=Konhira Shrine annual Grand festival |url=https://ohmatsuri.com/en/matsuri-list/konhira-shrine-annual-grand-festival |access-date=2023-10-17 |website=ohmatsuri.com |language=en}} ==Overview== '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社, Kotohira Jinja, also Konpira Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in [[:en:Nemuro, Hokkaidō|Nemuro, Hokkaidō]]. == Deities == *[[Ōmononushi no Kami]] • [[Kotoshironushi no Kami]] • [[Ukanomitama no Kami]] *The shrine also houses the sacred objects of shrines from the {{ill|Northern Territories|en|Northern Territories|WD=Q1217330|lt=Northern Territories|1=Northern Territories}}, including Taraku Island, Shibotsu Island, Suisho Island, Shikotan Island, and Kunashiri Island, and festivals are held on the respective dates of these shrines. == History == The shrine was founded in {{ill|1806|en|1806|WD=Q6903|lt=1806|1=1806}} (Bunka 3) by {{ill|Takadaya Kahei|en|Takadaya Kahei|WD=Q4450102|lt=Takadaya Kahei|1=Takadaya Kahei}} in the Nemuro District of Nemuro Province. In {{ill|1881|en|1881|WD=Q7808|lt=1881|1=1881}} (Meiji 14), it was relocated to its current location. The shrine was designated as a village shrine the same year and was promoted to a prefectural shrine in {{ill|1919|en|1919|WD=Q2157|lt=1919|1=1919}} (Taishō 8). In {{ill|2005|en|2005|WD=Q2019|lt=2005|1=2005}} (Heisei 18), the shrine celebrated its 200th anniversary, during which the mikoshi (portable shrine) was refurbished and a new shrine office was built, including the Mikoshi Hall and festival Museum. == Kotohira Shrine festival == The Kotohira Shrine festival is held over three days from August 9 (Eve festival), August 10 (Main festival, Mikoshi Procession), and August 11 (Mikoshi Procession). The festival districts that offer dedications, including drum and kanabō (iron club) performances, and parade floats, were established during the Taishō era and consist of four districts: "First festival District," "Third festival District," "Eastern festival District," and "Western festival District." On the night of the 9th, a performance titled "Sound and Strength Competition" features the parade floats and tekomai (hand drum dance). On the 10th, performances of drums and kanabō are held. == location == *1-4 Kotohira-chō, Nemuro-shi, Hokkaidō == External Links == *[https://www.nemuro-kotohira.com/ Official Website of Kotohira Shrine] *[http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/top.html Hokkaido Shrine Association] *[http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/16/16001.html Kotohira Shrine] *[http://www.city.nemuro.hokkaido.jp/section/kanko/event_cale/konpira/konpira.html Nemuro City Office (Nemuro Event Information: Kotohira Shrine festival)]{{Dead link|date=August 2023}} *{{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.co.jp/HeartLand/3922/ |title=Nemuro Topics (images and videos of Kotohira Shrine festival) |date=19990427034754}} *[http://www.nemuro-kankou.com/events/kotohira.html Nemuro City Tourist Association: Kotohira Shrine festival]{{Dead link|date=August 2023}} *[http://crabnavi.ninpou.jp/nemuro-kotohira/top.html Nemuro Tourism and festival (Site introducing Kotohira Shrine festival and tourist attractions)] {{shinto-stub}} ==References== {{Reflist}} {{translated page|ja|金刀比羅神社 (根室市)|version=101314516|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:金刀比羅神社 (根室市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11646492}} [[Category:Buildings in Nemuro]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:History of Nemuro]] [[Category:K]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Kotohira shrines]] [[Category:Nemuro Province]] [[Category:Nemuro Province|Shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha merges]] [[Category:Shinbutsu shūgō]] [[Category:Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages]]

[[ja:金刀比羅神社 (根室市)]] [[ja:金刀比羅神社 (根室市)]] [[ja:金刀比羅神社 (根室市)]] 金刀比羅神社_(根室市)&action=edit {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Kotohira Shrine (Nemuro City).jpg | location = Kotobira Town 1-4, {{ill|Nemuro, Hokkaido|en|Nemuro, Hokkaido|WD=Q502205|lt=Nemuro|1=Nemuro, Hokkaido}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|en|Hokkaido|WD=Q35581|lt=Hokkaido|1=Hokkaido}} | Position = {{ill-coordinates|43|20|37.64|N|145|35|14.90|E|inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Ōmononushi|WD=Q10936701|1=Ōmononushi}} | Classification = Former {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | Established = 1806 (Bunka 3) | MainHall = Irimoya-zukuri (hip-and-gable style) | Alternatename = | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = August 9–11 | Rituals = | Map = {{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=43|lat_min=20|lat_sec=37.6|lon_deg=145|lon_min=35|lon_sec=14.9|label=Kotohira
Shrine|position=left|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|AlternativeMap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} | location = {{ill|Nemuro|WD=Q502205|1=Nemuro, Hokkaido}} | coordinates = {{Coord|43.343789|N|145.587472|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社), also known as Konpira Shrine, is located in {{ill|Nemuro, Hokkaido|en|Nemuro, Hokkaido|WD=Q502205|lt=Nemuro|1=Nemuro, Hokkaido}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|en|Hokkaido|WD=Q35581|lt=Hokkaido|1=Hokkaido}}. == Deities == * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}} * The shrine also holds sacred objects from shrines located on the {{ill|Northern Territories|ja|北方地域|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Northern Territories|1=South Kuril Islands|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11401819}} of {{ill|Polonsky Island|en|Polonsky Island|WD=Q904875|lt=Taraku Island|1=Polonsky Island}}, {{ill|Zelyony Island (Kuril Islands)|en|Zelyony Island (Kuril Islands)|WD=Q904898|lt=Shibotsu Island|1=Zelyony Island (Kuril Islands)}}, [[:en:Tanfiliev Island|Suisho Island]], {{ill|Shikotan|en|Shikotan|WD=Q1043128|lt=Shikotan|1=Shikotan}}, and {{ill|Kunashir|en|Kunashir|WD=Q847285|lt=Kunashiri|1=Kunashir}}, conducting festivals on their respective anniversaries. == History == Kotohira Shrine was established in 1806 (Bunka 3) in {{ill|Nemuro Province|en|Nemuro Province|WD=Q1063001|lt=Nemuro Province|1=Nemuro Province}}, {{ill|Nemuro District|ja|根室郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nemuro District|1=Nemuro district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11110970}} by {{ill|Takadaya Kahei|en|Takadaya Kahei|WD=Q4450102|lt=Kahē Takadaya|1=Takadaya Kahei}}. It was relocated to its current site in 1881 (Meiji 14). The shrine was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in 1881 and promoted to {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in 1919 (Taishō 8). In 2005 (Heisei 18), the shrine celebrated its 200th anniversary, during which the sacred palanquin (mikoshi) was refurbished. A new shrine office was also constructed, including a "Mikoshi Hall and Festival Museum." == Kotohira Shrine Grand Festival == The annual grand festival is held over three days from August 9 (evening festival) to August 11 (main festival with portable shrine procession). The festival includes taiko drumming, performances with golden staves, floats, and traditional hand dances. Four festival districts, established during the {{ill|Taishō period|ja|大正時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishō period|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大正|1=Taishō era|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q329174}}, participate: "First Festival District," "Third Festival District," "Eastern Festival District," and "Western Festival District." On the night of August 9, a competition titled "Sound and Strength Performance" features floats and hand dances, while August 10 showcases taiko drumming and golden staves. The festival was designated an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Asset of Hokkaido|ja|無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Intangible Folk Cultural Asset of Hokkaido|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 民俗文化財|1=Folk Cultural Property|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24895900}} in 2020 (Reiwa 2). == Address == * Kotobira Town 1-4, Nemuro, Hokkaido == External Links == * [https://www.nemuro-kotohira.com/ Official Website of Kotohira Shrine] * [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/top.html Hokkaido Shrine Association] * [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/16/16001.html Kotohira Shrine Information] * [http://www.city.nemuro.hokkaido.jp/section/kanko/event_cale/konpira/konpira.html Nemuro City Event Information: Kotohira Shrine Festival] {{ill-broken-link|date=August 2023}} * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.co.jp/HeartLand/3922/ |title=Nemuro Topics (Kotohira Shrine Festival images and videos) |date=April 27, 1999}} * [http://www.nemuro-kankou.com/events/kotohira.html Nemuro Tourist Association: Kotohira Shrine Grand Festival] {{ill-broken-link|date=August 2023}} * [http://crabnavi.ninpou.jp/nemuro-kotohira/top.html Nemuro Tourism and Festivals: Kotohira Shrine Festival and Sightseeing] {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|金刀比羅神社 (根室市)|version=101314516|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:金刀比羅神社 (根室市)]] [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]] ==Merged second translation== {{infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|image=Kotohira Shrine (Nemuro City).jpg | name = Kotohira Shrine | image = [[File:Kotohira Shrine (Nemuro City).jpg|270px]]
Worship Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|43|20|37.64|N|145|35|14.90|E}}}} | location = 1-4 Kotohira-chō, Nemuro-shi, Hokkaidō | coordinates = {{Coord|43|20|37.64|N|145|35|14.90|E|display=title,inline}} | deity = [[Ōmononushi no Kami]] • [[Kotoshironushi no Kami]] • [[Ukanomitama no Kami]] | type = Former [[Ken-sha|Prefectural Shrine]] | founded = {{ill|1806|en|1806|WD=Q6903|lt=1806|1=1806}} (Bunka 3) | Main Building Style = Irimoya-zukuri | festival = August 9–11 | map = {{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=43|lat_min=20|lat_sec=37.6|lon_deg=145|lon_min=35|lon_sec=14.9|label=Kotohira
Shrine|position=left|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|Alternativemap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} }} {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (根室市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine (Nemuro City)|1=Nemuro Kotohira Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata}} is a {{ill|Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira Shrines|lt=Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrines|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in {{ill|Japan|simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan|qq=}}{{Cite web|date=2017-08-07 |title=Nemuro Kotohira Shrine |url=https://nemuro-hokkaido.com/facility/nemuro-kotohira-shrine/ |access-date=2023-10-17 |website=NEMURO |language=ja|qq=draft}}. It worships {{ill|Konpira Gongen|simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Gongen|qq=}}. It is one of the easternmost shrines in Japan. {{ill|Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira Shrines|ja|金刀比羅神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrines|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} are {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}}s dedicated to [[Ōmononushi]]. Due to [[Shinbutsu-shūgō]] they often also worship [[Konpira Gongen]] They are part of the famous Inahachikonten Shrines (稲八金天神社) of the [[Shrine Consolidation Policy]] alongside {{ill|Inari shrine|en|Inari shrine|WD=Q514480|lt=Inari shrine|1=Inari shrine}}, [[Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]], {{ill|Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira Shrines|ja|金刀比羅神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrines|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, and [[Tenmangū|Tenmangu Shrine]]. == History == The shrine's roots can be traced back to 1806 when Kahei Takadaya, a pioneer of northern sea fishing, paid homage to it while overseeing the fishing grounds. Initially located elsewhere, the shrine was moved to its present location in 1881.{{Cite web |date=2018-06-15 |title=Kotohira Shrine |url=https://map.uu-hokkaido.jp/e/nemuro-kotohira-shrine/ |access-date=2023-10-17 |website=Sightseeing information in Hokkaido |language=en-US}} == Attractions == Upon visiting the shrine, devotees and tourists alike are encouraged to explore the surrounding buildings and facilities. A notable feature within the shrine complex is the festival Museum (Omatsuri Shiryokan). This museum houses various documents and displays, among which the luxurious portable shrine, or Omikoshi, stands out. Visitors can also find the Goshuin, a letter bearing the shogun’s scarlet seal. ==festival== The festival goes from August 9 to August 11. It is a major festival of Hokkkaido. It has a major [[mikoshi]] that requires 60 people to carry.{{Cite web |last=Calendar |first=Japanese Traditional festival |date=2023-10-17 |title=Konhira Shrine annual Grand festival |url=https://ohmatsuri.com/en/matsuri-list/konhira-shrine-annual-grand-festival |access-date=2023-10-17 |website=ohmatsuri.com |language=en}} ==Overview== '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社, Kotohira Jinja, also Konpira Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in [[:en:Nemuro, Hokkaidō|Nemuro, Hokkaidō]]. == Deities == *[[Ōmononushi no Kami]] • [[Kotoshironushi no Kami]] • [[Ukanomitama no Kami]] *The shrine also houses the sacred objects of shrines from the {{ill|Northern Territories|en|Northern Territories|WD=Q1217330|lt=Northern Territories|1=Northern Territories}}, including Taraku Island, Shibotsu Island, Suisho Island, Shikotan Island, and Kunashiri Island, and festivals are held on the respective dates of these shrines. == History == The shrine was founded in {{ill|1806|en|1806|WD=Q6903|lt=1806|1=1806}} (Bunka 3) by {{ill|Takadaya Kahei|en|Takadaya Kahei|WD=Q4450102|lt=Takadaya Kahei|1=Takadaya Kahei}} in the Nemuro District of Nemuro Province. In {{ill|1881|en|1881|WD=Q7808|lt=1881|1=1881}} (Meiji 14), it was relocated to its current location. The shrine was designated as a village shrine the same year and was promoted to a prefectural shrine in {{ill|1919|en|1919|WD=Q2157|lt=1919|1=1919}} (Taishō 8). In {{ill|2005|en|2005|WD=Q2019|lt=2005|1=2005}} (Heisei 18), the shrine celebrated its 200th anniversary, during which the mikoshi (portable shrine) was refurbished and a new shrine office was built, including the Mikoshi Hall and festival Museum. == Kotohira Shrine festival == The Kotohira Shrine festival is held over three days from August 9 (Eve festival), August 10 (Main festival, Mikoshi Procession), and August 11 (Mikoshi Procession). The festival districts that offer dedications, including drum and kanabō (iron club) performances, and parade floats, were established during the Taishō era and consist of four districts: "First festival District," "Third festival District," "Eastern festival District," and "Western festival District." On the night of the 9th, a performance titled "Sound and Strength Competition" features the parade floats and tekomai (hand drum dance). On the 10th, performances of drums and kanabō are held. == location == *1-4 Kotohira-chō, Nemuro-shi, Hokkaidō == External Links == *[https://www.nemuro-kotohira.com/ Official Website of Kotohira Shrine] *[http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/top.html Hokkaido Shrine Association] *[http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/16/16001.html Kotohira Shrine] *[http://www.city.nemuro.hokkaido.jp/section/kanko/event_cale/konpira/konpira.html Nemuro City Office (Nemuro Event Information: Kotohira Shrine festival)]{{Dead link|date=August 2023}} *{{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.co.jp/HeartLand/3922/ |title=Nemuro Topics (images and videos of Kotohira Shrine festival) |date=19990427034754}} *[http://www.nemuro-kankou.com/events/kotohira.html Nemuro City Tourist Association: Kotohira Shrine festival]{{Dead link|date=August 2023}} *[http://crabnavi.ninpou.jp/nemuro-kotohira/top.html Nemuro Tourism and festival (Site introducing Kotohira Shrine festival and tourist attractions)] {{shinto-stub}} ==References== {{Reflist}} {{translated page|ja|金刀比羅神社 (根室市)|version=101314516|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:金刀比羅神社 (根室市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11646492}} [[Category:Buildings in Nemuro]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:History of Nemuro]] [[Category:K]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Kotohira shrines]] [[Category:Nemuro Province]] [[Category:Nemuro Province|Shrines]] [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]] [[Category:Shinbutsu shūgō]] [[Category:Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages]]

Hokkaido}} | coordinates = {{Coord|43.343789|N|145.587472|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社), also known as Konpira Shrine, is located in {{ill|Nemuro, Hokkaido|en|Nemuro, Hokkaido|WD=Q502205|lt=Nemuro|1=Nemuro, Hokkaido}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|en|Hokkaido|WD=Q35581|lt=Hokkaido|1=Hokkaido}}. == Deities == * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}} * The shrine also holds sacred objects from shrines located on the {{ill|Northern Territories|ja|北方地域|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Northern Territories|1=South Kuril Islands|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11401819}} of {{ill|Polonsky Island|en|Polonsky Island|WD=Q904875|lt=Taraku Island|1=Polonsky Island}}, {{ill|Zelyony Island (Kuril Islands)|en|Zelyony Island (Kuril Islands)|WD=Q904898|lt=Shibotsu Island|1=Zelyony Island (Kuril Islands)}}, [[:en:Tanfiliev Island|Suisho Island]], {{ill|Shikotan|en|Shikotan|WD=Q1043128|lt=Shikotan|1=Shikotan}}, and {{ill|Kunashir|en|Kunashir|WD=Q847285|lt=Kunashiri|1=Kunashir}}, conducting festivals on their respective anniversaries. == History == Kotohira Shrine was established in 1806 (Bunka 3) in {{ill|Nemuro Province|en|Nemuro Province|WD=Q1063001|lt=Nemuro Province|1=Nemuro Province}}, {{ill|Nemuro District|ja|根室郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nemuro District|1=Nemuro district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11110970}} by {{ill|Takadaya Kahei|en|Takadaya Kahei|WD=Q4450102|lt=Kahē Takadaya|1=Takadaya Kahei}}. It was relocated to its current site in 1881 (Meiji 14). The shrine was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in 1881 and promoted to {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in 1919 (Taishō 8). In 2005 (Heisei 18), the shrine celebrated its 200th anniversary, during which the sacred palanquin (mikoshi) was refurbished. A new shrine office was also constructed, including a "Mikoshi Hall and Festival Museum." == Kotohira Shrine Grand Festival == The annual grand festival is held over three days from August 9 (evening festival) to August 11 (main festival with portable shrine procession). The festival includes taiko drumming, performances with golden staves, floats, and traditional hand dances. Four festival districts, established during the {{ill|Taishō period|ja|大正時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishō period|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大正|1=Taishō era|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q329174}}, participate: "First Festival District," "Third Festival District," "Eastern Festival District," and "Western Festival District." On the night of August 9, a competition titled "Sound and Strength Performance" features floats and hand dances, while August 10 showcases taiko drumming and golden staves. The festival was designated an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Asset of Hokkaido|ja|無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Intangible Folk Cultural Asset of Hokkaido|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 民俗文化財|1=Folk Cultural Property|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24895900}} in 2020 (Reiwa 2). == Address == * Kotobira Town 1-4, Nemuro, Hokkaido == External Links == * [https://www.nemuro-kotohira.com/ Official Website of Kotohira Shrine] * [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/top.html Hokkaido Shrine Association] * [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/16/16001.html Kotohira Shrine Information] * [http://www.city.nemuro.hokkaido.jp/section/kanko/event_cale/konpira/konpira.html Nemuro City Event Information: Kotohira Shrine Festival] {{ill-broken-link|date=August 2023}} * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.co.jp/HeartLand/3922/ |title=Nemuro Topics (Kotohira Shrine Festival images and videos) |date=April 27, 1999}} * [http://www.nemuro-kankou.com/events/kotohira.html Nemuro Tourist Association: Kotohira Shrine Grand Festival] {{ill-broken-link|date=August 2023}} * [http://crabnavi.ninpou.jp/nemuro-kotohira/top.html Nemuro Tourism and Festivals: Kotohira Shrine Festival and Sightseeing] {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|金刀比羅神社 (根室市)|version=101314516|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:金刀比羅神社 (根室市)]] [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]] ==Merged second translation== {{infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|image=Kotohira Shrine (Nemuro City).jpg | name = Kotohira Shrine | image = [[File:Kotohira Shrine (Nemuro City).jpg|270px]]
Worship Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|43|20|37.64|N|145|35|14.90|E}}}} | location = 1-4 Kotohira-chō, Nemuro-shi, Hokkaidō | coordinates = {{Coord|43|20|37.64|N|145|35|14.90|E|display=title,inline}} | deity = [[Ōmononushi no Kami]] • [[Kotoshironushi no Kami]] • [[Ukanomitama no Kami]] | type = Former [[Ken-sha|Prefectural Shrine]] | founded = {{ill|1806|en|1806|WD=Q6903|lt=1806|1=1806}} (Bunka 3) | Main Building Style = Irimoya-zukuri | festival = August 9–11 | map = {{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=43|lat_min=20|lat_sec=37.6|lon_deg=145|lon_min=35|lon_sec=14.9|label=Kotohira
Shrine|position=left|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|Alternativemap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} }} {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (根室市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine (Nemuro City)|1=Nemuro Kotohira Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata}} is a {{ill|Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira Shrines|lt=Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrines|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in {{ill|Japan|simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan|qq=}}{{Cite web|date=2017-08-07 |title=Nemuro Kotohira Shrine |url=https://nemuro-hokkaido.com/facility/nemuro-kotohira-shrine/ |access-date=2023-10-17 |website=NEMURO |language=ja|qq=draft}}. It worships {{ill|Konpira Gongen|simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Gongen|qq=}}. It is one of the easternmost shrines in Japan. {{ill|Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira Shrines|ja|金刀比羅神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrines|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} are {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}}s dedicated to [[Ōmononushi]]. Due to [[Shinbutsu-shūgō]] they often also worship [[Konpira Gongen]] They are part of the famous Inahachikonten Shrines (稲八金天神社) of the [[Shrine Consolidation Policy]] alongside {{ill|Inari shrine|en|Inari shrine|WD=Q514480|lt=Inari shrine|1=Inari shrine}}, [[Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]], {{ill|Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira Shrines|ja|金刀比羅神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrines|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, and [[Tenmangū|Tenmangu Shrine]]. == History == The shrine's roots can be traced back to 1806 when Kahei Takadaya, a pioneer of northern sea fishing, paid homage to it while overseeing the fishing grounds. Initially located elsewhere, the shrine was moved to its present location in 1881.{{Cite web |date=2018-06-15 |title=Kotohira Shrine |url=https://map.uu-hokkaido.jp/e/nemuro-kotohira-shrine/ |access-date=2023-10-17 |website=Sightseeing information in Hokkaido |language=en-US}} == Attractions == Upon visiting the shrine, devotees and tourists alike are encouraged to explore the surrounding buildings and facilities. A notable feature within the shrine complex is the festival Museum (Omatsuri Shiryokan). This museum houses various documents and displays, among which the luxurious portable shrine, or Omikoshi, stands out. Visitors can also find the Goshuin, a letter bearing the shogun’s scarlet seal. ==festival== The festival goes from August 9 to August 11. It is a major festival of Hokkkaido. It has a major [[mikoshi]] that requires 60 people to carry.{{Cite web |last=Calendar |first=Japanese Traditional festival |date=2023-10-17 |title=Konhira Shrine annual Grand festival |url=https://ohmatsuri.com/en/matsuri-list/konhira-shrine-annual-grand-festival |access-date=2023-10-17 |website=ohmatsuri.com |language=en}} ==Overview== '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社, Kotohira Jinja, also Konpira Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in [[:en:Nemuro, Hokkaidō|Nemuro, Hokkaidō]]. == Deities == *[[Ōmononushi no Kami]] • [[Kotoshironushi no Kami]] • [[Ukanomitama no Kami]] *The shrine also houses the sacred objects of shrines from the {{ill|Northern Territories|en|Northern Territories|WD=Q1217330|lt=Northern Territories|1=Northern Territories}}, including Taraku Island, Shibotsu Island, Suisho Island, Shikotan Island, and Kunashiri Island, and festivals are held on the respective dates of these shrines. == History == The shrine was founded in {{ill|1806|en|1806|WD=Q6903|lt=1806|1=1806}} (Bunka 3) by {{ill|Takadaya Kahei|en|Takadaya Kahei|WD=Q4450102|lt=Takadaya Kahei|1=Takadaya Kahei}} in the Nemuro District of Nemuro Province. In {{ill|1881|en|1881|WD=Q7808|lt=1881|1=1881}} (Meiji 14), it was relocated to its current location. The shrine was designated as a village shrine the same year and was promoted to a prefectural shrine in {{ill|1919|en|1919|WD=Q2157|lt=1919|1=1919}} (Taishō 8). In {{ill|2005|en|2005|WD=Q2019|lt=2005|1=2005}} (Heisei 18), the shrine celebrated its 200th anniversary, during which the mikoshi (portable shrine) was refurbished and a new shrine office was built, including the Mikoshi Hall and festival Museum. == Kotohira Shrine festival == The Kotohira Shrine festival is held over three days from August 9 (Eve festival), August 10 (Main festival, Mikoshi Procession), and August 11 (Mikoshi Procession). The festival districts that offer dedications, including drum and kanabō (iron club) performances, and parade floats, were established during the Taishō era and consist of four districts: "First festival District," "Third festival District," "Eastern festival District," and "Western festival District." On the night of the 9th, a performance titled "Sound and Strength Competition" features the parade floats and tekomai (hand drum dance). On the 10th, performances of drums and kanabō are held. == location == *1-4 Kotohira-chō, Nemuro-shi, Hokkaidō == External Links == *[https://www.nemuro-kotohira.com/ Official Website of Kotohira Shrine] *[http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/top.html Hokkaido Shrine Association] *[http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/16/16001.html Kotohira Shrine] *[http://www.city.nemuro.hokkaido.jp/section/kanko/event_cale/konpira/konpira.html Nemuro City Office (Nemuro Event Information: Kotohira Shrine festival)]{{Dead link|date=August 2023}} *{{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.co.jp/HeartLand/3922/ |title=Nemuro Topics (images and videos of Kotohira Shrine festival) |date=19990427034754}} *[http://www.nemuro-kankou.com/events/kotohira.html Nemuro City Tourist Association: Kotohira Shrine festival]{{Dead link|date=August 2023}} *[http://crabnavi.ninpou.jp/nemuro-kotohira/top.html Nemuro Tourism and festival (Site introducing Kotohira Shrine festival and tourist attractions)] {{shinto-stub}} ==References== {{Reflist}} {{translated page|ja|金刀比羅神社 (根室市)|version=101314516|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:金刀比羅神社 (根室市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11646492}} [[Category:Buildings in Nemuro]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:History of Nemuro]] [[Category:K]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Kotohira shrines]] [[Category:Nemuro Province]] [[Category:Nemuro Province|Shrines]] [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]] [[Category:Shinbutsu shūgō]] [[Category:Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages]]

Hokkaido}} | coordinates = {{Coord|43.343789|N|145.587472|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社), also known as Konpira Shrine, is located in {{ill|Nemuro, Hokkaido|en|Nemuro, Hokkaido|WD=Q502205|lt=Nemuro|1=Nemuro, Hokkaido}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|en|Hokkaido|WD=Q35581|lt=Hokkaido|1=Hokkaido}}. == Deities == * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}} * The shrine also holds sacred objects from shrines located on the {{ill|Northern Territories|ja|北方地域|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Northern Territories|1=South Kuril Islands|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11401819}} of {{ill|Polonsky Island|en|Polonsky Island|WD=Q904875|lt=Taraku Island|1=Polonsky Island}}, {{ill|Zelyony Island (Kuril Islands)|en|Zelyony Island (Kuril Islands)|WD=Q904898|lt=Shibotsu Island|1=Zelyony Island (Kuril Islands)}}, [[:en:Tanfiliev Island|Suisho Island]], {{ill|Shikotan|en|Shikotan|WD=Q1043128|lt=Shikotan|1=Shikotan}}, and {{ill|Kunashir|en|Kunashir|WD=Q847285|lt=Kunashiri|1=Kunashir}}, conducting festivals on their respective anniversaries. == History == Kotohira Shrine was established in 1806 (Bunka 3) in {{ill|Nemuro Province|en|Nemuro Province|WD=Q1063001|lt=Nemuro Province|1=Nemuro Province}}, {{ill|Nemuro District|ja|根室郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nemuro District|1=Nemuro district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11110970}} by {{ill|Takadaya Kahei|en|Takadaya Kahei|WD=Q4450102|lt=Kahē Takadaya|1=Takadaya Kahei}}. It was relocated to its current site in 1881 (Meiji 14). The shrine was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in 1881 and promoted to {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in 1919 (Taishō 8). In 2005 (Heisei 18), the shrine celebrated its 200th anniversary, during which the sacred palanquin (mikoshi) was refurbished. A new shrine office was also constructed, including a "Mikoshi Hall and Festival Museum." == Kotohira Shrine Grand Festival == The annual grand festival is held over three days from August 9 (evening festival) to August 11 (main festival with portable shrine procession). The festival includes taiko drumming, performances with golden staves, floats, and traditional hand dances. Four festival districts, established during the {{ill|Taishō period|ja|大正時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishō period|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大正|1=Taishō era|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q329174}}, participate: "First Festival District," "Third Festival District," "Eastern Festival District," and "Western Festival District." On the night of August 9, a competition titled "Sound and Strength Performance" features floats and hand dances, while August 10 showcases taiko drumming and golden staves. The festival was designated an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Asset of Hokkaido|ja|無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Intangible Folk Cultural Asset of Hokkaido|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 民俗文化財|1=Folk Cultural Property|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24895900}} in 2020 (Reiwa 2). == Address == * Kotobira Town 1-4, Nemuro, Hokkaido == External Links == * [https://www.nemuro-kotohira.com/ Official Website of Kotohira Shrine] * [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/top.html Hokkaido Shrine Association] * [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/16/16001.html Kotohira Shrine Information] * [http://www.city.nemuro.hokkaido.jp/section/kanko/event_cale/konpira/konpira.html Nemuro City Event Information: Kotohira Shrine Festival] {{ill-broken-link|date=August 2023}} * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.co.jp/HeartLand/3922/ |title=Nemuro Topics (Kotohira Shrine Festival images and videos) |date=April 27, 1999}} * [http://www.nemuro-kankou.com/events/kotohira.html Nemuro Tourist Association: Kotohira Shrine Grand Festival] {{ill-broken-link|date=August 2023}} * [http://crabnavi.ninpou.jp/nemuro-kotohira/top.html Nemuro Tourism and Festivals: Kotohira Shrine Festival and Sightseeing] {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|金刀比羅神社 (根室市)|version=101314516|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:金刀比羅神社 (根室市)]] [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]] ==Merged second translation== {{infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|image=Kotohira Shrine (Nemuro City).jpg | name = Kotohira Shrine | image = [[File:Kotohira Shrine (Nemuro City).jpg|270px]]
